yudhāmanyuś cha vikrānta uttamaujāś cha vīryavān
saubhadro draupadeyāś cha sarva eva mahā-rathāḥ [6]
Translation: Among these soldiers are the expert archers Bhīma, Arjuna, and others equally skilled in battle, like Sātyaki, King Virāṭa, the great fighter Drupada, Dhṛṣṭaketu, Chekitāna, the mighty hero Kāśīrāj, Purujit, Kuntibhoja, the noble King Śaibya, the valiant Yudhāmanyu, the herioc Uttamaujā, Abhimanyu, and the sons of Draupadī. They are all great warriors, indeed.

Text:
अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम ।
नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते ॥७॥
Transcription:
asmākaṁ tu viśiṣṭā ye tān nibodha dvijottama
nāyakā mama sainyasya saṁjñārthaṁ tān bravīmi te [7]
Translation: O best of the twice-born, please consider, too, the foremost amongst us, the commanders of my armies. I submit their names before you:

Text:
भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः ।
अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिर्जयद्रथः ॥८॥
अन्ये च बहवः शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविताः ।
नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः सर्व्वे युद्धविशारदाः ॥९॥
Transcription:
bhavān bhīṣmaś cha karṇaś cha kṛpaś cha samitiñjayaḥ
aśvatthāmā vikarṇaś cha saumadattir jayadrathaḥ [8]
anye cha bahavaḥ śūrā mad-arthe tyakta-jīvitāḥ
nānā-śastra-praharaṇāḥ sarve yuddha-viśāradāḥ [9]
Translation: Firstly there is your respected self, then Bhīṣma, Karṇa, the ever victorious Kṛpāchārya, Aśvatthāmā, Vikarṇa, Bhūriśravā, Jayadratha, and many other heroes skilled in the art of warfare, all armed with various weapons and prepared to lay down their very lives for me.

Text:
अपर्य्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम् ।
पर्य्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम् ॥१०॥
Transcription:
aparyāptaṁ tad asmākaṁ balaṁ bhīṣmābhirakṣitam
paryāptaṁ tv idam eteṣāṁ balaṁ bhīmābhirakṣitam [10]
Translation: The strength of our army, headed by Bhīṣma, seems inestimable, yet the strength of the Pāṇḍavas’ army, protected by Bhīma, is estimable.

Text:
अयनेषु च सर्व्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः ।
भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व्व एव हि ॥११॥
Transcription:
ayaneṣu cha sarveṣu yathā-bhāgam avasthitāḥ
bhīṣmam evābhirakṣantu bhavantaḥ sarva eva hi [11]
Translation: Now take up your posts at the strategic points of the formation in support of Bhīṣma.

Text:
तस्य सञ्जनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्धः पितामहः ।
सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान् ॥१२॥
Transcription:
tasya sañjanayan harṣaṁ kuru-vṛddhaḥ pitāmahaḥ
siṁha-nādaṁ vinadyochchaiḥ śaṅkhaṁ dadhmau pratāpavān [12]
Translation: Then, with a war-cry like the roar of a lion, the venerable elder, Bhīṣma, loudly blew his conchshell to encourage Duryodhan.

Text:
ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्य्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः ।
सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥१३॥
Transcription:
tataḥ śaṅkhāś cha bheryaś cha paṇavānaka-gomukhāḥ
sahasaivābhyahanyanta sa śabdas tumulo ’bhavat [13]
Translation: At once there arose a tumultuous vibration of conchshells, kettledrums, hand-drums, bugles, and the resounding of many instruments.

Text:
ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ ।
माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः ॥१४॥
Transcription:
tataḥ śvetair hayair yukte mahati syandane sthitau
mādhavaḥ pāṇḍavaś chaiva divyau śaṅkhau pradadhmatuḥ [14]
Translation: Then Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna, in a magnificent chariot drawn by white horses, blew their divine conchshells.

Text:
पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनञ्जयः ।
पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्म्मा वृकोदरः ॥१५॥
Transcription:
pāñchajanyaṁ hṛṣīkeśo devadattaṁ dhanañjayaḥ
pauṇḍraṁ dadhmau mahā-śaṅkhaṁ bhīma-karmā vṛkodaraḥ [15]
Translation: Hṛṣīkeś blew His conchshell Pāñchajanya; Dhanañjaya sounded his, the Devadatta; and Bhīmasen, the performer of mighty tasks, blew the conchshell Pauṇḍra.

Text:
अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।
नकुलः सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ ॥१६॥
Transcription:
anantavijayaṁ rājā kuntī-putro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ
nakulaḥ sahadevaś cha sughoṣa-maṇipuṣpakau [16]
Translation: King Yudhiṣṭhir, the son of Kuntī, blew the Anantavijay, while Nakul and Sahadev blew the conchshells Sughoṣa and Maṇipuṣpaka.

Text:
काश्यश्च परमेष्वासः शिखण्डी च महारथः ।
धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजितः ॥१७॥
द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व्वशः पृथिवीपते ।
सौभद्रश्च महाबाहुः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक् ॥१८॥
Transcription:
kāśyaś cha parameṣv-āsaḥ śikhaṇḍī cha mahā-rathaḥ
dhṛṣṭadyumno virāṭaś cha sātyakiś chāparājitaḥ [17]
drupado draupadeyāś cha sarvaśaḥ pṛthivī-pate
saubhadraś cha mahā-bāhuḥ śaṅkhān dadhmuḥ pṛthak pṛthak [18]
Translation: O lord of the earth, the skilled archer Kāśīrāj, the great warrior Śikhaṇḍī, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, King Virāṭa, the unconquerable Sātyaki, King Drupada, the sons of Draupadī, and the mighty son of Subhadrā, Abhimanyu—all blew their conchshells.

Text:
स घोषो धार्त्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत् ।
नभश्च पृथिवीञ्चैव तुमुलोऽभ्यनुनादयन् ॥१९॥
Transcription:
sa ghoṣo dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ hṛdayāni vyadārayat
nabhaś cha pṛthivīñ chaiva tumulo ’bhyanunādayan [19]
Translation: Resounding throughout the land and heavens, the tremendous vibration of those conchshells pierced the hearts of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

Text:
अथ व्यवस्थितान्दृष्ट्वा धार्त्तराष्ट्रान्कपिध्वजः ।
प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसम्पाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डवः ।
हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते ॥२०॥
Transcription:
atha vyavasthitān dṛṣṭvā dhārtarāṣṭrān kapi-dhvajaḥ
pravṛtte śastra-sampāte dhanur udyamya pāṇḍavaḥ
hṛṣīkeśaṁ tadā vākyam idam āha mahī-pate [20]
Translation: O King, as the battle was about to commence, Arjuna, whose chariot was adorned with the flag of Hanumān, seeing Duryodhan and his army poised for battle, took up his bow and said to Śrī Kṛṣṇa:

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ॥२१॥
यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान् ।
कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन्रणसमुद्यमे ॥२२॥
योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः ।
धार्त्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः ॥२३॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
senayor ubhayor madhye rathaṁ sthāpaya me ’chyuta [21]
yāvad etān nirīkṣe ’haṁ yoddhu-kāmān avasthitān
kair mayā saha yoddhavyam asmin raṇa-samudyame [22]
yotsyamānān avekṣe ’haṁ ya ete ’tra samāgatāḥ
dhārtarāṣṭrasya durbuddher yuddhe priya-chikīrṣavaḥ [23]
Translation: O Kṛṣṇa, please place my chariot between the two armies so I may observe the warriors with whom I must contend on this battlefield, and who have come to fight for the satisfaction of the wicked Duryodhan.

Text:
सञ्जय उवाच ।
एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् ॥२४॥
Transcription:
sañjaya uvācha
evam ukto hṛṣīkeśo guḍākeśena bhārata
senayor ubhayor madhye sthāpayitvā rathottamam
Translation: Sañjaya said: O descendant of Bharat, after the vigilant Arjuna spoke these words, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of the perceptions of all living beings, drew the grand chariot between both armies...

Text:
तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान्पार्थः पितॄनथ पितामहान् ।
आचार्य्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातॄन्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा ।
श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि ॥२६॥
Transcription:
tatrāpaśyat sthitān pārthaḥ pitṝn atha pitāmahān
āchāryān mātulān bhrātṝn putrān pautrān sakhīṁs tathā
śvaśurān suhṛdaś chaiva senayor ubhayor api [26]
Translation: There, within both armies, Arjuna saw paternal and maternal elders, grandfathers, teachers, brothers, sons, grandsons, fathers-in-law, friends, and other well-wishers.

Text:
तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्व्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् ।
कृपया परयाविष्टो विषीदन्नदमब्रवीत् ॥२७॥
Transcription:
tān samīkṣya sa kaunteyaḥ sarvān bandhūn avasthitān
kṛpayā parayāviṣṭo viṣīdann idam abravīt [27]
Translation: Seeing his kinsmen present on the battlefield, Arjuna, the son of Kuntī, was grief-stricken and overcome with pity. He said:

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच
दृष्ट्वेमान्स्वजनान्कृष्ण युयुत्सून्समवस्थितान् ।
सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखञ्च परिशुष्यति ॥२८॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
dṛṣṭvemān svajanān kṛṣṇa yuyutsūn samavasthitān
sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhañ cha pariśuṣyati [28]
Translation: O Kṛṣṇa, seeing all these relatives and friends assembled and eager for battle, the strength is draining from my limbs and my mouth feels parched.

Text:
वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ।
गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते ॥२९॥
Transcription:
vepathuś cha śarīre me roma-harṣaś cha jāyate
gāṇḍīvaṁ sraṁsate hastāt tvak chaiva paridahyate [29]
Translation: I am trembling and the hairs on my body are standing on end, my skin is burning, and I am losing grip of my Gāṇḍīva bow.

Text:
न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः ।
निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव ॥३०॥
Transcription:
na cha śaknomy avasthātuṁ bhramatīva cha me manaḥ
nimittāni cha paśyāmi viparītāni keśava [30]
Translation: O Keśava, I cannot maintain my composure any longer. My mind is disturbed, and I see bad omens.

Text:
न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ।
न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च ॥३१॥
Transcription:
na cha śreyo ’nupaśyāmi hatvā svajanam āhave
na kāṅkṣe vijayaṁ kṛṣṇa na cha rājyaṁ sukhāni cha [31]
Translation: I do not see any good in killing my kinsmen in this battle. O Kṛṣṇa, I have no wish for victory or to enjoy a kingdom.

Text:
किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा ।
येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च ॥३२॥
त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च ।
आचार्य्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः ॥३३॥
मातुलाः श्वशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः संबन्धिनस्तथा ।
एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन ॥३४॥
Transcription:
kiṁ no rājyena govinda kiṁ bhogair jīvitena vā
yeṣām arthe kāṅkṣitaṁ no rājyaṁ bhogāḥ sukhāni cha [32]
ta ime ’vasthitā yuddhe prāṇāṁs tyaktvā dhanāni cha
āchāryāḥ pitaraḥ putrās tathaiva cha pitāmahāḥ [33]
mātulāḥ śvaśurāḥ pautrāḥ śyālāḥ sambandhinas tathā
etān na hantum ichchhāmi ghnato ’pi madhusūdana [34]
Translation: O Govinda, if those for whom we desire all this—teachers, paternal and maternal elders, sons, grandfathers, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, and other relatives—are about to risk their wealth and lives in battle, of what value is our kingdom and its royal pleasures, or even life itself? O Madhusūdan, I do not wish to kill them even though I, myself, may be killed.

Text:
अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते ।
निहत्य धार्त्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्द्दन ॥३५॥
Transcription:
api trailokya-rājyasya hetoḥ kiṁ nu mahī-kṛte
nihatya dhārtarāṣṭrān naḥ kā prītiḥ syāj janārdana [35]
Translation: O Janārdan, even if we gain sovereignty over the three worlds, not to speak of ruling the earth, how can we be happy if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra?

Text:
पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः ।
तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्त्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान् ।
स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव ॥३६॥
Transcription:
pāpam evāśrayed asmān hatvaitān ātatāyinaḥ
tasmān nārhā vayaṁ hantuṁ dhārtarāṣṭrān svabāndhavān
svajanaṁ hi kathaṁ hatvā sukhinaḥ syāma mādhava [36]
Translation: Only sin will befall us if we slay our teachers and guardians, though they be our adversaries. We cannot take the lives of our own relatives, the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra. O Mādhava, how can we be happy by killing our kinsmen?

Text:
यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः ।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् ॥३७॥
कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्त्तितुम् ।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्द्दन ॥३८॥
Transcription:
yadyapy ete na paśyanti lobhopahata-chetasaḥ
kula-kṣaya-kṛtaṁ doṣaṁ mitra-drohe cha pātakam [37]
kathaṁ na jñeyam asmābhiḥ pāpād asmān nivartitum
kula-kṣaya-kṛtaṁ doṣaṁ prapaśyadbhir janārdana [38]
Translation: Athough they, overwhelmed with greed, cannot see the sin of betraying one’s friends and destroying the dynasty, O Janārdan, how can we not refrain from this heinous act, being in full knowledge of the consequences?

Text:
कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्म्माः सनातनाः ।
धर्म्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्म्मोऽभिभवत्युत ॥३९॥
Transcription:
kula-kṣaye praṇaśyanti kula-dharmāḥ sanātanāḥ
dharme naṣṭe kulaṁ kṛtsnam adharmo ’bhibhavaty uta [39]
Translation: With the decline of the dynasty, its religious traditions are lost. When religious practices are lost, irreligion overcomes the entire dynasty.

Text:
अधर्म्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः ।
स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः ॥४०॥
Transcription:
adharmābhibhavāt kṛṣṇa praduṣyanti kula-striyaḥ
strīṣu duṣṭāsu vārṣṇeya jāyate varṇa-saṅkaraḥ [40]
Translation: O Kṛṣṇa, descendant of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty, when the dynasty is thus overcome by irreligion, the women of the family become corrupt and adulterated progeny is the result.

Text:
सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च ।
पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः ॥४१॥
Transcription:
saṅkaro narakāyaiva kula-ghnānāṁ kulasya cha
patanti pitaro hy eṣāṁ lupta-piṇḍodaka-kriyāḥ [41]
Translation: Adulterated progeny forces both the family and its aggressors into a hellish existence. The same fate befalls their ancestors, because regular offerings of food and water to them cease.

Text:
दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः ।
उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्म्माः कुलधर्म्माश्च शाश्वताः ॥४२॥
Transcription:
doṣair etaiḥ kula-ghnānāṁ varṇa-saṅkara-kārakaiḥ
utsādyante jāti-dharmāḥ kula-dharmāś cha śāśvatāḥ [42]
Translation: Such heinous acts by the destroyers of the dynasty are the cause of adulterated progeny, ruining the culture of a civilized society and noble lineage.

Text:
उत्सन्नकुलधर्म्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्द्दन ।
नरके नियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥४३॥
Transcription:
utsanna-kula-dharmāṇāṁ manuṣyāṇāṁ janārdana
narake niyataṁ vāso bhavatīty anuśuśruma [43]
Translation: O Janārdan, we have heard that persons whose familial, social and religious traditions are disrupted dwell always in hell.

Text:
अहो बत महत्पापं कर्त्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् ।
यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः ॥४४॥
Transcription:
aho bata mahat-pāpaṁ kartuṁ vyavasitā vayam
yad rājya-sukha-lobhena hantuṁ svajanam udyatāḥ [44]
Translation: Alas, what a grave sin we have resolved to commit! Driven by greed for trifling royal happiness, we are about to slay our own kinsmen.

Text:
यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः ।
धार्त्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ॥४५॥
Transcription:
yadi mām apratīkāram aśastraṁ śastra-pāṇayaḥ
dhārtarāṣṭrā raṇe hanyus tan me kṣemataraṁ bhavet [45]
Translation: If, unarmed and unresisting, I am killed on this battlefield by the armed sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, that will be more auspicious for me.

Text:
सञ्जय उवाच ।
एवमुक्त्वार्ज्जुनः सङ्ख्ये  रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् ।
विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥४६॥
Transcription:
sañjaya uvācha
evam uktvārjunaḥ saṅkhye rathopastha upāviśat
visṛjya sa-śaraṁ chāpaṁ śoka-saṁvigna-mānasaḥ [46]
Translation: Sañjaya said: There, on the battlefield, speaking these words, Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot, his heart filled with sadness.

Text:
सञ्जय उवाच ।
तं तथा कृपयाविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम् ।
विषीदन्तमदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः ॥१॥
Transcription:
sañjaya uvācha
taṁ tathā kṛpayāviṣṭam aśru-pūrṇākulekṣaṇam
viṣīdantam idaṁ vākyam uvācha madhusūdanaḥ [1]
Translation: Sañjaya said: The Supreme Lord, Madhusūdan, then spoke to the saddened Arjuna whose heart was filled with pity, his eyes brimming with tears.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् ।
अनार्य्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्त्तिकरमर्ज्जुन ॥२॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
kutas tvā kaśmalam idaṁ viṣame samupasthitam
anārya-juṣṭam asvargyam akīrti-karam arjuna [2]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: O Arjuna, how has such illusion overcome you at this crucial moment? This does not befit an Āryan, and it leads not to heaven but to infamy.

Text:
क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्यपपद्यते ।
क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप ॥३॥
Transcription:
klaibyaṁ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayy upapadyate
kṣudraṁ hṛdaya-daurbalyaṁ tyaktvottiṣṭha parantapa [3]
Translation: O son of Kuntī, give up this cowardice, for it does not befit you. O great hero, cast off this petty weakness of heart and arise for battle!

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
कथं भीष्ममहं सङ्ख्ये  द्रोणञ्च मधुसूदन ।
इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन ॥४॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
kathaṁ bhīṣmam ahaṁ saṅkhye droṇañ cha madhusūdana
iṣubhiḥ pratiyotsyāmi pūjārhāv arisūdana [4]
Translation: Arjuna said: O Madhusūdan, slayer of the enemy, how can I counterattack, shooting arrows at venerable Bhīṣma and my teacher Droṇa, who are worthy of my worship?

Text:
गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावाञ्
श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके ।
हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनहैव
भुञ्जीय भोगान्रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् ॥५॥
Transcription:
gurūn ahatvā hi mahānubhāvāñ
śreyo bhoktuṁ bhaikṣyam apīha loke
hatvārtha-kāmāṁs tu gurūn ihaiva
bhuñjīya bhogān rudhira-pradigdhān [5]
Translation: It is better to live in this world and beg, without taking the lives of our noble elders and teachers. Otherwise, by killing them we shall remain in this world only to enjoy their wealth and possessions tainted with their blood.

Text:
न चैतद्वद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो
यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः ।
यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषामस्
तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्त्तराष्ट्राः ॥६॥
Transcription:
na chaitad vidmaḥ kataran no garīyo
yad vā jayema yadi vā no jayeyuḥ
yān eva hatvā na jijīviṣāmas
te ’vasthitāḥ pramukhe dhārtarāṣṭrāḥ [6]
Translation: I cannot understand which will be better—victory or defeat; for if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who now stand before us on the battlefront, we shall not wish to live.

Text:
कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः
पृच्छामि त्वां धर्म्मसम्मूढचेताः ।
यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्चतं ब्रूहि तन्मे
शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां  प्रपन्नम् ॥७॥
Transcription:
kārpaṇya-doṣopahata-svabhāvaḥ
pṛchchhāmi tvāṁ dharma-sammūḍha-chetāḥ
yach chhreyaḥ syān niśchitaṁ brūhi tan me
śiṣyas te ’haṁ śādhi māṁ tvāṁ prapannam [7]
Translation: Now I am bewildered. What is my real duty? I am losing heart and distraught. So please tell me clearly what is the best course of action to take. I am Your surrendered disciple; please instruct me.

Text:
न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद्
यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम् ।
अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धं
राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम् ॥८॥
Transcription:
na hi prapaśyāmi mamāpanudyād
yach chhokam uchchhoṣaṇam indriyāṇām
avāpya bhūmāv asapatnam ṛddhaṁ
rājyaṁ surāṇām api chādhipatyam [8]
Translation: Even if we gain an unrivalled, flourishing kingdom on earth and sovereignty over heaven, I see nothing to dispel my debilitating grief.

Text:
सञ्जय उवाच ।
एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तपः ।
न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह ॥९॥
Transcription:
sañjaya uvācha
evam uktvā hṛṣīkeśaṁ guḍākeśaḥ parantapaḥ
na yotsya iti govindam uktvā tūṣṇīṁ babhūva ha [9]
Translation: Sañjaya said: Thus the valiant, vigilant Arjuna spoke to Kṛṣṇa, who is Hṛṣīkeś, the Lord of the senses of all beings. Then he declared, “Govinda, I will not fight”, and fell silent.

Text:
तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः ॥१०॥
Transcription:
tam uvācha hṛṣīkeśaḥ prahasann iva bhārata
senayor ubhayor madhye viṣīdantam idaṁ vachaḥ [10]
Translation: O Bhārat, there amidst the armies, Śrī Hṛṣīkeś, smiling, spoke thus to the grief-stricken Arjuna:

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे ।
गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः ॥११॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
aśochyān anvaśochas tvaṁ prajñā-vādāṁś cha bhāṣase
gatāsūn agatāsūṁś cha nānuśochanti paṇḍitāḥ [11]
Translation: The Lord said: O Arjuna, you are lamenting for that which is unworthy of grief, yet speaking words of wisdom. The wise lament neither for the living nor the dead.

Text:
न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः ।
न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्व्वे वयमतः परम् ॥१२॥
Transcription:
na tv evāhaṁ jātu nāsaṁ na tvaṁ neme janādhipāḥ
na chaiva na bhaviṣyāmaḥ sarve vayam ataḥ param [12]
Translation: Never was there a time when you, I, or all these kings did not exist. Just as we exist in the present, so have we existed in the past, and shall continue to exist in the future.

Text:
देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा ।
तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥१३॥
Transcription:
dehino ’smin yathā dehe kaumāraṁ yauvanaṁ jarā
tathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati [13]
Translation: As the living being passes through the bodily changes of childhood, youth, and old age, it similarly attains another body at death. The wise are not deluded by this.

Text:
मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः ।
आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत ॥१४॥
Transcription:
mātrā-sparśās tu kaunteya śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ
āgamāpāyino ’nityās tāṁs titikṣasva bhārata [14]
Translation: O son of Kuntī, the engagement of the senses with their objects produces the sensations of cold, heat, pleasure, and pain. But these effects are temporary—they come and go. Therefore, O Bhārat, you must endure them.

Text:
यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ ।
समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥१५॥
Transcription:
yaṁ hi na vyathayanty ete puruṣaṁ puruṣarṣabha
sama-duḥkha-sukhaṁ dhīraṁ so ’mṛtatvāya kalpate [15]
Translation: O noblest of men, the wise man who is equipoised in pleasure and pain, and undisturbed by sense-experiences, is alone qualified for immortality.

Text:
नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः ।
उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥१६॥
Transcription:
nāsato vidyate bhāvo nābhāvo vidyate sataḥ
ubhayor api dṛṣṭo ’ntas tv anayos tattva-darśibhiḥ [16]
Translation: Of the asat (destructible object, such as the mutable body and associated phenomena) there is no eternal existence; of the sat (eternal object, the soul) there is no destruction or change. The nature of each has been realised by the seers of the truth.

Text:
अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्व्वमिदं ततम् ।
विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्त्तुमर्हति ॥१७॥
Transcription:
avināśi tu tad viddhi yena sarvam idaṁ tatam
vināśam avyayasyāsya na kaśchit kartum arhati [17]
Translation: Know that the soul, by which the entire body is pervaded, is indestructible. No one can destroy the imperishable soul.

Text:
अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः ।
अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत ॥१८॥
Transcription:
antavanta ime dehā nityasyoktāḥ śarīriṇaḥ
anāśino ’prameyasya tasmād yudhyasva bhārata [18]
Translation: The soul is said to be eternal, indestructible and immeasurable. Only these physical bodies are subject to destruction. Therefore fight, O Bhārat.

Text:
य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम् ।
उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥१९॥
Transcription:
ya enaṁ vetti hantāraṁ yaś chainaṁ manyate hatam
ubhau tau na vijānīto nāyaṁ hanti na hanyate [19]
Translation: Those who think the living being slays, and those who think it is slain, are ignorant of the true nature of the soul; for the soul neither slays nor is slain.

Text:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्
नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः ।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥२०॥
Transcription:
na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchin
nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ
ajo nityaḥ śāśvato ’yaṁ purāṇo
na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre [20]
Translation: The soul is neither born nor dies; it has neither been nor will it be created, because it is unborn and eternal. It is ever-youthful, yet ancient. It is not destroyed when the body is destroyed.

Text:
वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् ।
कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥२१॥
Transcription:
vedāvināśinaṁ nityaṁ ya enam ajam avyayam
kathaṁ sa puruṣaḥ pārtha kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam [21]
Translation: O Pārtha, knowing the soul to be indestructible, eternal, birthless and immutable, how can a person kill or cause anyone to be killed?

Text:
वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय
नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि ।
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णान्य्
अन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥२२॥
Transcription:
vāsāṁsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya
navāni gṛhṇāti naro ’parāṇi
tathā śarīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇāny
anyāni saṁyāti navāni dehī [22]
Translation: As a person adopts new garments, discarding those that are old and worn, similarly, the soul continues to adopt new bodies, leaving those that are old and useless.

Text:
नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः ।
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥२३॥
Transcription:
nainaṁ chhindanti śastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ
na chainaṁ kledayanty āpo na śoṣayati mārutaḥ [23]
Translation: Weapons cannot pierce the soul, and fire cannot burn it. Water cannot wet it, and air cannot dry it.

Text:
अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च ।
नित्यः सर्व्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥२४॥
Transcription:
achchhedyo ’yam adāhyo ’yam akledyo ’śoṣya eva cha
nityaḥ sarva-gataḥ sthāṇur achalo ’yaṁ sanātanaḥ
Translation: It is said that the soul is indivisible and cannot be burned, moistened, or dried. It is eternal, it reaches all planes yet remains still, immovable, and ever-present.

Text:
अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम् ।
तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैनं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥२६॥
Transcription:
atha chainaṁ nitya-jātaṁ nityaṁ vā manyase mṛtam
tathāpi tvaṁ mahā-bāho nainaṁ śochitum arhasi
Translation: And, O best of warriors, even if you think that the soul is perpetually subject to birth and death, you still have no reason to lament.

Text:
अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत ।
अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना ॥२८॥
Transcription:
avyaktādīni bhūtāni vyakta-madhyāni bhārata
avyakta-nidhanāny eva tatra kā paridevanā [28]
Translation: O Bhārat, when all living beings are unmanifest before birth, manifest between birth and death, and again unmanifest at death, why lament for them?

Text:
आश्चर्य्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेनम्
आश्चर्य्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः ।
आश्चर्य्यवच्चैनमन्यः शृणोति
श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् ॥२९॥
Transcription:
āścharyavat paśyati kaśchid enam
āścharyavad vadati tathaiva chānyaḥ
āścharyavach chainam anyaḥ śṛṇoti
śrutvāpy enaṁ veda na chaiva kaśchit [29]
Translation: Some see the soul as astonishing, some describe it as astonishing, some hear of it as astonishing, while others, though hearing about it, know nothing of it.

Text:
देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्व्वस्य भारत ।
तस्मात्सर्व्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥३०॥
Transcription:
dehī nityam avadhyo ’yaṁ dehe sarvasya bhārata
tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni na tvaṁ śochitum arhasi [30]
Translation: O Bhārat, the soul dwelling within the bodies of all living beings is eternal and cannot be slain. Therefore, you need lament for no one.

Text:
स्वधर्म्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि ।
धर्म्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते ॥३१॥
Transcription:
svadharmam api chāvekṣya na vikampitum arhasi
dharmyāddhi yuddhāch chhreyo ’nyat kṣatriyasya na vidyate [31]
Translation: Moreover, considering your natural duty, you have no reason to waver, since there is no action more auspicious for a warrior than a battle for justice.

Text:
यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम् ।
सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् ॥३२॥
Transcription:
yadṛchchhayā chopapannaṁ svarga-dvāram apāvṛtam
sukhinaḥ kṣatriyāḥ pārtha labhante yuddham īdṛśam
Translation: O Pārtha, only the most fortunate warriors are blessed with the opportunity of such a battle, which has come to you like an open door to heaven.
Comment:
One’s natural duty or ‘svadharma’ is of two types—relative or absolute—according to the soul’s state of either material bondage or liberation respectively. In the liberated state, the natural function of the soul is purely spiritual devotional service, for the transcendental pleasure of the Supreme Lord alone. But in the state of material bondage, a person’s natural duty possesses mundane attributes. The conditioned soul accepts various births throughout the 8,400,000 species of life according to his actions, until by the influence of piety he obtains a human birth. Then, according to his nature, he may adopt a position and prescribed duty in the God-centred socio-religious system (Daiva-varṇāśram), which helps one attain the natural duty of the liberated state.
In this respect, the natural duty of the liberated soul is somewhat covered by the relativity of the material world; yet, within the God-centred socio-religious system, it is nonetheless considered a person’s natural duty, as, for example, smoke-covered fire is still considered fire, despite its covering.

Text:
अथ चेत्त्वमिमं धर्म्म्यं सङ्ग्रामं न करिष्यसि ।
ततः स्वधर्म्मं कीर्त्तिञ्च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥३३॥
Transcription:
atha chet tvam imaṁ dharmyaṁ saṅgrāmaṁ na kariṣyasi
tataḥ svadharmaṁ kīrtiñ cha hitvā pāpam avāpsyasi [33]
Translation: But if you choose not to fight this battle for justice, your religious principles will be lost, fame will desert you, and sin will overcome you.

Text:
अकीर्त्तिञ्चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम् ।
सम्भावितस्य चाकीर्त्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते ॥३४॥
Transcription:
akīrtiñ chāpi bhūtāni kathayiṣyanti te ’vyayām
sambhāvitasya chākīrtir maraṇād atirichyate [34]
Translation: People will speak of your infamy for all time to come; and for the renowned, infamy is worse than death.

Text:
भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः ।
येषाञ्च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् ॥३५॥
Transcription:
bhayād raṇād uparataṁ maṁsyante tvāṁ mahā-rathāḥ
yeṣāñ cha tvaṁ bahu-mato bhūtvā yāsyasi lāghavam [35]
Translation: The great warriors who highly honour you will ridicule you, considering that you were afraid to fight.

Text:
अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून्वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः ।
निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम् ॥३६॥
Transcription:
avāchya-vādāṁś cha bahūn vadiṣyanti tavāhitāḥ
nindantas tava sāmarthyaṁ tato duḥkhataraṁ nu kim [36]
Translation: Your enemies will mock you. What could be more painful?

Text:
हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् ।
तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥३७॥
Transcription:
hato vā prāpsyasi svargaṁ jitvā vā bhokṣyase mahīm
tasmād uttiṣṭha kaunteya yuddhāya kṛta-niśchayaḥ [37]
Translation: O son of Kuntī, if you are killed, you will attain heaven, and if you are victorious, you will enjoy your life on earth. Therefore, being confident of your success, arise for battle!

Text:
सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ ।
ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥३८॥
Transcription:
sukha-duḥkhe same kṛtvā lābhālābhau jayājayau
tato yuddhāya yujyasva naivaṁ pāpam avāpsyasi [38]
Translation: Considering pleasure and pain, gain and loss, and victory and defeat to be one and the same—fight. No sin will be incurred by you.

Text:
एषा तेऽभिहिता साङ्ख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां शृनु ॥
बुद्ध्या युक्तो यया पार्थ कर्म्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि ॥३९॥
Transcription:
eṣā te ’bhihitā sāṅkhye buddhir yoge tv imāṁ śṛnu
buddhyā yukto yayā pārtha karma-bandhaṁ prahāsyasi [39]
Translation: I have revealed to you this knowledge (buddhi) of the soul. Now hear from Me how to act in this knowledge (bhakti-yoga), through which you will be able to free yourself from the bondage of action.
Comment:
“It will be revealed that buddhi-yoga (yoga of wisdom) is a singular path. When buddhi-yoga is seen to be limited by the ideal of action, it is known as karma-yoga; when it extends beyond action up to the peak of knowledge, it is known as jñāna-yoga or sāṅkhya-yoga; and when, transcending the limitations of both action and knowledge it touches bhakti or devotion, it is then known as bhakti-yoga, or perfectly pure and complete buddhi-yoga.”
— Śrīla Bhakti Vinod Ṭhākur

Text:
नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते ।
स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्म्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् ॥४०॥
Transcription:
nehābhikrama-nāśo ’sti pratyavāyo na vidyate
svalpam apy asya dharmasya trāyate mahato bhayāt [40]
Translation: On this path there can be no loss and no harm. The slightest practice of such a spiritual ideal saves one from the greatest fear.
Comment:
Mahābhay, the greatest fear, is generally considered to be the fear of repeated birth and death in this world. But mahābhay also refers to sāyujya-mukti, the liberation of merging into the impersonal non-differentiated consciousness of Brahmaloka, Virajā, or a kind of impersonal samādhi. Renunciation alone leads to sāyujya-mukti, the cessation of birth and death, but not to a life in the positive plane. It takes one to a permanent ‘burial’ in Brahmaloka. The demon of mukti will devour one wholesale, and therefore it is even more dangerous than this ordinary life of action and reaction. Mukti means a strike in the organic system, so it must be avoided. For example, to either ignore duties in a factory or to declare a strike by cessation of duties are both abnormal.
Bhukti, exploitation, and mukti, renunciation, are both fearsome ghosts. Therefore, here, mahābhay means the great apprehension of falling prey to both the ghost of exploitation leading to birth and death, as well as the ghost of committing suicide by merging into the impersonalism of incarceration in Brahmaloka—fear of both bhoga-bhūmi, the land of exploitation, and tyāga-bhūmi, the plane of renunciation. It is stated in the(1.2.22, 1.1.9):

भुक्तिमुक्तिस्पृहा यावत् पिशाची हृदि वर्त्तते ।
तावद्भक्तिसुखस्यात्र कथमभ्युदयो भवेत् ॥

bhukti-mukti-spṛhā yāvat piśāchī hṛdi vartate
tāvad bhakti-sukhasyātra katham abhyudayo bhavet
“The ecstasy of devotion to Kṛṣṇa will never appear in a heart that is haunted by the ghosts of exploitation and renunciation.”

अन्याभिलाषिता शून्यं ज्ञानकर्म्माद्यनावृतम् ।
आनुकूल्येन कृष्णनुशीलनं भक्तरुत्तमा ॥

anyābhilāṣitā śūnyaṁ jñāna-karmādy anāvṛtam
ānukūlyena kṛṣṇānuśīlanaṁ bhaktir uttamā

“The highest devotion is that which is cultivated for the satisfaction of the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa, with no desire except to serve Him, and divested of all external attempts such as worldly action, knowledge in search of the impersonal Absolute, or the meditational attempt to become one with God.”
Only the Supreme Lord is abhay, beyond apprehension. Therefore nothing but participation in His service can free one from the ghosts of exploitation and renunciation, bhukti and mukti. If we subtract bhukti and mukti, then only positive bhakti, devotion, is the remainder. Therefore, without devotion, everything is mahābhay, great danger.

Text:
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन ।
बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम् ॥४१॥
Transcription:
vyavasāyātmikā buddhir ekeha kuru-nandana
bahu-śākhā hy anantāś cha buddhayo ’vyavasāyinām [41]
Translation: O descendant of the Kuru dynasty, resolute intelligence dedicated to Me is one-pointed—I am its only objective. But irresolute intelligence is splayed by endless desires for mundane enjoyment.

Text:
यामिमां पुष्पितां वाच प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः ।
वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः ॥४२॥
Transcription:
yām imāṁ puṣpitāṁ vāchaṁ pravadanty avipaśchitaḥ
veda-vāda-ratāḥ pārtha nānyad astīti vādinaḥ [42]
Translation: O Pārtha, unwise persons, not knowing that the chief directive of the Vedas is the attainment of the Supreme Truth, are fond of interpreting the fleetingly enchanting words of the karma-kāṇḍa section of the Vedas as ultimate.

Text:
कामात्मानः स्वर्गपराः जन्मकर्म्मफलप्रदाम् ।
क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्य्यगतिं प्रति ॥४३॥
Transcription:
kāmātmānaḥ svarga-parāḥ janma-karma-phala-pradām
kriyā-viśeṣa-bahulāṁ bhogaiśvarya-gatiṁ prati [43]
Translation: Their hearts are filled with desires and their goal is heaven. They advocate the many sacrifices and rituals that yield wealth, worldly pleasures, and high birth.

Text:
भोगैश्वर्य्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम् ।
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते ॥४४॥
Transcription:
bhogaiśvarya-prasaktānāṁ tayāpahṛta-chetasām
vyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ samādhau na vidhīyate [44]
Translation: Thus attached to enjoyment and opulence, deluded by these flowery words of the Vedas, they do not attain the resolve to dedicate themselves to the Supreme Lord.

Text:
त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्ज्जुन ।
निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥४५॥
Transcription:
traiguṇya-viṣayā vedā nistraiguṇyo bhavārjuna
nirdvandvo nitya-sattva-stho niryoga-kṣema ātmavān [45]
Translation: O Arjuna, the Vedas deal with the three modes of material nature (by accommodating fruitive action and liberation as an allowance and incentive for persons who are ignorant of the true objective of the Vedas). But you, Arjuna, give up duality and all pursuits for gain and preservation, stand ever in the plane of truth, and by the yoga of wisdom, transcend the modes of nature.
Comment: (The objective of the Vedas is transcendental. Without fruitive action or liberation, O Arjuna, follow the transcendental path of devotion to the Lord as taught by the Vedas.)

Text:
यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्व्वतः सम्प्लुतोदके ।
तावान्सर्व्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः ॥४६॥
Transcription:
yāvān artha udapāne sarvataḥ samplutodake
tāvān sarveṣu vedeṣu brāhmaṇasya vijānataḥ [46]
Translation: As all the purposes served by several ponds can be served better by a large lake, similarly, the fruits of worshipping the various gods by their respective Vedic prayers are surpassed by serving Me. The knower of Brahma who thus knows the purport of the Vedas fulfils all life’s objectives.

Text:
कर्म्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन ।
मा कर्म्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्म्मणि ॥४७॥
Transcription:
karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadāchana
mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stv akarmaṇi [47]
Translation: Your right is to your work, never to the fruits. Be neither motivated by the fruit of action nor inclined to give up action.

Text:
योगस्थः कुरु कर्म्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय ।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥४८॥
Transcription:
yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya
siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga uchyate [48]
Translation: O Dhanañjaya, give up the ego that you are the doer, and be equipoised in success and failure. Thus stand firm in the plane of yoga and do your prescribed duties. Such a state of balance is indeed known as yoga.

Text:
दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय ।
बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः ॥४९॥
Transcription:
dūreṇa hy avaraṁ karma buddhi-yogād dhanañjaya
buddhau śaraṇam anvichchha kṛpaṇāḥ phala-hetavaḥ [49]
Translation: O Dhanañjaya, (fruitive) action is far inferior to this yoga of wisdom; those motivated by the fruits of their actions are misers. Therefore, seek refuge in the selfless wisdom of equanimity.

Text:
बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते ।
तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्म्मसु कौशलम् ॥५०॥
Transcription:
buddhi-yukto jahātīha ubhe sukṛta-duṣkṛte
tasmād yogāya yujyasva yogaḥ karmasu kauśalam [50]
Translation: The wise person abstains from both good and bad actions in this world. Engage thus in yoga, as yoga is the art of all works.

Text:
कर्म्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः ।
जन्मबन्धविनिर्म्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥५१॥
Transcription:
karma-jaṁ buddhi-yuktā hi phalaṁ tyaktvā manīṣiṇaḥ
janma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ padaṁ gachchhanty anāmayam [51]
Translation: The wise are enlightened by giving up the fruits of their actions, and thus attaining liberation from the bondage of birth, they reach the plane beyond all suffering.

Text:
यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति ।
तदा गन्तासि निर्व्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च ॥५२॥
Transcription:
yadā te moha-kalilaṁ buddhir vyatitariṣyati
tadā gantāsi nirvedaṁ śrotavyasya śrutasya cha [52]
Translation: When your intelligence fully emerges from the dense forest of illusion, you will be indifferent to all that has been heard or is yet to be heard.

Text:
श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला ।
समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि ॥५३॥
Transcription:
śruti-vipratipannā te yadā sthāsyati niśchalā
samādhāv achalā buddhis tadā yogam avāpsyasi [53]
Translation: You shall attain perfect yoga or pure devotion when your intelligence, undisturbed by the apparent contradictions of the Vedas, abides in the Lord.

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव ।
स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥५४॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
sthita-prajñasya kā bhāṣā samādhi-sthasya keśava
sthita-dhīḥ kiṁ prabhāṣeta kim āsīta vrajeta kim [54]
Translation: Arjuna said: O Keśava, what are the characteristics of the person of firmly established wisdom, who is fully absorbed in consciousness of the Supreme (samādhi)? How does such a man of steady mind speak, be, and act in this world?

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
प्रजहाति यदा कामान्सर्व्वान्पार्थ मनोगतान् ।
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥५५॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
prajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha mano-gatān
ātmany evātmanā tuṣṭaḥ sthita-prajñas tadochyate [55]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: O Pārtha, one who rejects all desires that enter the mind and remains self-satisfied within is known as a person whose wisdom is firmly established.

Text:
दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः ।
वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ॥५६॥
Transcription:
duḥkheṣv anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣu vigata-spṛhaḥ
vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir uchyate [56]
Translation: One who is undisturbed by sorrow, whose hankering for pleasure has gone, and who is free from attachment, fear, and anger, is known as a silent sage of steady mind.

Text:
यः सर्व्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम् ।
नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥५७॥
Transcription:
yaḥ sarvatrānabhisnehas tat tat prāpya śubhāśubham
nābhinandati na dveṣṭi tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā [57]
Translation: One who is not attached to anything in this world, who neither enjoys nor resents auspiciousness or inauspiciousness—his wisdom is firmly established.

Text:
यदा संहरते चायं कूर्म्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्व्वशः ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥५८॥
Transcription:
yadā saṁharate chāyaṁ kūrmo ’ṅgānīva sarvaśaḥ
indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā [58]
Translation: When one’s senses are controlled and totally withdrawn from their objects, like the limbs of the tortoise withdrawn in its shell, his wisdom is firmly established.

Text:
विषया विनिवर्त्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः ।
रसवर्ज्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्त्तते ॥५९॥
Transcription:
viṣayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥ
rasa-varjaṁ raso ’py asya paraṁ dṛṣṭvā nivartate [59]
Translation: The embodied being may renounce the sense objects, but the taste for them remains. But that, too, vanishes for the man of wisdom, by his vision of the Supreme.

Text:
यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः ।
इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥६०॥
Transcription:
yatato hy api kaunteya puruṣasya vipaśchitaḥ
indriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ [60]
Translation: And yet, O son of Kuntī, the turbulent senses can forcibly plunder the mind of even the wise person striving for liberation.

Text:
तानि सर्व्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः ।
वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥६१॥
Transcription:
tāni sarvāṇi saṁyamya yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ
vaśe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā [61]
Translation: Devoted to Me, the bhakti-yogī brings his senses under proper control. Firmly established is the wisdom of one whose senses are controlled.

Text:
ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते ।
सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥६२॥
Transcription:
dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate
saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate [62]
Translation: But one whose thoughts dwell on the objects of the senses becomes attached to them. From attachment, desire is born, and from desire, anger arises.

Text:
क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः ।
स्मृतिभ्रंशाद्बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥६३॥
Transcription:
krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛti-vibhramaḥ
smṛti-bhraṁśād buddhi-nāśo buddhi-nāśāt praṇaśyati [63]
Translation: Anger gives rise to delusion, and the power of delusion causes forgetfulness; forgetfulness destroys the intelligence, and when the intelligence is lost, one loses all.

Text:
रागद्वेषविमुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन् ।
आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति ॥६४॥
Transcription:
rāga-dveṣa-vimuktais tu viṣayān indriyaiś charan
ātma-vaśyair vidheyātmā prasādam adhigachchhati [64]
Translation: But the integrated person who can control his mind at will, and who, free from attraction and repulsion, accepts his worldly necessities through his controlled senses, attains tranquillity.

Text:
प्रसादे सर्व्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते ।
प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्यशु बुद्धिः पर्य्यवतिष्ठते ॥६५॥
Transcription:
prasāde sarva-duḥkhānāṁ hānir asyopajāyate
prasanna-chetaso hy āśu buddhiḥ paryavatiṣṭhate [65]
Translation: When one attains a peaceful heart, all his sorrows are dispelled. The tranquil person’s wisdom swiftly becomes steadfast.

Text:
नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना ।
न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥६६॥
Transcription:
nāsti buddhir ayuktasya na chāyuktasya bhāvanā
na chābhāvayataḥ śāntir aśāntasya kutaḥ sukham [66]
Translation: A person bereft of self-control can have neither wisdom nor meditation. Without meditation there is no peace, and without peace, where is the hope for happiness?

Text:
इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते ।
तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ॥६७॥
Transcription:
indriyāṇāṁ hi charatāṁ yan mano ’nuvidhīyate
tad asya harati prajñāṁ vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi [67]
Translation: As a boat on the ocean is thrown off course by an unfavourable wind, similarly, the wisdom of a person lacking self-control is carried away by even one of the roaming senses that the mind might follow.

Text:
तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्व्वशः ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥६८॥
Transcription:
tasmād yasya mahā-bāho nigṛhītāni sarvaśaḥ
indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā [68]
Translation: Therefore, O subduer of the enemy, one whose senses are fully withdrawn from their objects is a person of firmly established wisdom.

Text:
या निशा सर्व्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्त्ति संयमी ।
यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥६९॥
Transcription:
yā niśā sarva-bhūtānāṁ tasyāṁ jāgarti saṁyamī
yasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśā paśyato muneḥ [69]
Translation: That which is day for the self-controlled sage is night for all beings, and that which is day for all beings is night for the sage who sees. (The living beings absorbed in mundane life are asleep to the spiritual joy of the realised soul, who remains indifferent to their quest for the pleasures of the senses, devoid of spiritual joy.)

Text:
आपूर्य्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं
समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् ।
तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्व्वे
स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी ॥७०॥
Transcription:
āpūryamāṇam achala-pratiṣṭhaṁ
samudram āpaḥ praviśanti yadvat
tadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśanti sarve
sa śāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī [70]
Translation: As the ocean remains full and unchanged though many rivers and streams enter into it, the person of firmly established wisdom attains peace despite all desires entering him. But a person who nurtures desires cannot know peace.

Text:
विहाय कामान्यः सर्व्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः ।
निर्म्ममो निरहङ्कारः स शान्तिमधिगच्छति ॥७१॥
Transcription:
vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumāṁś charati niḥspṛhaḥ
nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ sa śāntim adhigachchhati [71]
Translation: Only a person who lives his life abandoning all desires, hankering, ego and possessiveness, can know real peace.

Text:
एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति ।
स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्व्वाणमृच्छति ॥७२॥
Transcription:
eṣā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha naināṁ prāpya vimuhyati
sthitvāsyām anta-kāle ’pi brahma-nirvāṇam ṛchchhati [72]
Translation: O Pārtha, this is realisation of the Absolute, attaining which a person is freed from the delusion of mundane existence. Even at the time of death, only a momentary attainment of this state leads one to the divine abode.

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
ज्यायसी चेत्कर्म्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्द्दन ।
तत्किं कर्म्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव ॥१॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
jyāyasī chet karmaṇas te matā buddhir janārdana
tat kiṁ karmaṇi ghore māṁ niyojayasi keśava [1]
Translation: Arjuna said: O Janārdan, O Keśava, if You consider wisdom superior to action, why, then, do you urge me to engage in the violence of warfare?

Text:
व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे ।
तदेकं वद निश्चत्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम् ॥२॥
Transcription:
vyāmiśreṇeva vākyena buddhiṁ mohayasīva me
tad ekaṁ vada niśchitya yena śreyo ’ham āpnuyām [2]
Translation: I am confused by Your words. They appear to be ambiguous, sometimes supporting action and sometimes supporting wisdom. So please tell me, which is the most beneficial path for me to take?

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ ।
ज्ञानयोगेन साङ्ख्यानां कर्म्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥३॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
loke ’smin dvi-vidhā niṣṭhā purā proktā mayānagha
jñāna-yogena sāṅkhyānāṁ karma-yogena yoginām [3]
Translation: The Supreme Lord replied: O sinless one, I have described the two paths to be found in this world—the yoga of wisdom practised by the realised and the yoga of selfless action practised by those active in the world. (Both paths touch the beginnings of devotional practice since the ladder to the plane of devotion is one, while the practice is twofold according to the stage attained.)

Text:
न कर्म्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते ।
न च सन्न्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥४॥
Transcription:
na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣkarmyaṁ puruṣo ’śnute
na cha sannyasanād eva siddhiṁ samadhigachchhati [4]
Translation: One cannot attain freedom from action by refraining from action. Nor can one attain fulfillment by renunciation alone.

Text:
न हि कश्चत्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्म्मकृत् ।
कार्य्यते ह्यवशः कर्म्म सर्व्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः ॥५॥
Transcription:
na hi kaśchit kṣaṇam api jātu tiṣṭhaty akarmakṛt
kāryate hy avaśaḥ karma sarvaḥ prakṛti-jair guṇaiḥ [5]
Translation: No one can refrain from activity for even a moment. Everyone is impelled to act by the modes of material nature. (Thus the person of impure consciousness should not disregard scripturally prescribed purificatory works.)

Text:
कर्म्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन् ।
इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते ॥६॥
Transcription:
karmendriyāṇi saṁyamya ya āste manasā smaran
indriyārthān vimūḍhātmā mithyāchāraḥ sa uchyate [6]
Translation: One who externally restrains his hands, legs, and other senses of action, but whose mind dwells on sense objects, is a fool. Know him to be a hypocrite.

Text:
यस्त्वन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्ज्जुन ।
कर्म्मेन्द्रियैः कर्म्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥७॥
Transcription:
yas tv indriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate ’rjuna
karmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam asaktaḥ sa viśiṣyate [7]
Translation: O Arjuna, superior is one who (though married) has stabilised his senses by the mind and engaged in the yoga of selfless action.

Text:
नियतं कुरु कर्म्म त्वं कर्म्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्म्मणः ।
शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्म्मणः ॥८॥
Transcription:
niyataṁ kuru karma tvaṁ karma jyāyo hy akarmaṇaḥ
śarīra-yātrāpi cha te na prasiddhyed akarmaṇaḥ [8]
Translation: Perform your prescribed duties, since to be active is better than idleness. Inactive persons cannot even maintain their existence. (By giving up fruitive action and performing your obligatory duties, your heart will be purified. Surpassing the plane of renunciation, you will attain pure devotion, transcendental to the mundane plane.)

Text:
यज्ञार्थात्कर्म्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्म्मबन्धनः ।
तदर्थं कर्म्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसङ्गः समाचर ॥९॥
Transcription:
yajñārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra loko ’yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ
tad-arthaṁ karma kaunteya mukta-saṅgaḥ samāchara [9]
Translation: Selfless duty performed as an offering to the Supreme Lord is called yajña, or sacrifice. O Arjuna, all action performed for any other purpose is the cause of bondage in this world of repeated birth and death. Therefore, remaining unattached to the fruits of action, perform all your duties in the spirit of such sacrifice. (Such selfless action is the way to begin devotional practice, gradually realise the presence of the Lord, and ultimately attain pure devotion transcendental to the modes of material nature [nirguṇa bhakti]).

Text:
सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः ।
अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक् ॥१०॥
Transcription:
saha-yajñāḥ prajāḥ sṛṣṭvā purovācha prajāpatiḥ
anena prasaviṣyadhvam eṣa vo ’stv iṣṭa-kāma-dhuk [10]
Translation: In the beginning, Lord Brahmā manifested the progeny of the world, along with sacrifices. He instructed them thus: “Take shelter of this religious principle of sacrifice; prosper and flourish. May such sacrifice be the bestower of the things you desire.”

Text:
देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः ।
परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ ॥११॥
Transcription:
devān bhāvayatānena te devā bhāvayantu vaḥ
parasparaṁ bhāvayantaḥ śreyaḥ param avāpsyatha [11]
Translation: “Propitiate the gods by sacrifices, and may they satisfy you by bestowing your desired fruits. In this way, through mutual nourishment, you will be the gainers of great auspiciousness.”

Text:
इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः ।
तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः ॥१२॥
Transcription:
iṣṭān bhogān hi vo devā dāsyante yajña-bhāvitāḥ
tair dattān apradāyaibhyo yo bhuṅkte stena eva saḥ [12]
Translation: Propitiated by sacrifices, the gods (My worldly representatives) will bestow upon you all your desired enjoyable things. But one who enjoys what is given by the gods without offering it to them, is a thief.

Text:
यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्व्वकिल्बिषैः ।
भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् ॥१३॥
Transcription:
yajña-śiṣṭāśinaḥ santo muchyante sarva-kilbiṣaiḥ
bhuñjate te tv aghaṁ pāpā ye pachanty ātma-kāraṇāt [13]
Translation: By accepting sacrificial remnants of the universal gods*, virtuous persons are liberated from all sins arising from violence towards other living entities. But those who prepare food for their own pleasure partake only of sin.
Comment: * The remnants referred to here are not the same as the Kṛṣṇa-prasād (food that has been offered to the Supreme Lord by His devotees). See 9.20–26.

Text:
अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्ज्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः ।
यज्ञाद्भवति पर्ज्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्म्मसमुद्भवः ॥१४॥
Transcription:
annād bhavanti bhūtāni parjanyād anna-sambhavaḥ
yajñād bhavati parjanyo yajñaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ [14]
Translation: The living beings’ bodies are manifest from food, food is manifest due to rain, rainfall ensues from the performance of sacrifice, and action is the cause of sacrifice.

Text:
कर्म्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम् ।
तस्मात्सर्व्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥१५॥
Transcription:
karma brahmodbhavaṁ viddhi brahmākṣara-samudbhavam
tasmāt sarva-gataṁ brahma nityaṁ yajñe pratiṣṭhitam [15]
Translation: Action is prescribed in the Vedas, and the Vedas originate in Akṣara, the Imperishable. Therefore, the all-pervading, imperishable Lord is ever present within the acts of sacrifice offered to Him.

Text:
एवं प्रवर्त्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्त्तयतीह यः ।
अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति ॥१६॥
Transcription:
evaṁ pravartitaṁ chakraṁ nānuvartayatīha yaḥ
aghāyur indriyārāmo moghaṁ pārtha sa jīvati [16]
Translation: O Arjuna, one who does not respect this natural causal cycle leads a life of sin, enjoying the senses in vain.

Text:
यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः ।
आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्य्यं न विद्यते ॥१७॥
Transcription:
yas tv ātma-ratir eva syād ātma-tṛptaś cha mānavaḥ
ātmany eva cha santuṣṭas tasya kāryaṁ na vidyate [17]
Translation: Yet there is no duty to fulfill for the realised person who delights within the self, being fully satisfied within. (His only worldly action or karma is as required for his subsistence.)

Text:
नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन ।
न चास्य सर्व्वभूतेषु कश्चदर्थव्यपाश्रयः ॥१८॥
Transcription:
naiva tasya kṛtenārtho nākṛteneha kaśchana
na chāsya sarva-bhūteṣu kaśchid artha-vyapāśrayaḥ [18]
Translation: In the world, he neither gains by action, nor loses by inaction. He does not depend on any other being (from Lord Brahmā down to the simplest life-form) for any purpose.

Text:
तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्य्यं कर्म्म समाचर ।
असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः ॥१९॥
Transcription:
tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samāchara
asakto hy ācharan karma param āpnoti pūruṣaḥ [19]
Translation: So perform your prescribed duties without attachment. By selflessly executing one’s duties a person attains liberation. (True liberation is the state of pure devotion, attained in the maturity of selfless action.)

Text:
कर्म्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः ।
लोकसङ्ग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन्कर्त्तुमर्हसि ॥२०॥
Transcription:
karmaṇaiva hi saṁsiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥ
loka-saṅgraham evāpi sampaśyan kartum arhasi [20]
Translation: King Janaka and other learned personalities succeeded in attaining devotion through the execution of their prescribed duties. So it is fitting that you fulfill your duty in consideration of the welfare of the people.

Text:
यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः ।
स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्त्तते ॥२१॥
Transcription:
yad yad ācharati śreṣṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ
sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate [21]
Translation: The masses follow the ways of great men, following the standards they set.

Text:
न मे पार्थास्ति कर्त्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन ।
नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त्त एव च कर्म्मणि ॥२२॥
Transcription:
na me pārthāsti kartavyaṁ triṣu lokeṣu kiñchana
nānavāptam avāptavyaṁ varta eva cha karmaṇi [22]
Translation: O Arjuna, I have no duty in the three worlds. I lack nothing, and there is nothing to be attained by Me; and yet, I, Myself, am active.

Text:
यदि ह्यहं न वर्त्तेयं जातु कर्म्मण्यतन्द्रितः ।
मम वर्त्त्मानुवर्त्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्व्वशः ॥२३॥
Transcription:
yadi hy ahaṁ na varteyaṁ jātu karmaṇy atandritaḥ
mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ [23]
Translation: O Arjuna, if ever I do not engage attentively in duties, then all men, following My example, will give up their duties.

Text:
उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्य्यां कर्म्म चेदहम् ।
सङ्करस्य च कर्त्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः ॥२४॥
Transcription:
utsīdeyur ime lokā na kuryāṁ karma ched aham
saṅkarasya cha kartā syām upahanyām imāḥ prajāḥ [24]
Translation: If I do not engage in duty, the people of these worlds will renounce their duties and fall to ruination. Thus I will be responsible for their downfall, caused by adulterated progeny.

Text:
सक्ताः कर्म्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्व्वन्ति भारत ।
कुर्य्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्चिकीर्षुर्लोकसङ्ग्रहम् ॥२५॥
Transcription:
saktāḥ karmaṇy avidvāṁso yathā kurvanti bhārata
kuryād vidvāṁs tathāsaktaś chikīrṣur loka-saṅgraham [25]
Translation: O Arjuna, as ignorant persons work with attachment, the wise, too, must work, but without attachment, in order to guide the general people who exist in the plane of worldly action. (They differ not in their work but in their attitudes of attachment and detachment.)

Text:
न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्म्मसङ्गिनाम् ।
योजयेत्सर्व्वकर्म्माणि विद्वान्युक्तः समाचरन् ॥२६॥
Transcription:
na buddhi-bhedaṁ janayed ajñānāṁ karma-saṅginām
yojayet sarva-karmāṇi vidvān yuktaḥ samācharan [26]
Translation: The scripturally learned teacher should not create confusion in the minds of ignorant, attached men by apparently advocating the neglect of duty for the cultivation of knowledge. Rather, he should set the example of selflessness in fulfilling duties, thus encouraging the ignorant in the performance of their prescribed duties.

Text:
प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्म्माणि सर्व्वशः ।
अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्त्ताहमिति मन्यते ॥२७॥
Transcription:
prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ
ahaṅkāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate [27]
Translation: All actions in the world are in every respect effected by the modes of material nature (which impell the senses). But a man deluded by bodily identification thinks, “I alone am accomplishing this work.”

Text:
तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्म्मविभागयोः ।
गुणा गुणेषु वर्त्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते ॥२८॥
Transcription:
tattvavit tu mahā-bāho guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ
guṇā guṇeṣu vartanta iti matvā na sajjate [28]
Translation: However, O mighty-armed Arjuna, one who is in knowledge of the division of the modes of material nature and action, knows that the modes are engaging within the modes, and he is detached from them. (One who knows that the three modes activated by the demigods impell the engagement of the senses with their objects, knows himself to be independent of these elements, therefore he does not consider himself a doer.)

Text:
प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढाः सज्जन्ते गुणकर्म्मसु ।
तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत् ॥२९॥
Transcription:
prakṛter guṇa-sammūḍhāḥ sajjante guṇa-karmasu
tān akṛtsna-vido mandān kṛtsna-vin na vichālayet [29]
Translation: Persons bewildered by the modes of nature are absorbed in the functions of the modes (the engagement of the senses with their objects). Enlightened persons should not agitate such ignorant, unfortunate persons by trying to convince them of higher philosophical truths. (Rather, they should advise the conditioned souls to practise selfless action, which liberates them from absorption in the modes.)

Text:
मयि सर्व्वाणि कर्म्माणि सन्न्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा ।
निराशीर्निर्म्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः ॥३०॥
Transcription:
mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-chetasā
nirāśīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ [30]
Translation: Surrender all your activities to Me with this understanding: “All my actions are under the control of the indwelling Lord.” In such consciousness, free from possessiveness and anguish, take recourse to battle (as your natural duty).

Text:
ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः ।
श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्म्मभिः ॥३१॥
Transcription:
ye me matam idaṁ nityam anutiṣṭhanti mānavāḥ
śraddhāvanto ’nasūyanto muchyante te ’pi karmabhiḥ [31]
Translation: Faithful, unbegrudging men who always practise My teaching, this yoga of selfless action, are liberated from the bondage of action, even though they engage in action.

Text:
ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम् ।
सर्व्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः ॥३२॥
Transcription:
ye tv etad abhyasūyanto nānutiṣṭhanti me matam
sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhāṁs tān viddhi naṣṭān achetasaḥ [32]
Translation: But the arrogant who resent My teaching are bereft of all knowledge. Know them to be lost in ignorance.

Text:
सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि ।
प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति ॥३३॥
Transcription:
sadṛśaṁ cheṣṭate svasyāḥ prakṛter jñānavān api
prakṛtiṁ yānti bhūtāni nigrahaḥ kiṁ kariṣyati [33]
Translation: For even a knowledgable person tends to act according to his nature (mundane inclinations). The living beings’ endeavours are dictated by their natures. What, then, can repression accomplish?

Text:
इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ ।
तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ ॥३४॥
Transcription:
indriyasyendriyasyārthe rāga-dveṣau vyavasthitau
tayor na vaśam āgachchhet tau hy asya paripanthinau [34]
Translation: Although the senses are attracted to and repelled by the sense objects, one must not be controlled by these urges, for they are the greatest enemy of the candidate for self-realisation.

Text:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्म्मो विगुणः परधर्म्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् ।
स्वधर्म्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्म्मो भयावहः ॥३५॥
Transcription:
śreyān svadharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt svanuṣṭhitāt
svadharme nidhanaṁ śreyaḥ para-dharmo bhayāvahaḥ [35]
Translation: Even if it is imperfect, it is better to do one’s duty according to one’s own nature, than to do another’s well. Even death in the discharge of one’s own duty is better, for to perform another’s is dangerous.
Comment:
The eternal, superexcellent, natural function (dharma) of the soul is pure devotion to Adhokṣaja, the transcendental Lord. Therefore, even if this function is somewhat imperfectly attempted by one with external malpractices, such an attempt will be superior to the ‘good’ practices within the material modes of nature and worldly chain of actions and habits foreign to the soul’s nature. Even if death occurs during the cultivation of such pure devotion in proper saintly association, it is the bestower of the ultimate good; but in the name of good practices to ignorantly follow ‘another’s path’—anything other than devotion to the Lord (dvitīyābhiniveś)—is, endangering to the spiritual progress of the soul.*

* भयं द्वितीयाभिनिवेशतः स्याद्
ईशादपेतस्य विपर्य्ययोऽस्मृतिः ।
तन्माययातो बुध आभजेत्तं
भक्त्यैकयेशं गुरुदेवतात्मा ॥

bhayaṁ dvitīyābhiniveśataḥ syād
īśād apetasya viparyayo ’smṛtiḥ
tan māyayāto budha ābhajet taṁ
bhaktyaikayeśaṁ guru-devatātmā
(SB: 11.2.37)

“A person of fine intelligence will serve with single-minded devotion his Lord who is his teacher and dearmost friend. But those who have turned away from the Lord adopt the illusory ego of considering the perishable body to be the self, forgetting their true identities. Their consciousness absorbed in ‘secondary pursuits’, i.e., mundane objectives, they are always fearful on account of the body and its attachments.”

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापञ्चरति पूरुषः ।
अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः ॥३६॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
atha kena prayukto ’yaṁ pāpañ charati pūruṣaḥ
anichchhann api vārṣṇeya balād iva niyojitaḥ [36]
Translation: Arjuna inquired: O descendant of the Vṛṣṇis, by what is the living being compelled to commit sinful activities, even unwillingly, as if by force?

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः ।
महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥३७॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
kāma eṣa krodha eṣa rajoguṇa-samudbhavaḥ
mahāśano mahā-pāpmā viddhy enam iha vairiṇam [37]
Translation: The Supreme Lord replied: It is the desire to enjoy the mundane that induces a person to commit sin, and in different situations it produces anger. It is utterly insatiable, extremely malicious, and the worst enemy of the living being in this world.

Text:
धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथादर्शो मलेन च ।
यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम् ॥३८॥
Transcription:
dhūmenāvriyate vahnir yathādarśo malena cha
yatholbenāvṛto garbhas tathā tenedam āvṛtam [38]
Translation: As fire is thinly veiled by smoke, as a mirror is thickly covered with dust, and as the embryo remains completely enclosed within the womb, similarly, this desire covers the consciousness of the living being (in these three degrees of intensity, according to the modes of material nature—goodness, passion, and ignorance respectively).

Text:
आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा ।
कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च ॥३९॥
Transcription:
āvṛtaṁ jñānam etena jñānino nitya-vairiṇā
kāma-rūpeṇa kaunteya duṣpūreṇānalena cha [39]
Translation: Knowledge is covered by desire, the constant enemy of the wise. It burns like an insatiable fire (never satisfied by offerings of butter).

Text:
इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते ।
एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम् ॥४०॥
Transcription:
indriyāṇi mano buddhir asyādhiṣṭhānam uchyate
etair vimohayaty eṣa jñānam āvṛtya dehinam [40]
Translation: It is said that this enemy, desire, dwelling within the senses, mind, and intelligence, covers the perception of the embodied living being and deludes him.

Text:
तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ ।
पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम् ॥४१॥
Transcription:
tasmāt tvam indriyāṇy ādau niyamya bharatarṣabha
pāpmānaṁ prajahi hy enaṁ jñāna-vijñāna-nāśanam [41]
Translation: Therefore, O noblest of the Bhāratas, control your senses and destroy this desire, the embodiment of sin, which destroys both knowledge and realisation.

Text:
इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः ।
मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्बुद्धेर्यः परतस्तु सः ॥४२॥
Transcription:
indriyāṇi parāṇy āhur indriyebhyaḥ paraṁ manaḥ
manasas tu parā buddhir buddher yaḥ paratas tu saḥ [42]
Translation: It is said by the wise that the senses are superior to matter, the mind is superior to the senses, and the intelligence is superior to the mind; and the soul is superior to even the intelligence.

Text:
एवं बुद्धेः परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना ।
जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम् ॥४३॥
Transcription:
evaṁ buddheḥ paraṁ buddhvā saṁstabhyātmānam ātmanā
jahi śatruṁ mahā-bāho kāma-rūpaṁ durāsadam [43]
Translation: O mighty Arjuna, knowing the soul to be superior to the intelligence, steady the mind with resolute intelligence and destroy the formidable enemy, desire.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
इमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम् ।
विवस्वान्मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत् ॥१॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
imaṁ vivasvate yogaṁ proktavān aham avyayam
vivasvān manave prāha manur ikṣvākave ’bravīt [1]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: I imparted this imperishable yoga of knowledge, achieved by selfless action, to Sūrya, the presiding deity of the sun. Sūrya taught it to his son Vaivasvata Manu, who passed it on to his son Ikṣvāku.

Text:
एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदुः ।
स कालेनेह महता योगो नष्टः परन्तप ॥२॥
Transcription:
evaṁ paramparā-prāptam imaṁ rājarṣayo viduḥ
sa kāleneha mahatā yogo naṣṭaḥ parantapa [2]
Translation: O conqueror of the enemy, thus the saintly kings such as Nimi, Janaka, and others learned this yoga, which was passed down to them by disciplic succession. But in the course of time, this teaching has been lost.

Text:
स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योगः प्रोक्तः पुरातनः ।
भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम् ॥३॥
Transcription:
sa evāyaṁ mayā te ’dya yogaḥ proktaḥ purātanaḥ
bhakto ’si me sakhā cheti rahasyaṁ hy etad uttamam [3]
Translation: As you are my devotee and friend, I now reveal to you this supreme secret—the eternal yoga.

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
अपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः ।
कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति ॥४॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
aparaṁ bhavato janma paraṁ janma vivasvataḥ
katham etad vijānīyāṁ tvam ādau proktavān iti [4]
Translation: Arjuna said: Vivasvān, the sun-god, was born in ancient times, long before You were. Therefore, how am I to consider that You previously gave these teachings to him?

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
बहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चर्ज्जुन ।
तान्यहं वेद सर्व्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप ॥५॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
bahūni me vyatītāni janmāni tava chārjuna
tāny ahaṁ veda sarvāṇi na tvaṁ vettha parantapa [5]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: O chastiser of the enemy, both you and I have passed through many births. I can remember all of them, whereas you, O Arjuna, cannot.

Text:
अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन् ।
प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय सम्भवाम्यात्ममायया ॥६॥
Transcription:
ajo ’pi sann avyayātmā bhūtānām īśvaro ’pi san
prakṛtiṁ svām adhiṣṭhāya sambhavāmy ātma-māyayā [6]
Translation: Although My eternal form is transcendental to birth and death, and I am the Lord of all beings, I appear within the world in My divine nature, by My sweet will, extending My internal potency.

Text:
यदा यदा हि धर्म्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत ।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्म्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥७॥
Transcription:
yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata
abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛjāmy aham [7]
Translation: O Bhārata, whenever there is a decline of religion and an uprising of irreligion, I personally make My advent.

Text:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् ।
धर्म्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे ॥८॥
Transcription:
paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ vināśāya cha duṣkṛtām
dharma-saṁsthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge [8]
Translation: I appear age after age to deliver the saintly devotees, vanquish the miscreants, and firmly establish true religion.

Text:
जन्म कर्म्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ।
त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्ज्जुन ॥९॥
Transcription:
janma karma cha me divyam evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ
tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma naiti mām eti so ’rjuna [9]
Translation: O Arjuna, one who comes to know thus the truth of My Pastimes of divine birth and activities, does not undergo rebirth. After giving up this body, he attains Me. (My grace is revealed to him as My personal transcendental joyful potency, hlādinī-śakti. His heart is melted in divine love, and he attains My eternal devotional service.)

Text:
वीतरागभयक्रोधा मन्मया मामुपाश्रिताः ।
बहवो ज्ञानतपसा पूता मद्भावमागताः ॥१०॥
Transcription:
vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhā man-mayā mām upāśritāḥ
bahavo jñāna-tapasā pūtā mad-bhāvam āgatāḥ [10]
Translation: Free from attachment, fear, and anger, many persons have taken shelter in Me, absorbing their hearts in My services (hearing, chanting, and remembering). Purified by knowledge and the fire of ordeal in My service, they attained divine love for Me.

Text:
ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम् ।
मम वर्त्त्मानुवर्त्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्व्वशः ॥११॥
Transcription:
ye yathā māṁ prapadyante tāṁs tathaiva bhajāmy aham
mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ [11]
Translation: Howsoever people surrender to Me, I accordingly reward them. Being the ultimate goal of all paths, I am the objective to be attained by all. O Pārtha, all men follow My various paths.

Text:
काङ्क्षन्तः कर्म्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवताः ।
क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्म्मजा ॥१२॥
Transcription:
kāṅkṣantaḥ karmaṇāṁ siddhiṁ yajanta iha devatāḥ
kṣipraṁ hi mānuṣe loke siddhir bhavati karmajā [12]
Translation: Persons who desire the fruit of their actions worship the gods, since in the human plane, actions swiftly bear fruit.

Text:
चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्म्मविभागशः ।
तस्य कर्त्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्त्तारमव्ययम् ॥१३॥
Transcription:
chātur-varṇyaṁ mayā sṛṣṭaṁ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśaḥ
tasya kartāram api māṁ viddhy akartāram avyayam [13]
Translation: The four social divisions, classified according to the material modes of nature and corresponding activities, are made manifest by Me. Although I am the doer of this, know Me to be the non-doer and unchangable.

Text:
न मां कर्म्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्म्मफले स्पृहा ।
इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्म्मभिर्न स बध्यते ॥१४॥
Transcription:
na māṁ karmāṇi limpanti na me karma-phale spṛhā
iti māṁ yo ’bhijānāti karmabhir na sa badhyate [14]
Translation: I am never implicated by action nor do I desire the fruits of action. One who knows Me thus (as the Supreme Lord, transcendental to worldly action) is never bound by action.

Text:
एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म्म पूर्व्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः ।
कुरु कर्म्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्व्वैः पूर्व्वतरं कृतम् ॥१५॥
Transcription:
evaṁ jñātvā kṛtaṁ karma pūrvair api mumukṣubhiḥ
kuru karmaiva tasmāt tvaṁ pūrvaiḥ pūrvataraṁ kṛtam [15]
Translation: Knowing this basic principle, seekers of liberation in previous times renounced all fruitive work and performed their duties as a selfless offering to Me. Likewise, you should adopt this yoga of selfless action as did the great saintly persons of yore.

Text:
किं कर्म्म किमकर्म्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिताः ।
तत्ते कर्म्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥१६॥
Transcription:
kiṁ karma kim akarmeti kavayo ’py atra mohitāḥ
tat te karma pravakṣyāmi yaj jñātvā mokṣyase ’śubhāt [16]
Translation: What is action? What is inaction? Even the learned have difficulty understanding this. Hence, I shall enlighten you in this matter, knowing which you will liberated from inauspiciousness.

Text:
कर्म्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यञ्च विकर्म्मणः ।
अकर्म्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्म्मणो गतिः ॥१७॥
Transcription:
karmaṇo hy api boddhavyaṁ boddhavyañ cha vikarmaṇaḥ
akarmaṇaś cha boddhavyaṁ gahanā karmaṇo gatiḥ [17]
Translation: The true nature of action is difficult to comprehend. One should understand prescribed action, prohibited action, and inaction.

Text:
कर्म्मण्यकर्म्म यः पश्येदकर्म्मणि च कर्म्म यः ।
स बुद्धिमान्मनुष्येषु स युक्तः कृत्स्नकर्म्मकृत् ॥१८॥
Transcription:
karmaṇy akarma yaḥ paśyed akarmaṇi cha karma yaḥ
sa buddhimān manuṣyeṣu sa yuktaḥ kṛtsna-karma-kṛt [18]
Translation: One who sees action within inaction, and inaction within action, is wise among men. He is a yogī and a performer of all actions.

Text:
यस्य सर्व्वे समारम्भाः कामसङ्कल्पवर्ज्जिताः ।
ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्म्माणं तमाहुः पण्डितं बुधाः ॥१९॥
Transcription:
yasya sarve samārambhāḥ kāma-saṅkalpa-varjitāḥ
jñānāgni-dagdha-karmāṇaṁ tam āhuḥ paṇḍitaṁ budhāḥ [19]
Translation: One whose every action is without fruitive desire, and who burns all actions in the fire of knowledge, is described by the wise as learned.

Text:
त्यक्त्वा कर्म्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रयः ।
कर्म्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति सः ॥२०॥
Transcription:
tyaktvā karma-phalāsaṅgaṁ nitya-tṛpto nirāśrayaḥ
karmaṇy abhipravṛtto ’pi naiva kiñchit karoti saḥ [20]
Translation: Such a person, giving up attachment to the fruits of action, fully content in the eternal bliss within, and free from mundane dependence, though fully active, does nothing at all.

Text:
निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्व्वपरिग्रहः ।
शारीरं केवलं कर्म्म कुर्व्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥२१॥
Transcription:
nirāśīr yata-chittātmā tyakta-sarva-parigrahaḥ
śārīraṁ kevalaṁ karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣam [21]
Translation: Desireless, controlling mind and body by the intelligence, and renouncing all possessiveness and acquisition, no sinful or pious reaction is incurred by such a person who may act to acquire mundane objects merely for his bodily sustenance.

Text:
यदृच्छालाभसन्तुष्टो द्वन्द्वातीतो विमत्सरः ।
समः सिद्धावसिद्धौ च कृत्वापि न निबध्यते ॥२२॥
Transcription:
yadṛchchhā-lābha-santuṣṭo dvandvātīto vimatsaraḥ
samaḥ siddhāv asiddhau cha kṛtvāpi na nibadhyate [22]
Translation: Content with whatever is readily available and unaffected by dualities (of pleasure and pain, attraction and aversion), such a person has no envy. He is equipoised, neither elated nor dejected in success or failure. Though active, he is not bound by any action.

Text:
गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः।
यज्ञायाचरतः कर्म्म समग्रं प्रविलीयते ॥२३॥
Transcription:
gata-saṅgasya muktasya jñānāvasthita-chetasaḥ
yajñāyācharataḥ karma samagraṁ pravilīyate [23]
Translation: For one who is detached, enlightened, and liberated, all his actions, performed in the spirit of sacrifice, are perfectly dissipated.
Comment:
The actions of one practising the yoga of selfless action do not lead to apūrva as considered by the philosophers known as karma-mīmāṁsaka. According to their ethical yet atheistic ideology, the karma-mīmāṁsakas or mundane rationalists claim that pious actions produce an unseen, subtle potency known as apūrva, which must fructify at the appropriate time after death. Their conception that this fruit can later be shared by others is meant to show the eternality of karma, or action, but it ignores the presence of the Supreme Autocrat.
So the statement of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, “: all actions are dissipated,” ought not be misinterpreted to mean that the actions of liberated souls cause some such remote worldly consequence, or apūrva. Rather, the Lord indicates that the actions offered to Him by the selfless karma-yogī do not cause any subsequent reaction to be either enjoyed or suffered by others in this mundane plane.

Text:
ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्म हविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम् ।
ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्म्मसमधिना ॥२४॥
Transcription:
brahmārpaṇaṁ brahma havir brahmāgnau brahmaṇā hutam
brahmaiva tena gantavyaṁ brahma-karma-samadhinā [24]
Translation: The ladle is Brahma, the oblation is Brahma offered by Brahma into the fire of Brahma. One whose consciousness is fully absorbed in Brahma action certainly attains to Brahma, the Absolute.

Text:
दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिनः पर्य्युपासते ।
ब्रह्माग्नावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति ॥२५॥
Transcription:
daivam evāpare yajñaṁ yoginaḥ paryupāsate
brahmāgnāv apare yajñaṁ yajñenaivopajuhvati [25]
Translation: Some yogīs offer sacrifices to the demigods, and others offer the self into the fire of Brahma by vibrating the praṇava mantra, Om.

Text:
श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्निषु जुह्वति ।
शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्निषु जुह्वति ॥२६॥
Transcription:
śrotrādīnīndriyāṇy anye saṁyamāgniṣu juhvati
śabdādīn viṣayān anya indriyāgniṣu juhvati [26]
Translation: Some offer their senses of hearing, touching, seeing, tasting, and smelling into the fires of self-control; yet others offer the sense objects of sound, touch, form, taste, and smell into the fire of the senses.

Text:
सर्व्वाणीन्द्रियकर्म्माणि प्राणकर्म्माणि चापरे ।
आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते ॥२७॥
Transcription:
sarvāṇīndriya-karmāṇi prāṇa-karmāṇi chāpare
ātma-saṁyama-yogāgnau juhvati jñāna-dīpite [27]
Translation: Again, there are the yogīs who offer all the functions of the senses and the life forces into the yogic fire of self-purification ignited by knowledge.

Text:
द्रव्ययज्ञास्तपोयज्ञा योगयज्ञास्तथापरे ।
स्वाध्यायज्ञानयज्ञाश्च यतयः संशितव्रताः ॥२८॥
Transcription:
dravya-yajñās tapo-yajñā yoga-yajñās tathāpare
svādhyāya-jñāna-yajñāś cha yatayaḥ saṁśita-vratāḥ [28]
