Translation: Sincere in their practices and faithful to their vows, some persons sacrifice their possessions, some undergo strict penances as a sacrifice, some practise the eightfold mystic yoga as a sacrifice, and yet others perform ‘knowledge-sacrifice’ by singing and introspectively studying the Vedas.

Text:
अपाने जुह्वति प्राणं प्राणेऽपानं तथापरे ।
प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणाः ।
अपरे नियताहाराः प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति ॥२९॥
Transcription:
apāne juhvati prāṇaṁ prāṇe ’pānaṁ tathāpare
prāṇāpāna-gatī ruddhvā prāṇāyāma-parāyaṇāḥ
apare niyatāhārāḥ prāṇān prāṇeṣu juhvati [29]
Translation: Others practise breath-control. Closing the right nostril and inhaling through the left, they bring the ascending air into union with the descending; closing the left nostril and exhaling through the right, they similarly offer the descending air into the ascending; then closing both nostrils, they check both ascending and descending airs. Yet other practitioners of sense-control offer all the senses into the life-airs by reducing their food intake.

Text:
सर्व्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षयितकल्मषाः ।
यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो यान्ति ब्रह्म सनातनम् ॥३०॥
Transcription:
sarve ’py ete yajña-vido yajña-kṣayita-kalmaṣāḥ
yajña-śiṣṭāmṛta-bhujo yānti brahma sanātanam [30]
Translation: All these persons are knowers of sacrifice, purified by sacrifice. Enjoying the nectarean remnants of sacrifice, they attain to the eternal plane of the Absolute.

Text: नायं लोकोऽस्त्ययज्ञस्य कुतोऽन्यः कुरुसत्तम ॥३१॥
Transcription: nāyaṁ loko ’sty ayajñasya kuto ’nyaḥ kuru-sattama [31]
Translation: O Arjuna, foremost of the Kurus, without sacrifice one cannot attain fulfillment in this world, to say nothing of the next.

Text:
एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे ।
कर्म्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्व्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे ॥३२॥
Transcription:
evaṁ bahu-vidhā yajñā vitatā brahmaṇo mukhe
karma-jān viddhi tān sarvān evaṁ jñātvā vimokṣyase [32]
Translation: Thus many types of sacrifice are described in the Vedas and allied scriptures. Know that they are all accomplished through action (of thought, word, and deed). Knowing this, you will be liberated from the bondage of action.

Text:
श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप ।
सर्व्वं कर्म्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते ॥३३॥
Transcription:
śreyān dravyamayād yajñāj jñāna-yajñaḥ parantapa
sarvaṁ karmākhilaṁ pārtha jñāne parisamāpyate [33]
Translation: O Arjuna, subduer of the enemy, ‘knowledge-sacrifice’ is superior to the sacrifice of material objects, because the perfection of all action, including its fruit, is its culmination in knowledge.

Text:
तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया ।
उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ॥३४॥
Transcription:
tad viddhi praṇipātena paripraśnena sevayā
upadekṣyanti te jñānaṁ jñāninas tattva-darśinaḥ [34]
Translation: You will be able to attain knowledge by satisfying the divine master with submission, relevant inquiry, and sincere service. The enlightened souls who are learned in scriptural knowledge and endowed with direct realisation of the Supreme Absolute Truth will impart divine knowledge to you.

Text:
यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव ।
येन भूतान्यशेषाणि द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि ॥३५॥
Transcription:
yaj jñātvā na punar moham evaṁ yāsyasi pāṇḍava
yena bhūtāny aśeṣāṇi drakṣyasy ātmany atho mayi [35]
Translation: O Pāṇḍava, when you are enlightened by this knowledge of the reality, you will no longer be subject to your present illusion. You will see the one spiritual nature in all species of life, from highest to lowest, and that all are situated within Me.

Text:
अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्व्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः ।
सर्व्वं ज्ञानप्लवेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि ॥३६॥
Transcription:
api ched asi pāpebhyaḥ sarvebhyaḥ pāpa-kṛttamaḥ
sarvaṁ jñāna-plavenaiva vṛjinaṁ santariṣyasi [36]
Translation: Even if you are the most sinful of all sinners, you will cross the ocean of sins by the boat of knowledge.

Text:
यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्निर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्ज्जुन ।
ज्ञानाग्निः सर्व्वकर्म्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा ॥३७॥
Transcription:
yathaidhāṁsi samiddho ’gnir bhasmasāt kurute ’rjuna
jñānāgniḥ sarva-karmāṇi bhasmasāt kurute tathā [37]
Translation: As a blazing fire renders wood to ashes, O Arjuna, the fire of knowledge burns up all worldly actions.

Text:
न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते ।
तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति ॥३८॥
Transcription:
na hi jñānena sadṛśaṁ pavitram iha vidyate
tat svayaṁ yoga-saṁsiddhaḥ kālenātmani vindati [38]
Translation: In this world, there is nothing as pure as knowledge. Eventually, a person who has achieved perfection in the yoga of selfless action naturally realises this knowledge within his heart.

Text:
श्रद्धावान्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः ।
ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥३९॥
Transcription:
śraddhāvān labhate jñānaṁ tat-paraḥ saṁyatendriyaḥ
jñānaṁ labdhvā parāṁ śāntim achireṇādhigachchati [39]
Translation: It is the devoted, faithful, and sense-controlled person who attains knowledge. Achieving enlightenment, such a person soon attains the supreme peace.

Text:
अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति ।
नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मनः ॥४०॥
Transcription:
ajñaś chāśraddadhānaś cha saṁśayātmā vinaśyati
nāyaṁ loko ’sti na paro na sukhaṁ saṁśayātmanaḥ [40]
Translation: The ignorant, faithless, and doubting person is doomed to misfortune. For the doubting soul there can be happiness neither in this life nor the next.

Text:
योगसन्न्यस्तकर्म्माणं ज्ञानसञ्छिन्नसंशयम् ।
आत्मवन्तं न कर्म्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय ॥४१॥
Transcription:
yoga-sannyasta-karmāṇaṁ jñāna-sañchhinna-saṁśayam
ātmavantaṁ na karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanañjaya [41]
Translation: O Dhanañjaya, renouncing all worldly actions by following the path of selfless action, one who severs all his doubts by knowledge and realises his internal divine nature, can never be bound by action.

Text:
तस्मादज्ञानसम्भूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनात्मनः
छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत ॥४२॥
Transcription:
tasmād ajñāna-sambhūtaṁ hṛt-sthaṁ jñānāsinātmanaḥ
chhittvainaṁ saṁśayaṁ yogam ātiṣṭhottiṣṭha bhārata [42]
Translation: Therefore, O Bhārat, with the sword of knowledge slash to shreds all these doubts in your heart, which are born of ignorance. Take refuge in selfless action and arise for battle!

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
सन्न्यासं कर्म्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगञ्च शंससि ।
यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्चितम् ॥१॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
sannyāsaṁ karmaṇāṁ kṛṣṇa punar yogañ cha śaṁsasi
yach chhreya etayor ekaṁ tan me brūhi suniśchitam [1]
Translation: Arjuna said: O Kṛṣṇa, you speak of renunciation of actions, and then again, You speak of the path of selfless action. Therefore, please tell me clearly which is superior.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
सन्न्यासः कर्म्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ ।
तयोस्तु कर्म्मसन्न्यासात्कर्म्मयोगो विशिष्यते ॥२॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
sannyāsaḥ karma-yogaś cha niḥśreyasa-karāv ubhau
tayos tu karma-sannyāsāt karma-yogo viśiṣyate [2]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: Both renunciation of action and the path of selfless action bring great benefit. Yet, of the two, know that the way of selfless action is superior.

Text:
ज्ञेयः स नित्यसन्न्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति ।
निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते ॥३॥
Transcription:
jñeyaḥ sa nitya-sannyāsī yo na dveṣṭi na kāṅkṣati
nirdvandvo hi mahā-bāho sukhaṁ bandhāt pramuchyate [3]
Translation: O mighty-armed Arjuna, know that one who is free from attraction and aversion is truly renounced, though performing action; for one who is free from duality easily attains liberation from bondage.

Text:
साङ्ख्य योगौ पृथग्बालाः प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिताः ।
एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम् ॥४॥
Transcription:
sāṅkhya-yogau pṛthag bālāḥ pravadanti na paṇḍitāḥ
ekam apy āsthitaḥ samyag ubhayor vindate phalam [4]
Translation: The path of renunciation (sāṅkhya-yoga) and the path of selfless action (karma-yoga) are considered separate paths by the unwise, but not by the learned. One who perfectly follows either of these paths will achieve the result of both.

Text:
यत्साङ्ख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते ।
एकं साङ्ख्यं  च योगञ्च यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥५॥
Transcription:
yat sāṅkhyaiḥ prāpyate sthānaṁ tad yogair api gamyate
ekaṁ sāṅkhyañ cha yogañ cha yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati [5]
Translation: The state achieved by the renunciation of action is also achieved by the performance of selfless action. One who sees these two paths to be one and the same, actually sees.

Text:
सन्न्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः ।
योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म न चिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥६॥
Transcription:
sannyāsas tu mahā-bāho duḥkham āptum ayogataḥ
yoga-yukto munir brahma na chireṇādhigachchhati [6]
Translation: O mighty hero, without selfless action, mere renunciation of action is the cause of sorrow. But the wise man who engages in selfless action swiftly attains to the Absolute.

Text:
योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः ।
सर्व्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्व्वन्नपि न लिप्यते ॥७॥
Transcription:
yoga-yukto viśuddhātmā vijitātmā jitendriyaḥ
sarva-bhūtātmabhūtātmā kurvann api na lipyate [7]
Translation: The clean-hearted person thus engaged in yoga controls his mind and senses. Becoming filled with affection and compassion for all beings, although fully active, he is never implicated by any action.

Text:
नैव किञ्चित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् ।
पश्यञ्शृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपञ्श्वसन् ॥८॥
प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्त्तन्त इति धारयन् ॥९॥
Transcription:
naiva kiñchit karomīti yukto manyeta tattva-vit
paśyañ śṛṇvan spṛśañ jighrann aśnan gachchhan svapañ śvasan [8]
pralapan visṛjan gṛhṇann unmiṣan nimiṣann api
indriyāṇīndriyārtheṣu vartanta iti dhārayan [9]
Translation: The realised karma-yogī performs the activities of seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, breathing, speaking, evacuating, grasping objects, blinking the eyes, and so on, and he understands, “My sense organs—eyes, ears, skin, nose, and tongue—function in relation to their respective objects of form, sound, touch, smell, and taste.” Thus, he considers, “I do not perform any action at all.”

Text:
ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्म्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः ।
लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा ॥१०॥
Transcription:
brahmaṇy ādhāya karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā karoti yaḥ
lipyate na sa pāpena padma-patram ivāmbhasā [10]
Translation: One who, though living in this world, selflessly offers all his actions to the Supreme Lord, is not polluted by sin, as a lotus leaf remains on the water yet is untouched by it.

Text:
कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि ।
योगिनः कर्म्म कुर्व्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्मशुद्धये ॥११॥
Transcription:
kāyena manasā buddhyā kevalair indriyair api
yoginaḥ karma kurvanti saṅgaṁ tyaktvātma-śuddhaye [11]
Translation: By giving up attachment, the karma-yogīs perform action merely through the body, mind, intelligence and even the senses, for self-purification.

Text:
युक्तः कर्म्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम् ।
अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥१२॥
Transcription:
yuktaḥ karma-phalaṁ tyaktvā śāntim āpnoti naiṣṭhikīm
ayuktaḥ kāma-kāreṇa phale sakto nibadhyate [12]
Translation: Giving up attachment to the fruits of his actions, the materially unmotivated (niṣkām) karma-yogī attains constant peace, or liberation from actions and reactions. But the fruitive worker (sakām karmī) is attached to the fruits of his actions, and he becomes implicated by his endeavours.

Text:
सर्व्वकर्म्माणि मनसा सन्न्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी ।
नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्व्वन्न कारयन् ॥१३॥
Transcription:
sarva-karmāṇi manasā sannyasyāste sukhaṁ vaśī
nava-dvāre pure dehī naiva kurvan na kārayan [13]
Translation: Mentally renouncing all actions, the sense-controlled embodied living being resides happily within the city of nine gates*, neither acting nor causing work for others.
Comment: * The body of nine gates — two eyes, two nostrils, two ears, mouth, anus, and genital.

Text:
न कर्त्तृत्वं न कर्म्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः ।
न कर्म्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्त्तते ॥१४॥
Transcription:
na kartṛtvaṁ na karmāṇi lokasya sṛjati prabhuḥ
na karma-phala-saṁyogaṁ svabhāvas tu pravartate [14]
Translation: The Lord does not generate anyone’s ego of considering themselves ‘the doer’, nor their actions, nor their association with the fruits of their actions. These are all a result of their external nature of ignorance since time immemorial.

Text:
नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः ।
अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः ॥१५॥
Transcription:
nādatte kasyachit pāpaṁ na chaiva sukṛtaṁ vibhuḥ
ajñānenāvṛtaṁ jñānaṁ tena muhyanti jantavaḥ [15]
Translation: The Supreme Lord accepts neither the sin nor the piety of anyone. The living beings fall into illusion because their knowledge is covered by ignorance.

Text:
ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः ।
तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥१६॥
Transcription:
jñānena tu tad ajñānaṁ yeṣāṁ nāśitam ātmanaḥ
teṣām ādityavaj jñānaṁ prakāśayati tat param [16]
Translation: For persons whose divine knowledge has awakened, their ignorance is destroyed, and their wisdom, like the shining sun, reveals the Supreme Reality.

Text:
तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणाः ।
गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषाः ॥१७॥
Transcription:
tad-buddhayas tad-ātmānas tan-niṣṭhās tat-parāyaṇāḥ
gachchhanty apunar-āvṛttiṁ jñāna-nirdhūta-kalmaṣāḥ [17]
Translation: Those whose illusion has been completely dispelled by knowledge always think of Me, the Supreme Lord; I am their meditation, and they abide in Me alone, hearing and singing My unending glories in pure devotion. Thus, they are liberated from the mundane.

Text:
विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि ।
शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः ॥१८॥
Transcription:
vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini
śuni chaiva śvapāke cha paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśinaḥ [18]
Translation: The enlightened souls see transcendence within all living beings, whether the humble and learned brāhmaṇ, the cow, the elephant, the dog, or the dog-eater. Therefore, they are to be known as paṇḍit—men of true wisdom.

Text:
इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः ।
निर्द्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद्ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः ॥१९॥
Transcription:
ihaiva tair jitaḥ sargo yeṣāṁ sāmye sthitaṁ manaḥ
nirdoṣaṁ hi samaṁ brahma tasmād brahmaṇi te sthitāḥ [19]
Translation: Those whose minds are equipoised in Brahma have conquered the cycle of birth and death while living within this world. By their perfect spiritual equanimity, they are always situated in transcendence.

Text:
न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम् ।
स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद्ब्रह्मणि स्थितः ॥२०॥
Transcription:
na prahṛṣyet priyaṁ prāpya nodvijet prāpya chāpriyam
sthira-buddhir asammūḍho brahma-vid brahmaṇi sthitaḥ [20]
Translation: Absorbed in transcendence, endowed with steady intelligence, and free from the delusion of thinking of the body and associated objects as ‘me’ and ‘mine,’ the knower of the Absolute is neither happy when pleasant things come his way nor sad when unpleasant things come his way.

Text:
बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् ।
स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते ॥२१॥
Transcription:
bāhya-sparśeṣv asaktātmā vindaty ātmani yat sukham
sa brahma-yoga-yuktātmā sukham akṣayam aśnute [21]
Translation: Detaching his mind from external pleasures, such a knower of the Absolute Truth tastes the inner joy of self-realisation. Then, absorbing himself in meditation on the Absolute, he experiences inexhaustible bliss.

Text:
ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते ।
आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः ॥२२॥
Transcription:
ye hi saṁsparśajā bhogā duḥkha-yonaya eva te
ādy-antavantaḥ kaunteya na teṣu ramate budhaḥ [22]
Translation: O son of Kuntī, the pleasures that arise from the contact of the senses with their objects are the cause of unhappiness, as they are transient. The wise never delight in such pleasures.

Text:
शक्नोतीहैव यः सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् ।
कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्तः स सुखी नरः ॥२३॥
Transcription:
śaknotīhaiva yaḥ soḍhuṁ prāk śarīra-vimokṣaṇāt
kāma-krodhodbhavaṁ vegaṁ sa yuktaḥ sa sukhī naraḥ [23]
Translation: Know that one who, before leaving the body, utilises the opportunity of this life to check the impulses of desire and anger—he is a yogī who knows true happiness.

Text:
योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः ।
स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्व्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ॥२४॥
Transcription:
yo ’ntaḥ-sukho ’ntar-ārāmas tathāntar-jyotir eva yaḥ
sa yogī brahma-nirvāṇaṁ brahma-bhūto ’dhigachchhati [24]
Translation: Such a yogī sees the self within and delights in the bliss of the self within. Attaining the transcendental state, he attains liberation from matter (and entry into the abode of Brahma).

Text:
लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्व्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः ।
छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्व्वभूतहिते रताः ॥२५॥
Transcription:
labhante brahma-nirvāṇam ṛṣayaḥ kṣīṇa-kalmaṣāḥ
chhinna-dvaidhā yatātmānaḥ sarva-bhūta-hite-ratāḥ [25]
Translation: Sinless, free from doubt, self-controlled, and dedicated to the welfare of all living beings, the seers of truth attain such liberation.

Text:
कामक्रोधविमुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् ।
अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्व्वाणं वर्त्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥२६॥
Transcription:
kāma-krodha-vimuktānāṁ yatīnāṁ yata-chetasām
abhito brahma-nirvāṇaṁ vartate viditātmanām [26]
Translation: Persons of the renounced order who have controlled their thoughts, who are free from desire and anger, and who have achieved knowledge of the nature of the soul, are liberated whether they live or die.

Text:
स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः ।
प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ ॥२७॥
यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायणः ।
विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो यः सदा मुक्त एव सः ॥२८॥
Transcription:
sparśān kṛtvā bahir bāhyāṁś chakṣuś chaivāntare bhruvoḥ
prāṇāpānau samau kṛtvā nāsābhyantara-chāriṇau [27]
yatendriya-mano-buddhir munir mokṣa-parāyaṇaḥ
vigatechchhā-bhaya-krodho yaḥ sadā mukta eva saḥ [28]
Translation: Expelling from the mind all the external sense objects of sound, touch, form, taste, and smell; centering the concentration and stabilising the incoming and outgoing breath; subjugating the senses, mind, and intelligence; dedicating himself to liberation; his desire, fear, and anger gone, such a sage is ever liberated.

Text:
भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्व्वलोकमहेश्वरम् ।
सुहृदं सर्व्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति ॥२९॥
Transcription:
bhoktāraṁ yajña-tapasāṁ sarva-loka-maheśvaram
suhṛdaṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ jñātvā māṁ śāntim ṛchchhati [29]
Translation: I am the enjoyer and objective of the sacrifices performed by persons of action, and of the austerities performed by persons of knowledge. I am Nārāyaṇ, the indwelling monitor of all planes of life, the worshippable Supreme Personality who awards liberation. I am Kṛṣṇa, the well-wisher of all and the dear friend of the devotees. One who knows Me thus, attains the bliss of knowing his own original divine identity.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
अनाश्रितः कर्म्मफलं कार्य्यं कर्म्म करोति यः ।
स सन्न्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रियः ॥१॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
anāśritaḥ karma-phalaṁ kāryaṁ karma karoti yaḥ
sa sannyāsī cha yogī cha na niragnir na chākriyaḥ [1]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: Know a genuine sannyāsī and yogī as one who, without expectation of the fruits of his actions, performs his duties as prescribed by the scriptures. One is not a sannyāsī merely by renouncing the performance of the fire sacrifice and other prescribed duties, and one is not a yogī merely by becoming physically inactive.

Text:
यं सन्न्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव ।
न ह्यसन्न्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन ॥२॥
Transcription:
yaṁ sannyāsam iti prāhur yogaṁ taṁ viddhi pāṇḍava
na hy asannyasta-saṅkalpo yogī bhavati kaśchana [2]
Translation: O Arjuna, that which is known as sannyās (complete renunciation) you should know to be yoga, since without renouncing fruitive desire no one can be a yogī.

Text:
आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म्म कारणमुच्यते ।
योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शमः कारणमुच्यते ॥३॥
Transcription:
ārurukṣor muner yogaṁ karma kāraṇam uchyate
yogārūḍhasya tasyaiva śamaḥ kāraṇam uchyate [3]
Translation: For the yoga practitioner desirous of attaining steady yoga meditation, action is said to be the means. When he has achieved yoga meditation, renunciation of all action is said to be the means of remaining in meditation.

Text:
यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्म्मस्वनुषज्जते ।
सर्व्वसङ्कल्पसन्न्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥४॥
Transcription:
yadā hi nendriyārtheṣu na karmasv anuṣajjate
sarva-saṅkalpa-sannyāsī yogārūḍhas tadochyate [4]
Translation: When the yoga practitioner does not become attached to the objects of the senses, and to any action in pursuit of their enjoyment, and renounces all thoughts of enjoyment, then he is said to have attained to yoga.

Text:
उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत् ।
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः ॥५॥
Transcription:
uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayet
ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ [5]
Translation: One must elevate himself by the mind, not degrade himself by the mind; for the mind is sometimes the friend of the living being, and in another situation that very mind is his enemy.

Text:
बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनैवात्मात्मना जितः ।
अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्त्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥६॥
Transcription:
bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenaivātmātmanā jitaḥ
anātmanas tu śatrutve vartetātmaiva śatruvat [6]
Translation: For one who has conquered the mind, his mind is his friend. For one unable to control the mind, his mind works against him like an enemy.

Text:
जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः ।
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः ॥७॥
Transcription:
jitātmanaḥ praśāntasya param ātmā samāhitaḥ
śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣu tathā mānāpamānayoḥ [7]
Translation: The yogī who has conquered the mind remains deeply absorbed in yogic trance, peaceful, and undisturbed by cold and heat, happiness and unhappiness, or honour and dishonour.

Text:
ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रियः ।
युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्ट्राश्मकाञ्चनः ॥८॥
Transcription:
jñāna-vijñāna-tṛptātmā kūṭastho vijitendriyaḥ
yukta ity uchyate yogī sama-loṣṭrāśma-kāñchanaḥ [8]
Translation: One who is always satisfied within by both knowledge and realisation, who is always fixed in his spiritual nature, who is sense-controlled, and who has the vision of equality for a clod of earth, a rock, or gold—it is said that such a person has attained yoga.

Text:
सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु ।
साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते ॥९॥
Transcription:
suhṛn-mitrāry-udāsīna-madhyastha-dveṣya-bandhuṣu
sādhuṣv api cha pāpeṣu sama-buddhir viśiṣyate [9]
Translation: Such an eminent yogī has the vision of equality towards a well-wisher, a friend, an enemy, an indifferent person, a mediator, a hateful person, a relative, a saint, and a sinner.

Text:
योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः  ।
एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः ॥१०॥
Transcription:
yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānaṁ rahasi sthitaḥ
ekākī yata-chittātmā nirāśīr aparigrahaḥ [10]
Translation: The yogī should constantly concentrate his mind by remaining alone in a solitary place, his mind and body controlled, free from desire and acquisition.

Text:
शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः ।
नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चेलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥११॥
तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः ।
उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये ॥१२॥
Transcription:
śuchau deśe pratiṣṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ
nāty-uchchhritaṁ nāti-nīchaṁ chelājina-kuśottaram [11]
tatraikāgraṁ manaḥ kṛtvā yata-chittendriya-kriyaḥ
upaviśyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātma-viśuddhaye [12]
Translation: In a clean place, not too high and not too low, the yogī should establish a firm seat of Kuśa grass, deerskin, and cloth. Then, sitting on that seat, subjugating all mental and sensual activity, with one-pointed concentration he should practise meditational trance in order to purify his heart.

Text:
समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः ।
सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् ॥१३॥
प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः ।
मनः संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्परः ॥१४॥
Transcription:
samaṁ kāya-śiro-grīvaṁ dhārayann achalaṁ sthiraḥ
samprekṣya nāsikāgraṁ svaṁ diśaś chānavalokayan [13]
praśāntātmā vigata-bhīr brahmachāri-vrate sthitaḥ
manaḥ saṁyamya mach-chitto yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ [14]
Translation: Soberly keeping his body, head, and neck straight and upright, poised, the yogī should fix his gaze at one point, as though between the eyes, without casting a glance in any direction. Tranquil, fearless, and reposed in the vow of celibacy, he should meditate on Me (as four-armed Viṣṇu), and devoting himself to Me, he should thus practise yoga.

Text:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानसः ।
शान्तिं निर्व्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति ॥१५॥
Transcription:
yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī niyata-mānasaḥ
śāntiṁ nirvāṇa-paramāṁ mat-saṁsthām adhigachchhati [15]
Translation: In this way, constantly absorbing his mind in meditation (dhyāna-yoga), the yogī who has ceased hankering for material enjoyment achieves the peace of liberation from mundanity, the peace which abides in Me (in my Brahma effulgence).

Text:
नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नतः ।
न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्ज्जुन ॥१६॥
Transcription:
nāty-aśnatas tu yogo ’sti na chaikāntam anaśnataḥ
na chāti-svapna-śīlasya jāgrato naiva chārjuna [16]
Translation: O Arjuna, yoga cannot be practised by one who overeats, undereats, oversleeps, or undersleeps.

Text:
युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्म्मसु ।
युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दुःखहा ॥१७॥
Transcription:
yuktāhāra-vihārasya yukta-cheṣṭasya karmasu
yukta-svapnāvabodhasya yogo bhavati duḥkha-hā [17]
Translation: For a person who eats, relaxes, and works in a regulated way, and who keeps regular hours in proper measure, his practice of yoga dispells all his suffering.

Text:
यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते ।
निःस्पृहः सर्व्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥१८॥
Transcription:
yadā viniyataṁ chittam ātmany evāvatiṣṭhate
niḥspṛhaḥ sarva-kāmebhyo yukta ity ucyate tadā [18]
Translation: When a yogī withdraws the mind from mundane thoughts and centres it unwaveringly in the self, then, free from mundane desires, he is said to be actually situated in yoga, or linked with the Absolute.

Text:
यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता ।
योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः ॥१९॥
Transcription:
yathā dīpo nivāta-stho neṅgate sopamā smṛtā
yogino yata-chittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ [19]
Translation: It is said, as a lamp-flame situated in a windless place does not waver, similarly, the mind of the yogī absorbed in the self never flickers in its concentration.

Text:
यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया ।
यत्र चैवात्मनात्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति ॥२०॥
Transcription:
yatroparamate chittaṁ niruddhaṁ yoga-sevayā
yatra chaivātmanātmānaṁ paśyann ātmani tuṣyati
Translation: The mind controlled by yoga practice is tranquil, pacified by detachment from mundane association. The yogī remains satisfied in the Lord alone, directly seeing the Supersoul through his purified consciousness.

Text:
सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्व्वानशेषतः ।
मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्ततः ॥२४॥
Transcription:
saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmāṁs tyaktvā sarvān aśeṣataḥ
manasaivendriya-grāmaṁ viniyamya samantataḥ [24]
Translation: To practice yoga, one must abandon all thoughts that give rise to desires, and using the mind, withdraw the senses from all material objects.

Text:
शनैः शनैरुपरमेद्बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया ।
आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥२५॥
Transcription:
śanaiḥ śanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛti-gṛhītayā
ātma-saṁsthaṁ manaḥ kṛtvā na kiñchid api chintayet [25]
Translation: By steadying the intelligence, one should gradually still the mind and focus it within the self alone.

Text:
यतो यतो निश्चलति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् ।
ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥२६॥
Transcription:
yato yato niśchalati manaś chañchalam asthiram
tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmany eva vaśaṁ nayet [26]
Translation: The wavering and unsteady mind should be brought back from whatever mundane objects it pursues, and be held within the self alone.

Text:
प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम् ।
उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम् ॥२७॥
Transcription:
praśānta-manasaṁ hy enaṁ yoginaṁ sukham uttamam
upaiti śānta-rajasaṁ brahma-bhūtam akalmaṣam [27]
Translation: The transcendental joy of self-realisation comes to the yogī whose passions are quieted, who is peacefulhearted, free from the defects of attachment, fear, and anger, and always situated in the spiritual nature.

Text:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः ।
सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते ॥२८॥
Transcription:
yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī vigata-kalmaṣaḥ
sukhena brahma-saṁsparśam atyantaṁ sukham aśnute [28]
Translation: Thus absorbed in self-realisation, the clean-hearted yogī easily attains the boundless ecstasy of contacting the divine. He realises the Supersoul. (Such yoga is of a devotional nature).

Text:
सर्व्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्व्वभूतानि चात्मनि ।
ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्व्वत्र समदर्शनः ॥२९॥
Transcription:
sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānaṁ sarva-bhūtāni chātmani
īkṣate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśanaḥ [29]
Translation: His consciousness united with the Infinite, the master of yoga sees the universal spirituality. He sees the Supreme Soul within all beings, and all beings within the Supreme.

Text:
यो मां पश्यति सर्व्वत्र सर्व्वञ्च मयि पश्यति ।
तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति ॥३०॥
Transcription:
yo māṁ paśyati sarvatra sarvañ cha mayi paśyati
tasyāhaṁ na praṇaśyāmi sa cha me na praṇaśyati [30]
Translation: For one who sees Me in everything and everything in Me, I am never lost to him, and he is never lost to Me.

Text:
सर्व्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थितः ।
सर्व्वथा वर्त्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्त्तते ॥३१॥
Transcription:
sarva-bhūta-sthitaṁ yo māṁ bhajaty ekatvam āsthitaḥ
sarvathā vartamāno ’pi sa yogī mayi vartate [31]
Translation: The yogī who takes shelter of Me and adores Me in My non-different form as the Supersoul situated within the heart of every living being, abides in Me in all circumstances.

Text:
आत्मौपम्येन सर्व्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्ज्जुन ।
सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं स योगी परमो मतः ॥३२॥
Transcription:
ātmaupamyena sarvatra samaṁ paśyati yo ’rjuna
sukhaṁ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṁ sa yogī paramo mataḥ [32]
Translation: I consider the best of all yogīs to be the one who knows the happiness and unhappiness of all beings as his own.

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन ।
एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥३३॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
yo ’yaṁ yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdana
etasyāhaṁ na paśyāmi chañchalatvāt sthitiṁ sthirām [33]
Translation: Arjuna said: O Madhusūdan, because of the fickle nature of the mind, I cannot conceive how one can maintain the equilibrium in yoga that You have described.

Text:
चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम् ।
तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥३४॥
Transcription:
chañchalaṁ hi manaḥ kṛṣṇa pramāthi balavad dṛḍham
tasyāhaṁ nigrahaṁ manye vāyor iva suduṣkaram [34]
Translation: O Kṛṣṇa, the mind is fickle, turbulent, obstinate and very powerful. I consider controlling it to be more difficult than controlling the wind.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम् ।
अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते ॥३५॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
asaṁśayaṁ mahā-bāho mano durnigrahaṁ chalam
abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa cha gṛhyate [35]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: O heroic Arjuna, undoubtedly the mind is fickle and extremely difficult to control. However, O son of Kuntī, it is possible by practice and detachment.

Text:
असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः ।
वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः ॥३६॥
Transcription:
asaṁyatātmanā yogo duṣprāpa iti me matiḥ
vaśyātmanā tu yatatā śakyo ’vāptum upāyataḥ [36]
Translation: I consider that yoga is difficult to attain for the person of unrestrained mind. But one who strives to restrain the mind by the appropriate practice can succeed in yoga.

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
अयतिः श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानसः ।
अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति ॥३७॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
ayatiḥ śraddhayopeto yogāch chalita-mānasaḥ
aprāpya yoga-saṁsiddhiṁ kāṁ gatiṁ kṛṣṇa gachchhati [37]
Translation: Arjuna said: O Kṛṣṇa, what is the destination of a person who, due to his faith in the scriptures, engages in yoga practice, but lacking perseverence, proper practice, and abnegation, is distracted by mundane inclinations and fails to succeed in yoga practice?

Text:
कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति ।
अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मणः पथि ॥३८॥
Transcription:
kachchin nobhaya-vibhraṣṭaś chhinnābhram iva naśyati
apratiṣṭho mahā-bāho vimūḍho brahmaṇaḥ pathi [38]
Translation: O mighty hero, O Kṛṣṇa, having deviated from the yoga of attaining the Absolute, shelterless and fallen from the paths of both action and meditation, is not such a person utterly lost, like a scattered cloud?

Text:
एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः ।
त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते ॥३९॥
Transcription:
etan me saṁśayaṁ kṛṣṇa chhettum arhasy aśeṣataḥ
tvad-anyaḥ saṁśayasyāsya chhettā na hy upapadyate [39]
Translation: O Kṛṣṇa! No one but You can dispel this doubt of mine. Please mercifully slash it at the root.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते ।
न हि कल्याणकृत्कश्चिद्दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति ॥४०॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
pārtha naiveha nāmutra vināśas tasya vidyate
na hi kalyāṇa-kṛt kaśchid durgatiṁ tāta gachchhati [40]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: O Arjuna, son of Kuntī, there is no loss for the unsuccessful yoga practitioner as he is not denied the happiness of the heavenly plane in this world, nor is he denied the chance of the fortune to see the Supersoul in the transcendental realm. This is so, O dear one, because a person who performs virtuous actions never suffers an ill fate.

Text:
प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः ।
शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते ॥४१॥
Transcription:
prāpya puṇya-kṛtāṁ lokān uṣitvā śāśvatīḥ samāḥ
śuchīnāṁ śrīmatāṁ gehe yoga-bhraṣṭo ’bhijāyate [41]
Translation: After residing for many years in all those heavenly planes that are attainable by the performers of great sacrifices such as the Aśvamedha, the unsuccessful yoga practitioner takes birth in the home of honourable, prosperous persons given to pure and honest practices.

Text:
अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् ।
एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् ॥४२॥
Transcription:
athavā yoginām eva kule bhavati dhīmatām
etaddhi durlabhataraṁ loke janma yad īdṛśam [42]
Translation: Or he takes birth in the home or family of teachers of yoga, who are dedicated to yoga practice. Such a birth is more rarely attained in this world.

Text:
तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्व्वदैहिकम् ।
यतते च ततो भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन ॥४३॥
Transcription:
tatra taṁ buddhi-saṁyogaṁ labhate paurva-daihikam
yatate cha tato bhūyaḥ saṁsiddhau kuru-nandana [43]
Translation: O son of Kuru, within one of these situations, the wisdom he had attained in his previous life is revived, and he again strives for success.

Text:
पूर्व्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि सः ।
जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्त्तते ॥४४॥
Transcription:
pūrvābhyāsena tenaiva hriyate hy avaśo ’pi saḥ
jijñāsur api yogasya śabda-brahmātivartate [44]
Translation: Despite himself, he is carried by the yoga practice of his previous life. In his search, he surpasses the fruitive rituals of the Vedas.

Text:
प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः ।
अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥४५॥
Transcription:
prayatnād yatamānas tu yogī saṁśuddha-kilbiṣaḥ
aneka-janma-saṁsiddhas tato yāti parāṁ gatim [45]
Translation: Striving harder than before, the yogī’s consciousness is cleansed of all worldly attachment, and he achieves the fruit of many lifetimes of yoga practice—he attains the supreme destination.

Text:
तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः ।
कर्म्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्ज्जुन ॥४६॥
Transcription:
tapasvibhyo ’dhiko yogī jñānibhyo ’pi mato ’dhikaḥ
karmibhyaś chādhiko yogī tasmād yogī bhavārjuna [46]
Translation: The yogī is superior to persons engaged in severe austerities (such as the Chāndrāyaṇ vow), superior to the person of knowledge who worships Brahma, and superior to the person of action. Know this to be My conclusion. Therefore, O Arjuna, be a yogī.

Text:
योगिनामपि सर्व्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना ।
श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः ॥४७॥
Transcription:
yoginām api sarveṣāṁ mad-gatenāntar-ātmanā
śraddhāvān bhajate yo māṁ sa me yuktatamo mataḥ [47]
Translation: The best of all yogīs is the devotee who has full faith in the authoritative pure devotional scriptures, and who adores Me with all his heart, hearing and singing My divine glories, rendering all services unto Me. This is My opinion.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः ।
असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु ॥१॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
mayy āsakta-manāḥ pārtha yogaṁ yuñjan mad-āśrayaḥ
asaṁśayaṁ samagraṁ māṁ yathā jñāsyasi tach chhṛṇu [1]
Translation: The Lord said: O Pārtha, hear from Me how, with mind attached to Me, the Supreme Lord, linking your consciousness with Me and taking refuge in Me alone, you will without doubt be able to know Me, My holy abode, My opulences, and My associates.

Text:
ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः ।
यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते ॥२॥
Transcription:
jñānaṁ te ’haṁ sa-vijñānam idaṁ vakṣyāmy aśeṣataḥ
yaj jñātvā neha bhūyo ’nyaj jñātavyam avaśiṣyate [2]
Translation: Now I shall reveal to you this knowledge of My absolute power as well as the realisation of My sweetness, knowing which, nothing will remain to be known, here on this supreme path.

Text:
मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये ।
यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ॥३॥
Transcription:
manuṣyāṇāṁ sahasreṣu kaśchid yatati siddhaye
yatatām api siddhānāṁ kaśchin māṁ vetti tattvataḥ [3]
Translation: Of countless souls, few reach the human form; of thousands of human beings, very few may try to realise the soul and the Supersoul; and of thousands who have attained such realisation, hardly one can actually know Me, Śyāmasundar.

Text:
भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च ।
अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा ॥४॥
Transcription:
bhūmir āpo ’nalo vāyuḥ khaṁ mano buddhir eva cha
ahaṅkāra itīyaṁ me bhinnā prakṛtir aṣṭadhā [4]
Translation: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intelligence, and ego are the eight divisions of My illusory potency in this world.
Comment:
In this verse, it is expressed that jñān, or knowledge, in the true sense of the word and in accordance with the precepts of devotion, is actually Bhagavad-aiśvarya-jñān, or knowledge of the supremacy of the Lord. The general seekers of knowledge may consider realisation of the soul—as distinct from the body and material elements—to be enlightenment, but this does not constitute ultimate knowledge.

Thus, to explain the conception of His Lordship, the Lord reveals His forms, His potencies, and their characteristics: “My different aspects are Brahma, Paramātmā, and Bhagavān. Brahma is a non-differentiated, formless aspect of My potency. Paramātmā, the Supersoul, is also an appearance of My potency (in relation to the cosmic manifestation), and this aspect of Mine is not eternally manifest. Only My form of Bhagavān, the Supreme Lord, is My eternal Self, and in this form My three eternal potencies exist as antaraṅgā- or chit-śhakti—the internal, divine potency; bahiraṅgā- or māyā-śakti—the external, illusory potency; and taṭasthā- or jīva-śakti—the marginal potency comprising the innumerable living beings.”

In this verse, the Lord has described His external, illusory potency.

— Śrīla Bhakti Vinod Ṭhākur

Text:
अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं  विद्धि मे पराम् ।
जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्य्यते जगत् ॥५॥
Transcription:
apareyam itas tv anyāṁ prakṛtiṁ viddhi me parām
jīva-bhūtāṁ mahā-bāho yayedaṁ dhāryate jagat [5]
Translation: O mighty hero, Arjuna, this external, worldly nature is inferior. But know that superior to this nature is My marginal potency, comprised of the individual souls. Worldly existence is adopted by the souls for enjoyment through fruitive actions. (The divine world emanates from My internal potency and the mundane world from My external potency. The potency of the living beings is known as marginal as they are constitutionally situated midway between these planes. They may choose to reside either in the mundane plane or the divine.)

Text:
एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्व्वाणीत्युपधारय ।
अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा ॥६॥
Transcription:
etad yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇīty upadhāraya
ahaṁ kṛtsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā [6]
Translation: Know that all species, either moving or stationary, are manifest from these two natures as the field of action and the knower of the field; and I alone am the cause of the manifestation and dissolution of the entire universe.

Text:
मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय ।
मयि सर्व्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥७॥
Transcription:
mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat kiñchid asti dhanañjaya
mayi sarvam idaṁ protaṁ sūtre maṇi-gaṇā iva [7]
Translation: O Arjuna, there is nothing superior to Me. All existence depends on Me, like gems strung on a thread.

Text:
रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्य्ययोः ।
प्रणवः सर्व्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु ॥८॥
Transcription:
raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śaśi-sūryayoḥ
praṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣu śabdaḥ khe pauruṣaṁ nṛṣu [8]
Translation: O son of Kuntī, I am the taste of water, I am the radiance of the sun and the moon. I am Om, the fundamental vibration in all the Vedas, I am the sound in the ether, and I am the masculinity in men.

Text:
पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्याञ्च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ ।
जीवनं सर्व्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ॥९॥
Transcription:
puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛthivyāñ cha tejaś chāsmi vibhāvasau
jīvanaṁ sarva-bhūteṣu tapaś chāsmi tapasviṣu [9]
Translation: I am the pure fragrance of the earth, and I am the radiance of fire. I am the life-span of all beings, and in ascetics, I am the power to endure duality such as cold and heat.

Text:
बीजं मां सर्व्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् ।
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥१०॥
Transcription:
bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanam
buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham [10]
Translation: O Pārtha, know Me as the eternal, original cause of all life-forms. I am the intelligence of the intelligent, the power of the powerful.

Text:
बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्ज्जितम् ।
धर्म्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ ॥११॥
Transcription:
balaṁ balavatāṁ chāhaṁ kāma-rāga-vivarjitam
dharmāviruddho bhūteṣu kāmo ’smi bharatarṣabha [11]
Translation: O Arjuna, I am the strength of the strong, without selfishness and attachment; and I am the desire for procreation in the living beings which does not contravene the universal principles of religion.

Text:
ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये ।
मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि ॥१२॥
Transcription:
ye chaiva sāttvikā bhāvā rājasās tāmasāś cha ye
matta eveti tān viddhi na tv ahaṁ teṣu te mayi [12]
Translation: Furthermore, know that all things, of the nature of goodness, passion, and ignorance, are manifest from Me alone. Nevertheless, I am not in them. They, subordinate to Me, are in Me.

Text:
त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्व्वमिदं जगत् ।
मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम् ॥१३॥
Transcription:
tribhir guṇamayair bhāvair ebhiḥ sarvam idaṁ jagat
mohitaṁ nābhijānāti mām ebhyaḥ param avyayam [13]
Translation: The entire world of living beings is deluded by the three modes of material nature. Therefore, no one can know Me, the Supreme Lord, who am transcendental to these modes and immutable.

Text:
दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया ।
मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते ॥१४॥
Transcription:
daivī hy eṣā guṇamayī mama māyā duratyayā
mām eva ye prapadyante māyām etāṁ taranti te [14]
Translation: My alluring, ‘trimodal’, illusory potency is practically insurmountable. However, those who take shelter in Me can overcome this powerful obstacle.

Text:
न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाःप्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः ।
माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः ॥१५॥
Transcription:
na māṁ duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥ
māyayāpahṛta-jñānā āsuraṁ-bhāvam-āśritāḥ [15]
Translation: These four kinds of wrongdoers do not surrender unto Me: fruitive workers, comparable to animals; fallen persons who take the highest path, devotion, but later reject it, considering it inadequate or worthless; those whose knowledge covered by illusion, Māyā (despite their scriptural study, they consider that only the form of Lord Nārāyaṇ is worshippable, and they think that Lord Kṛṣṇa, Lord Rāma and other factual forms are merely mortal); and the demoniac (impersonalists who dismember My form by their arrows of false logic, resembling demons like Jarāsandha).

Text:
चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्ज्जुन ।
आर्त्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ ॥१६॥
Transcription:
chatur-vidhā bhajante māṁ janāḥ sukṛtino ’rjuna
ārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī cha bharatarṣbha [16]
Translation: O Arjuna, best of the Bhāratas, four kinds of persons have the fortune to worship Me: the afflicted, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of happiness, and the self-realised.

Text:
तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिर्विशिष्यते ।
प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः ॥१७॥
Transcription:
teṣāṁ jñānī nitya-yukta eka-bhaktir viśiṣyate
priyo hi jñānino ’ty-artham ahaṁ sa cha mama priyaḥ [17]
Translation: Of these four types of devotees, best is the self-realised person who is My pure devotee, and whose consciousnesss is fully absorbed in Me. For I, Śyāmasundar, am very dear to him, and he is dear to Me.

Text:
उदाराः सर्व्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् ।
आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम् ॥१८॥
Transcription:
udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tv ātmaiva me matam
āsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā mām evānuttamāṁ gatim [18]
Translation: Certainly they are all noble souls, dear to Me, yet the spiritually self-realised soul is as beloved to Me as My own self, for he has given his heart to Me as his only shelter and ultimate goal of life.

Text:
बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते ।
वासुदेवः सर्व्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः ॥१९॥
Transcription:
bahūnāṁ janmanām ante jñānavān māṁ prapadyate
vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā sudurlabhaḥ [19]
Translation: After many births, the enlightened soul (blessed with devotional association) realises that everything is of the nature of (subordinate to) Vāsudev, and thus he surrenders unto Me. Such a great soul is very rare.

Text:
कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः ।
तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया ॥२०॥
Transcription:
kāmais tais tair hṛta-jñānāḥ prapadyante ’nya-devatāḥ
taṁ taṁ niyamam āsthāya prakṛtyā niyatāḥ svayā [20]
Translation: Persons misled by worldly desires, being governed by their own natures, worship other godly personalities like the sun-god and the many demigods, adopting the corresponding rituals to attain their objectives.

Text:
यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्च्चितुमिच्छति ।
तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् ॥२१॥
Transcription:
yo yo yāṁ yāṁ tanuṁ bhaktaḥ śraddhayārchitum ichchhati
tasya tasyāchalāṁ śraddhāṁ tām eva vidadhāmy aham [21]
Translation: According to a person’s desire, as his faith grows to worship a particular demigod, I, as the Supersoul in his heart, make his faith strong in that demigod (representing a form of Me).

Text:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते ।
लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान् ॥२२॥
Transcription:
sa tayā śraddhayā yuktas tasyārādhanam īhate
labhate cha tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān [22]
Translation: His faith nourished by Me, such a devotee continues worshipping the deity of that demigod and attains all his desired objects from that deity. Again, this happens by My sanction alone, since I am the Supersoul, situated within the heart of the demigod, too.

Text:
अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम् ।
देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि ॥२३॥
Transcription:
antavat tu phalaṁ teṣāṁ tad bhavaty alpa-medhasām
devān deva-yajo yānti mad-bhaktā yānti mām api [23]
Translation: But the fruit obtained by those unwise worshippers of the demigods is temporary. They reach the gods they worship, but My devotees come to Me.

Text:
अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः ।
परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम् ॥२४॥
Transcription:
avyaktaṁ vyaktim āpannaṁ manyante mām abuddhayaḥ
paraṁ bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam [24]
Translation: My eternal superexcellent nature—My form, qualities, activities, and Pastimes—are all transcendental, but unwise persons cannot know the truth, and they think, “The formless Brahma took birth in Vasudev’s cell as an ordinary human being.”

Text:
नाहं प्रकाशः सर्व्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः ।
मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् ॥२५॥
Transcription:
nāhaṁ prakāśaḥ sarvasya yoga-māyā-samāvṛtaḥ
mūḍho ’yaṁ nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam [25]
Translation: By My sweet will, remaining concealed by My own potency, I am not manifest to anyone and everyone. So the foolish cannot know Me, the son of Vasudev, unborn and ever-existent in My divine Śyāmasundar form of beautiful feature like a blackish rain-cloud.

Text:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्त्तमानानि चार्ज्जुन ।
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ॥२६॥
Transcription:
vedāhaṁ samatītāni vartamānāni chārjuna
bhaviṣyāṇi cha bhūtāni māṁ tu veda na kaśchana [26]
Translation: O Arjuna, I know all living beings of the past, present and future, but no one can know Me in truth.

Text:
इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत ।
सर्व्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप ॥२७॥
Transcription:
ichchhā-dveṣa-samutthena dvandva-mohena bhārata
sarva-bhūtāni sammohaṁ sarge yānti parantapa [27]
Translation: O Arjuna, chastiser of the enemy, at the manifestation of the universe, all beings become deluded by duality based on likes and dislikes.

Text:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्म्मणाम् ।
ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्म्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः ॥२८॥
Transcription:
yeṣāṁ tv anta-gataṁ pāpaṁ janānāṁ puṇya-karmaṇām
te dvandva-moha-nirmuktā bhajante māṁ dṛḍha-vratāḥ [28]
Translation: But the performers of virtuous deeds are purified of all sin (by the fortune of association with My pure devotee). Thus freed from the illusion of duality, they worship Me with firm resolve.

Text:
जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये ।
ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म्म चाखिलम् ॥२९॥
Transcription:
jarā-maraṇa-mokṣāya mām āśritya yatanti ye
te brahma tad viduḥ kṛtsnam adhyātmaṁ karma chākhilam [29]
Translation: Those who strive for freedom from the world of disease and death, placing their trust and devotion in Me—they know Brahma, they know the soul, and they know karma, the action that binds the soul again and again to this mundane plane of suffering.

Text:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञञ्च ये विदुः ।
प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः ॥३०॥
Transcription:
sādhibhūtādhidaivaṁ māṁ sādhiyajñañ cha ye viduḥ
prayāṇa-kāle ’pi cha māṁ te vidur yukta-chetasaḥ [30]
Translation: And those who know Me in relation to the worldly plane, the celestial plane, and the plane of sacrifice—such persons, their consciousness absorbed in Me, know Me even at the time of death. (Afflicted in the face of fearful death, they do not forget Me.)

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
किं तद्ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म्म पुरुषोत्तम ।
अधिभूतञ्च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते ॥१॥
अधियज्ञः कथं कोऽत्र देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन ।
प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभिः ॥२॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
kiṁ tad-brahma kim adhyātmaṁ kiṁ karma puruṣottama
adhibhūtañ cha kiṁ proktam adhidaivaṁ kim uchyate [1]
adhiyajñaḥ kathaṁ ko ’tra dehe ’smin madhusūdana
prayāṇa-kāle cha kathaṁ jñeyo ’si niyatātmabhiḥ [2]
Translation: Arjuna inquired: O Supreme Person, what is Brahma, and what is the soul? What is action, and what is the worldly plane? What is the celestial plane? O Madhusūdan: within the body, who is the agent of sacrifice, and how is He present? And how are You to be known by the self-controlled at the time of death?

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
अक्षरं परमं ब्रह्म स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते ।
भूतभावोद्भवकरो विसर्गः कर्म्मसंज्ञितः ॥३॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
akṣaraṁ paramaṁ brahma svabhāvo ’dhyātmam uchyate
bhūta-bhāvodbhava-karo visargaḥ karma-saṁjñitaḥ [3]
Translation: The Supreme Lord spoke thus: It is said that Brahma is the imperishable Absolute, and the soul is the living being’s own pure spiritual nature. By action is meant the creative force (or offerings by charity, sacrifice, and austerity, etc., to a demigod), manifesting, through the gross and subtle elements, the bodies of the human and other species of life.

Text:
अधिभूतं क्षरो भावः पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम् ।
अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र देहे देहभृतां वर ॥४॥
Transcription:
adhibhūtaṁ kṣaro bhāvaḥ puruṣaś chādhidaivatam
adhiyajño ’ham evātra dehe deha-bhṛtāṁ vara [4]
Translation: O Arjuna, best of beings, the worldly plane means that which is perishable (such as the body). The celestial plane is the collective universal form which encompasses and governs the demigods; and I am the Lord of sacrifice, present as the Supersoul within the bodies of all living beings, as the sanctioner and rewarder of their actions (of sacrifices, etc.).

Text:
अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम् ।
यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥५॥
Transcription:
anta-kāle cha mām eva smaran muktvā kalevaram
yaḥ prayāti sa mad-bhāvaṁ yāti nāsty atra saṁśayaḥ [5]
Translation: One who thinks of Me at the time of death attains to My nature on departing this world. Of this there is no doubt.

Text:
यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम् ।
तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः ॥६॥
Transcription:
yaṁ yaṁ vāpi smaran bhāvaṁ tyajaty ante kalevaram
taṁ tam evaiti kaunteya sadā tad-bhāva-bhāvitaḥ [6]
Translation: O son of Kuntī, one who has always thought of a particular object will remember it and attain to it after leaving the body.

Text:
तस्मात्सर्व्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च ।
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयः ॥७॥
Transcription:
tasmāt sarveṣu kāleṣu mām anusmara yudhya cha
mayy arpita-mano-buddhir mām evaiṣyasy asaṁśayaḥ [7]
Translation: Therefore always remembering Me, fight, as it is your natural duty. Thus dedicating your mind and intelligence to Me, you will reach Me. Of this there is no doubt.

Text:
अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन चेतसा नान्यगामिना ।
परमं पुरुषं दिव्यं याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन् ॥८॥
Transcription:
abhyāsa-yoga-yuktena chetasā nānya-gāminā
paramaṁ puruṣaṁ divyaṁ yāti pārthānuchintayan [8]
Translation: O Pārtha, the yogī who practises meditation with an undeviating mind, always thinking of the effulgent Supreme Person, reaches Him.

Text:
कविं पुराणमनुशासितारम्
अणोरणीयांसमनुस्मरेद्यः ।
सर्व्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूपम्
आदित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात् ॥९।
Transcription:
kaviṁ purāṇam anuśāsitāram
aṇor aṇīyāṁsam anusmared yaḥ
sarvasya dhātāram achintya-rūpam
āditya-varṇaṁ tamasaḥ parastāt
Translation: He, the Supreme Person, is the all-knowing, beginingless, merciful teacher of devotion unto Himself. Although more subtle that the atom, He is the infinite support of all existence. His form is supramundane, that is, of a humanlike feature, yet He is self-illuminating and all-illuminating like the sun. He is transcendental to illusion.

Text:
यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्ति
विशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागाः ।
यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्य्यं चरन्ति
तत्ते पदं सङ्ग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये ॥११॥
Transcription:
yad akṣaraṁ veda-vido vadanti
viśanti yad yatayo vīta-rāgāḥ
yad ichchhanto brahmacharyaṁ charanti
tat te padaṁ saṅgraheṇa pravakṣye [11]
Translation: Along with the method of attaining it, I shall describe to you that objective which the learned knowers of the Vedas call Om, the Imperishable, within which the desireless ascetics enter, and which the devout seekers aspire to reach by practising the vow of celibacy.

Text:
सर्व्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च ।
मूर्ध्न्यधायात्मनः प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम् [१२]
Transcription:
sarva-dvārāṇi saṁyamya mano hṛdi nirudhya cha
mūrdhny ādhāyātmanaḥ prāṇam āsthito yoga-dhāraṇām
Translation: Controlling all the gateways of the senses, holding the mind steady within the heart, establishing the life-force between the eyebrows, and fully absorbing the thoughts in the spiritual self...

Text:
अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः ।
तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः ॥१४॥
Transcription:
ananya-chetāḥ satataṁ yo māṁ smarati nityaśaḥ
tasyāhaṁ sulabhaḥ pārtha nitya-yuktasya yoginaḥ [14]
Translation: O Pārtha, a person who one-pointedly remembers Me always, in all circumstances, and who, in his relationship with Me (in servitude, friendship, etc.) constantly aspires for My divine association—I am easily reached by such a devotee.

Text:
मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दुःखालयमशाश्वतम् ।
नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः संसिद्धिं परमां गताः ॥१५॥
Transcription:
mām upetya punar janma duḥkhālayam aśāśvatam
nāpnuvanti mahātmānaḥ saṁsiddhiṁ paramāṁ gatāḥ [15]
Translation: The great souls who become My associates in My divine Pastimes, having reached Me, are never again born into transitory existence, which is the abode of suffering.

Text:
आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्त्तिनोऽर्ज्जुन ।
मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते ॥१६॥
Transcription:
ābrahma-bhuvanāl lokāḥ punar āvartino ’rjuna
mām upetya tu kaunteya punar janma na vidyate [16]
Translation: O Arjuna, from the plane of Lord Brahmā downwards, all planes or their residents are subject to return. But, O Kaunteya, there is no rebirth after reaching Me.

Text:
सहस्रयुगपर्य्यन्तमहर्यद्ब्रह्मणो विदुः ।
रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः ॥१७॥
Transcription:
sahasra-yuga-paryantam ahar yad brahmaṇo viduḥ
rātriṁ yuga-sahasrāntāṁ te ’ho-rātra-vido janāḥ [17]
Translation: A day of Lord Brahmā lasts for one thousand chatur-yugas*, and his night is of the same duration. Persons who know this have the true conception of day and night.
Comment: * One yuga (age) in the time calculation of the demigods equals the four yugas or one chatur-yuga in the time calculation of man, or 4,320,000 years.

Text:
अव्यक्ताद्व्यक्तयः सर्व्वाः प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे ।
रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसंज्ञके ॥१८॥
Transcription:
avyaktād vyaktayaḥ sarvāḥ prabhavanty ahar-āgame
rātry-āgame pralīyante tatraivāvyakta-saṁjñake [18]
Translation: With the coming of day, the universal progeny with their bodies, senses, enjoyable objects, and abodes are born of Lord Brahmā in his waking state; and at nightfall, they are again absorbed into Lord Brahmā, who is known as ‘the unmanifest.’

Text:
भूतग्रामः स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते ।
रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे ॥१९।
Transcription:
bhūta-grāmaḥ sa evāyaṁ bhūtvā bhūtvā pralīyate
rātry-āgame ’vaśaḥ pārtha prabhavaty ahar-āgame [19]
Translation: O Pārtha, the multitude of beings are helplessly (subject to the law of karma) born again and again. With the coming of Lord Brahmā’s night they are absorbed, and with the coming of his day they are again born.

Text:
परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्योऽव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातनः ।
यः स सर्व्वेषु भूतेषु नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति ॥२०॥
Transcription:
paras tasmāt tu bhāvo ’nyo ’vyakto ’vyaktāt sanātanaḥ
yaḥ sa sarveṣu bhūteṣu naśyatsu na vinaśyati [20]
Translation: But superior to this unmanifest (Lord Brahmā) there is another unmanifest, and that existence is beginningless and transcendental to the perception of the living beings. He is not destroyed when all beings are destroyed.

Text:
अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहुः परमां गतिम् ।
यं प्राप्य न निवर्त्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥२१॥
Transcription:
avyakto ’kṣara ity uktas tam āhuḥ paramāṁ gatim
yaṁ prāpya na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṁ mama [21]
Translation: He is said to be the Unmanifest and the Invincible, proclaimed (by the Upaniṣads) to be the supreme goal, reaching whom one does not return to this world. Know that destination to be My supreme abode.

Text:
पुरुषः स परः पार्थ भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया ।
यस्यान्तःस्थानि भूतानि येन सर्व्वमिदं ततम् ॥२२॥
Transcription:
puruṣaḥ sa paraḥ pārtha bhaktyā labhyas tv ananyayā
yasyāntaḥsthāni bhūtāni yena sarvam idaṁ tatam [22]
Translation: O Pārtha, I, the Supreme Person, within whom all beings are situated and by whom this whole universe is pervaded, am attainable only by exclusive devotion.

Text:
यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिञ्चैव योगिनः ।
प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ ॥२३॥
Transcription:
yatra kāle tv anāvṛttim āvṛttiñ chaiva yoginaḥ
prayātā yānti taṁ kālaṁ vakṣyāmi bharatarṣabha [23]
Translation: O Bharatarṣabha, now I shall describe to you the circumstances in which the yogīs attain either liberation or rebirth at the moment of death.

Text:
अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम् ।
तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः ॥२४॥
Transcription:
agnir jyotir ahaḥ śuklaḥ ṣaṇ-māsā uttarāyaṇam
tatra prayātā gachchhanti brahma brahma-vido janāḥ [24]
Translation: Upon leaving the body, the knowers of Brahma attain Brahma via the path of fire, light, day, the waxing moon, and the sun’s northern ascent.

Text:
धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम्  ।
तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्त्तते ॥२५॥
Transcription:
dhūmo rātris tathā kṛṣṇaḥ ṣaṇ-māsā dakṣiṇāyanam
tatra chāndramasaṁ jyotir yogī prāpya nivartate [25]
Translation: Upon leaving the body, the karma-yogī reaches the heavenly plane via the path of smoke, night, the waning moon, and the sun’s southern ascent, but returns to undergo rebirth.

Text:
शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते ।
एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययावर्त्तते पुनः ॥२६॥
Transcription:
śukla-kṛṣṇe gatī hy ete jagataḥ śāśvate mate
ekayā yāty anāvṛttim anyayāvartate punaḥ [26]
Translation: These two paths of the world, the bright and the dark, are accepted as being perpetual. By the bright path liberation is attained, and by the dark path one is reborn.

Text:
नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्योगी मुह्यति कश्चन ।
तस्मात्सर्व्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्ज्जुन ॥२७॥
Transcription:
naite sṛtī pārtha jānan yogī muhyati kaśchana
tasmāt sarveṣu kāleṣu yoga-yukto bhavārjuna [27]
Translation: O Pārtha, the devotee (bhakti-yogī) who knows these bright and dark paths is not deluded. Therefore, O Arjuna, be ever fixed in transcendental, exclusive devotion.

Text:
वेदेषु यज्ञेषु तपःसु चैव
दानेषु यत्पुण्यफलं प्रदिष्टम् ।
अत्येति तत्सर्व्वमिदं विदित्वा
योगी परं स्थानमुपैति चाद्यम् ॥२८॥
Transcription:
vedeṣu yajñeṣu tapaḥsu chaiva
dāneṣu yat puṇya-phalaṁ pradiṣṭam
atyeti tat sarvam idaṁ viditvā
yogī paraṁ sthānam upaiti chādyam [28]
Translation: Thus knowing of My glories and the glories of devotion to Me, the devoted soul, surpassing the scripturally cited fruits of piety achieved by chanting the Vedas and performing sacrifice, austerity, and charity, attains My supreme, holy abode.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
इदं तु ते गुह्यतमं प्रवक्ष्याम्यनसूयवे ।
ज्ञानं विज्ञानसहितं यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥१॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
idaṁ tu te guhyatamaṁ pravakṣyāmy anasūyave
jñānaṁ vijñāna-sahitaṁ yaj jñātvā mokṣyase ’śubhāt [1]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: I now reveal to you, who are without envy, this most hidden treasure of knowledge of pure devotion, with direct realisation of Me, knowing which you will be liberated from inauspiciousness—from all that is opposed to devotion.

Text:
राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम् ।
प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्म्यं सुसुखं कर्त्तुमव्ययम् ॥२॥
Transcription:
rāja-vidyā rāja-guhyaṁ pavitram idam uttamam
pratyakṣāvagamaṁ dharmyaṁ susukhaṁ kartum avyayam [2]
Translation: Know this to be the king of secrets, the king of knowledge, supreme, pure, and imperishable. It is known by direct realisation (by one eager to serve Me), it is the perfection of religion, and very easy to practise.

Text:
अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्म्मस्यास्य परन्तप ।
अप्राप्य मां निवर्त्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्त्मनि ॥३॥
Transcription:
aśraddadhānāḥ puruṣā dharmasyāsya parantapa
aprāpya māṁ nivartante mṛtyu-saṁsāra-vartmani [3]
Translation: O conqueror of the enemy, men who have no faith in this hidden treasure of devotion to Me are unable to reach Me, and so they remain wandering on the path of death in the material world.

Text:
मया ततमिदं सर्व्वं जगदव्यक्तमूर्त्तिना ।
मत्स्थानि सर्व्वभूतानि न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः ॥४॥
Transcription:
mayā tatam idaṁ sarvaṁ jagad avyakta-mūrtinā
mat-sthāni sarva-bhūtāni na chāhaṁ teṣv avasthitaḥ [4]
Translation: This entire universe is pervaded by Me in My unmanifest form, and all beings are situated in Me; yet I am not in them.

Text:
न च मत्स्थानि भूतानि पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम् ।
भूतभृन्न च भूतस्थो ममात्मा भूतभावनः ॥५॥
Transcription:
na cha mat-sthāni bhūtāni paśya me yogam aiśvaram
bhūta-bhṛn na cha bhūta-stho mamātmā bhūta-bhāvanaḥ [5]
Translation: Again, they are not in Me. Behold My supreme mystical power of inconceivable oneness and difference (achintya-bhedābheda). I, Myself, am the mainstay and origin of all beings, but I am not in them.

Text:
यथाकाशस्थितो नित्यं वायुः सर्व्वत्रगो महान्
तथा सर्व्वाणि भूतानि मत्स्थानीत्युपधारय ॥६॥
Transcription:
yathākāśa-sthito nityaṁ vāyuḥ sarvatra-go mahān
tathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni mat-sthānīty upadhāraya [6]
Translation: Know that as the mighty wind blowing everywhere rests always in the sky, similarly, all beings rest in Me.

Text:
सर्व्वभूतानि कौन्तेय प्रकृतिं यान्ति मामिकाम् ।
कल्पक्षये पुनस्तानि कल्पादौ विसृजाम्यहम् ॥७॥
Transcription:
sarva-bhūtāni kaunteya prakṛtiṁ yānti māmikām
kalpa-kṣaye punas tāni kalpādau visṛjāmy aham [7]
Translation: O son of Kuntī, at the end of the millenium, all beings are merged in the material nature of three modes, which is My external potency; and at the beginning of a new millennium, I make them manifest again.

Text:
प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः ।
भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात् ॥८॥
Transcription:
prakṛtiṁ svām avaṣṭabhya visṛjāmi punaḥ punaḥ
bhūta-grāmam imaṁ kṛtsnam avaśaṁ prakṛter vaśāt [8]
Translation: I control My potency of material nature, to repeatedly manifest the multitude of beings, who are helplessly controlled by their natures.

Text:
न च मां तानि कर्म्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय ।
उदासीनवदासीनमसक्तं तेषु कर्म्मसु ॥९॥
Transcription:
na cha māṁ tāni karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanañjaya
udāsīnavad āsīnam asaktaṁ teṣu karmasu [9]
Translation: O conqueror of wealth, I remain detached and situated as neutral in the cosmic manifestation, sustenance, and withdrawal, and these functions cannot bind Me.

Text:
मयाध्यक्षेण प्रकृतिः सूयते सचराचरम् ।
हेतुनानेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्त्तते ॥१०॥
Transcription:
mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ sūyate sa-charācharam
hetunānena kaunteya jagad viparivartate [10]
Translation: O Kaunteya, ordained by Me, My illusory potency produces this universe of moving and stationary beings. Thus it is manifest over and over again.

Text:
अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम् ।
परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम् ॥११॥
Transcription:
avajānanti māṁ mūḍhā mānuṣīṁ tanum āśritam
paraṁ bhāvam ajānanto mama bhūta-maheśvaram [11]
Translation: Not knowing the transcendental nature of My form of human features, ignorant persons blaspheme Me, the Supreme Lord of all beings, considering Me a mere mortal.

Text:
मोघाशा मोघकर्म्माणो मोघज्ञाना विचेतसः ।
राक्षसीमासुरीञ्चैव प्रकृतिं मोहिनीं श्रिताः ॥१२॥
Transcription:
moghāśā mogha-karmāṇo mogha-jñānā vichetasaḥ
rākṣasīm āsurīñ chaiva prakṛtiṁ mohinīṁ śritāḥ [12]
Translation: Their hopes, acts, knowledge, and thoughts all in vain, such persons acquire the ignorant and demoniac nature, which leads to their downfall.

Text:
महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः ।
भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम् ॥१३॥
Transcription:
mahātmānas tu māṁ pārtha daivīṁ prakṛtim āśritāḥ
bhajanty ananya-manaso jñātvā bhūtādim avyayam [13]
Translation: But, O Pārtha, the great-hearted souls take refuge in the divine nature, and they whole-heartedly worship and adore Me, knowing Me as the eternal origin of all.

Text:
सततं कीर्त्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः ।
नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ॥१४॥
Transcription:
satataṁ kīrtayanto māṁ yatantaś cha dṛḍha-vratāḥ
namasyantaś cha māṁ bhaktyā nitya-yuktā upāsate [14]
Translation: They continually chant My glories, strive to learn spiritual truths about Me, and faithfully follow the devotional practices. Bowing to Me and worshipping Me with devotion, they aspire for their eternal relationship with Me.

Text:
ज्ञानयज्ञेन चाप्यन्ये यजन्तो मामुपासते ।
एकत्वेन पृथक्त्वेन बहुधा विश्वतोमुखम् ॥१५॥
Transcription:
jñāna-yajñena chāpy anye yajanto mām upāsate
ekatvena pṛthaktvena bahudhā viśvato-mukham [15]
Translation: Others perform the ‘knowledge-sacrifice’ and worship Me in oneness, others worship Me in differentiation, and yet others worship Me in a multitude of ways as the universal form.

Text:
अहं क्रतुरहं यज्ञः स्वधाहमहमौषधम् ।
मन्त्रोऽहमहमेवाज्यमहमग्निरहं हुतम् ॥१६॥
Transcription:
ahaṁ kratur ahaṁ yajñaḥ svadhāham aham auṣadham
mantro ’ham aham evājyam aham agnir ahaṁ hutam [16]
Translation: I am the Vedic Jyotiṣṭoma sacrifice and the five sacrifices to the Viśvadeva demigods and others as enjoined in the smṛti scriptures. I am the oblation to the ancestors, the food, and the mantra. I am the sacrificial ingredients such as ghee, I am the consecrated fire, and I am the act of offering.

Text:
पिताहमस्य जगतो माता धाता पितामहः ।
वेद्यं पवित्रमोंकार ऋक्साम यजुरेव च ॥१७॥
Transcription:
pitāham asya jagato mātā dhātā pitāmahaḥ
vedyaṁ pavitram oṁkāra ṛk sāma yajur eva cha [17]
Translation: I am the father and the mother of the universe, the ordainer of the living beings’ fruits of actions, the forefather, and the object of all knowledge. I am the purifying Om, the,, and.

Text:
गतिर्भर्त्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत् ।
प्रभवः प्रलयः स्थानं निधानं बीजमव्ययम् ॥१८॥
Transcription:
gatir bhartā prabhuḥ sākṣī nivāsaḥ śaraṇaṁ suhṛt
prabhavaḥ pralayaḥ sthānaṁ nidhānaṁ bījam avyayam [18]
Translation: And I am the goal, the maintainer, master, witness, refuge, guardian, and true friend. I am the universal manifestation, sustenance, and withdrawal. I am the reservoir, I am the original seed, I am the imperishable Supreme Lord.

Text:
तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णाम्युत्सृजामि च ।
अमृतञ्चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्ज्जुन ॥१९॥
Transcription:
tapāmy aham ahaṁ varṣaṁ nigṛhṇāmy utsṛjāmi cha
amṛtañ chaiva mṛtyuś cha sad asach chāham arjuna [19]
Translation: O Arjuna, I give heat, and the rain comes and goes by My will. I am immortality and death. I am truth and untruth.

Text:
त्रैविद्या मां सोमपाः पूतपापा
यज्ञैरिष्ट्वा स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते ।
ते पुण्यमासाद्य सुरेन्द्रलोकम्
अश्नन्ति दिव्यान्दिवि देवभोगान् ॥२०॥
Transcription:
trai-vidyā māṁ soma-pāḥ pūta-pāpā
yajñair iṣṭvā svar-gatiṁ prārthayante
te puṇyam āsādya surendra-lokam
aśnanti divyān divi deva-bhogān [20]
Translation: Persons who perform the fruitive ritualistic sacrifices prescribed in three of the Vedas worship Indra and other demigods. In fact, they worship Me, but in an indirect way. They drink the sacrificial remnants of the soma beverage, purify themselves of sin, and pray to attain the heavenly plane. As the result of their piety, they reach heaven and enjoy celestial pleasures.

Text:
ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालं
क्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्त्यलोकं विशन्ति ।
एवं त्रयीधर्म्ममनुप्रपन्न
गतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते ॥२१॥
Transcription:
te taṁ bhuktvā svarga-lokaṁ viśālaṁ
kṣīṇe puṇye martya-lokaṁ viśanti
evaṁ trayī-dharmam anuprapannā
gatāgataṁ kāma-kāmā labhante [21]
Translation: After enjoying the vast heavenly plane, upon the exhaustion of their pious merits they return to the human plane. In this way, persons desiring enjoyment who follow the Vedic rituals for demigod worship come and go—they undergo repeated birth and death in this material world.

Text:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्य्युपासते ।
तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ॥२२॥
Transcription:
ananyāś chintayanto māṁ ye janāḥ paryupāsate
teṣāṁ nityābhiyuktānāṁ yoga-kṣemaṁ vahāmy aham [22]
Translation: I bear the responsibility of acquiring and protecting the necessities of My fully dependent devotees who are always absorbed in thought of Me, and who worship Me in every way.

Text:
येऽप्यन्यदेवताभक्ता यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः ।
तेऽपि मामेव कौन्तेय यजन्त्यविधिपूर्व्वकम् ॥२३॥
Transcription:
ye ’py anya-devatā-bhaktā yajante śraddhayānvitāḥ
te ’pi mām eva kaunteya yajanty avidhi-pūrvakam [23]
Translation: O Kaunteya, the devotees of the demigods who have faith in worshipping them, are actually worshipping Me, but incorrectly.

Text:
अहं हि सर्व्वयज्ञानां भोक्ता च प्रभुरेव च ।
न तु मामभिजानन्ति तत्त्वेनातश्च्यवन्ति ते ॥२४॥
Transcription:
ahaṁ hi sarva-yajñānāṁ bhoktā cha prabhur eva cha
na tu mām abhijānanti tattvenātaś chyavanti te [24]
Translation: I alone am the enjoyer and rewarder of all sacrifices, but being ignorant of My position, the demigod worshippers fall to take birth again.

Text:
यान्ति देवव्रता देवान्पितॄन्यान्ति पितृव्रताः ।
भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम् ॥२५॥
Transcription:
yānti deva-vratā devān pitṝn yānti pitṛ-vratāḥ
bhūtāni yānti bhūtejyā yānti mad-yājino ’pi mām [25]
Translation: The demigod worshippers reach the demigods, the forefather worshippers go to the plane of their ancestors, and the worshippers of the spirits go to the spirits. But those who worship Me come to Me.

Text:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति ।
तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ॥२६॥
Transcription:
patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayachchhati
tad ahaṁ bhakty-upahṛtam aśnāmi prayatātmanaḥ [26]
Translation: If one offers Me with devotion a leaf, flower, fruit, or water, I affectionately accept that offering of the clean hearted devotee.

Text:
यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत् ।
यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम् ॥२७॥
Transcription:
yat karoṣi yad aśnāsi yaj juhoṣi dadāsi yat
yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kuruṣva mad-arpaṇam [27]
Translation: O Kaunteya, whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer in sacrifice, whatever you give, and whatever vow you may keep—do everything as an offering unto Me.

Text:
शुभाशुभफलैरेवं मोक्ष्यसे कर्म्मबन्धनैः ।
सन्न्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा विमुक्तो मामुपैष्यसि ॥२८॥
Transcription:
śubhāśubha-phalair evaṁ mokṣyase karma-bandhanaiḥ
sannyāsa-yoga-yuktātmā vimukto mām upaiṣyasi [28]
Translation: Acting thus, you will be freed from bondage to action and its auspicious or inauspicious results. With mind selflessly linked to Me, you will be perfectly liberated, and you will come to Me.

Text:
समोऽहं सर्व्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः ।
ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥२९॥
Transcription:
samo ’haṁ sarva-bhūteṣu na me dveṣyo ’sti na priyaḥ
ye bhajanti tu māṁ bhaktyā mayi te teṣu chāpy aham [29]
Translation: I am equal to all, so no one is My enemy or My friend. Yet, for those who serve Me with love, as they are bound by affection for Me, I am similarly bound by the tie of affection for them.

Text:
अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक् ।
साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः ॥३०॥
Transcription:
api chet sudurāchāro bhajate mām ananya-bhāk
sādhur eva sa mantavyaḥ samyag vyavasito hi saḥ [30]
Translation: If even a very sinful person serves Me exclusively with devotion, he should be regarded as saintly, for his resolve is perfect.

Text:
क्षिप्रं भवति धर्म्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति ।
कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति ॥३१॥
Transcription:
kṣipraṁ bhavati dharmātmā śaśvach-chhāntiṁ nigachchhati
kaunteya pratijānīhi na me bhaktaḥ praṇaśyati [31]
Translation:
He swiftly becomes a person of virtuous practices and attains constant peace. O son of Kuntī, declare to the world that My devotee is never vanquished!

or

O son of Kuntī, declare to the world that My devotee is never vanquished. One who declares this swiftly becomes virtuous and attains eternal joy.
Comment:
The second interpretation of this verse was revealed to Śrīla Bhakti Vinod Ṭhākur in a dream.
In the Śrīmad Bhāgavatam (11.11.32) Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa says:

आज्ञायैवं गुणान्दोषान्मयादिष्टानपि स्वकान् ।
धर्म्मान्सन्त्यज्य यः सर्व्वान्मां भजेत्स च सत्तमः ॥

ājñāyaivaṁ guṇān doṣān mayādiṣṭān api svakān
dharmān santyajya yaḥ sarvān māṁ bhajet sa cha sattamaḥ

“The best of the saintly persons are those who have surpassed the forms of duties that I, Myself, have recommended in the scriptures for the people in general. Although it is My directive, they cross it and render loving service to Me.”

In society, one must obey the law, but there is also the situation of crossing the law to show fidelity to the king. If one risks his life and reputation, and crossing the general law enters the royal chamber to combat an assassin, then he will be considered the most loyal servitor. Similarly, the Lord is saying, “I have already given some direction for the people in general. Do this, don’t do that, don’t cross these laws, etc. But if for My interest anyone takes the risk of committing sin, then he should be considered the best of My devotees.

“So, Arjuna, go and declare it, promise to the public that the ananya-bhāk (verse 30), the exclusively devoted persons, will never come to ruin. Then you will get the benefit. You will become dharmātmā, religious, and attain eternal divine happiness. The ananya-bhāk, the exclusive devotees, have already crossed the threshold of dharma, the standard of dutifulness, and taken the risk to throw themselves fully into My service. Sarva dharmān parityajya Mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja (18.66). There is no question of them ‘becoming’ virtuous or religious as they long before surpassed dharma and took the risk and entered into prema-dharma, My loving service, transcending both piety and sinfulness.”

Text:
मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः ।
स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम् ॥३२॥
Transcription:
māṁ hi pārtha vyapāśritya ye ’pi syuḥ pāpa-yonayaḥ
striyo vaiśyās tathā śūdrās te ’pi yānti parāṁ gatim [32]
Translation: O son of Pṛthā, persons of low birth, women, merchants or labourers—they, too, attain the supreme destination by taking full refuge in Me.

Text:
किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पुण्या भक्ता राजर्षयस्तथा ।
अनित्यमसुखं लोकमिमं प्राप्य भजस्व माम् ॥३३॥
Transcription:
kiṁ punar brāhmaṇāḥ puṇyā bhaktā rājarṣayas tathā
anityam asukhaṁ lokam imaṁ prāpya bhajasva mām [33]
Translation: So can there be any doubt that those brāhmaṇs and saintly kings who are pure devotees will achieve the supreme goal? Therefore, worship Me, since although this human body is temporary and a place of suffering, it is attained only after many births and it affords the best opportunity to render devotional service to Me.

Text:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु ।
मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥३४॥
Transcription:
man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru
mām evaiṣyasi yuktvaivam ātmānaṁ mat-parāyaṇaḥ [34]
Translation: Always think of Me, be my devotee, always worship Me and offer obeisances to Me. Thus offering yourself to Me and taking refuge in Me, you will come to Me.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
भूय एव महाबाहो शृणु मे परमं वचः ।
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया ॥१॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
bhūya eva mahā-bāho śṛṇu me paramaṁ vachaḥ
yat te ’haṁ prīyamāṇāya vakṣyāmi hita-kāmyayā [1]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: O heroic Arjuna, listen to My supreme word once again. Desiring your welfare, I shall speak to you, who are very dear to Me.

Text:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः ।
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणाञ्च सर्व्वशः ॥२॥
Transcription:
na me viduḥ sura-gaṇāḥ prabhavaṁ na maharṣayaḥ
aham ādir hi devānāṁ maharṣīṇāñ cha sarvaśaḥ [2]
Translation: Neither the demigods nor the great sages can understand My transcendental appearance in this world, as I alone am the origin of those celestial beings and sages.

Text:
यो मामजमनादिञ्च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम् ।
असम्मूढः स मर्त्त्येषु सर्व्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥३॥
Transcription:
yo mām ajam anādiñ cha vetti loka-maheśvaram
asammūḍhaḥ sa martyeṣu sarva-pāpaiḥ pramuchyate [3]
