Translation: Know that both material nature (māyā) and the person (individual soul) are beginningless, and know that the transformations (body, senses, and their functions) and the transformations of the modes of nature (such as pleasure, pain, sorrow, and delusion) are born of material nature.

Text:
कार्य्यकारणकर्त्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते ।
पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ॥२१॥
Transcription:
kārya-kāraṇa-kartṛtve hetuḥ prakṛtir uchyate
puruṣaḥ sukha-duḥkhānāṁ bhoktṛtve hetur uchyate [21]
Translation: Material nature is said to be the cause of the production of effect (the body) and cause (the senses); the person (in material bondage) is said to be the cause of the condition of being an enjoyer or a sufferer of happiness and unhappiness.

Text:
पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् ।
कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ॥२२॥
Transcription:
puruṣaḥ prakṛti-stho hi bhuṅkte prakṛti-jān guṇān
kāraṇaṁ guṇa-saṅgo ’sya sad-asad-yoni-janmasu [22]
Translation: Being situated within material nature, a person enjoys the modes born of material nature. His attachment to these modes is the cause of his repeated births in higher and lower species.

Text:
उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्त्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः ।
परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः ॥२३॥
Transcription:
upadraṣṭānumantā cha bhartā bhoktā maheśvaraḥ
paramātmeti chāpy ukto dehe ’smin puruṣaḥ paraḥ [23]
Translation: The Supreme Person—the witness, sanctioner, support, guardian, and almighty Lord within this body, is known as Paramātmā, the Supersoul.

Text:
य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिञ्च गुणैः सह ।
सर्व्वथा वर्त्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ॥२४॥
Transcription:
ya evaṁ vetti puruṣaṁ prakṛtiñ cha guṇaiḥ saha
sarvathā vartamāno ’pi na sa bhūyo ’bhijāyate [24]
Translation: Thus, one who truly knows the Supreme Person and material nature with its modes, will never in any circumstances have to take birth again.

Text:
ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना ।
अन्ये साङ्ख्ये न योगेन कर्म्मयोगेन चापरे ॥२५॥
Transcription:
dhyānenātmani paśyanti kechid ātmānam ātmanā
anye sāṅkhyena yogena karma-yogena chāpare [25]
Translation: With their pure consciousness, some persons see the Supersoul within the heart, in their meditation. Others perceive Him through enlightenment, and yet others by the yoga of selfless action.

Text:
अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते ।
तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः ॥२६॥
Transcription:
anye tv evam ajānantaḥ śrutvānyebhya upāsate
te ’pi chātitaranty eva mṛtyuṁ śruti-parāyaṇāḥ [26]
Translation: Further, there are those who, not knowing of these methods, engage in worship by hearing the scriptures. When their faith is deepened by such hearing, they, too, surpass the mortal plane.

Text:
यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम् ।
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥२७॥
Transcription:
yāvat sañjāyate kiñchit sattvaṁ sthāvara-jaṅgamam
kṣetra-kṣetrajña-saṁyogāt tad viddhi bharatarṣabha [27]
Translation: O Arjuna, best of the Bhāratas, whatever is born in the world, whether moving or stationary, know it to be manifest from the combination of the field and its knower.

Text:
समं सर्व्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् ।
विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥२८॥
Transcription:
samaṁ sarveṣu bhūteṣu tiṣṭhantaṁ parameśvaram
vinaśyatsv avinaśyantaṁ yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati [28]
Translation: One who sees the Supreme Lord situated equally within all beings as the imperishable within the perishable—such a person sees perfectly.

Text:
समं पश्यन्हि सर्व्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् ।
न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥२९॥
Transcription:
samaṁ paśyan hi sarvatra samavasthitam īśvaram
na hinasty ātmanātmānaṁ tato yāti parāṁ gatim [29]
Translation: Such a person with the vision of equality, seeing the Lord dwelling in every place alike, does not deceive himself, and thus he attains the supreme destination.

Text:
प्रकृत्यैव च कर्म्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्व्वशः ।
यः पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्त्तारं स पश्यति ॥३०॥
Transcription:
prakṛtyaiva cha karmāṇi kriyamāṇāni sarvaśaḥ
yaḥ paśyati tathātmānam akartāraṁ sa paśyati [30]
Translation: He who sees that all actions are brought about only by material nature, sees himself as non-doer.

Text:
यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति ।
तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा ॥३१॥
Transcription:
yadā bhūta-pṛthag-bhāvam eka-stham anupaśyati
tata eva cha vistāraṁ brahma sampadyate tadā [31]
Translation: When such an enlightened person realises that the different types of beings are situated within the one nature and that all are made manifest from that nature, he attains realisation of Brahma.

Text:
अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः ।
शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥३२॥
Transcription:
anāditvān nirguṇatvāt paramātmāyam avyayaḥ
śarīra-stho ’pi kaunteya na karoti na lipyate [32]
Translation: O Arjuna, since the Supersoul is beginningless, transcendental, and inexhaustible, although also present within the field (with the individual soul), He neither acts nor is affected by any action.

Text:
यथा सर्व्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते ।
सर्व्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते ॥३३॥
Transcription:
yathā sarva-gataṁ saukṣmyād ākāśaṁ nopalipyate
sarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate [33]
Translation: As the most subtle element of space pervades everything yet is not tainted by anything, similarly, the soul, though pervading the body, is not tainted by it.

Text:
यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः ।
क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ॥३४॥
Transcription:
yathā prakāśayaty ekaḥ kṛtsnaṁ lokam imaṁ raviḥ
kṣetraṁ kṣetrī tathā kṛtsnaṁ prakāśayati bhārata [34]
Translation: O Bhārat, as the one sun illuminates the entire world, the occupier of the field illuminates the entire field.

Text:
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा ।
भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षञ्च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् ॥३५॥
Transcription:
kṣetra-kṣetrajñayor evam antaraṁ jñāna-chakṣuṣā
bhūta-prakṛti-mokṣañ cha ye vidur yānti te param [35]
Translation: Those who, through the eyes of knowledge, can thus distinguish between the field and the knower of the field, and who can thus know the path of liberation of the living beings from material nature—such persons attain the Supreme.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
परं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् ।
यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्व्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः ॥१॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
paraṁ bhūyaḥ pravakṣyāmi jñānānāṁ jñānam uttamam
yaj jñātvā munayaḥ sarve parāṁ siddhim ito gatāḥ [1]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: Again, I shall describe to you the supreme teaching, the highest of all fields of knowledge, knowing which the sages attained the ultimate perfection beyond this mundane plane.

Text:
इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्म्यमागताः ।
सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ॥२॥
Transcription:
idaṁ jñānam upāśritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥ
sarge ’pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti cha [2]
Translation: By taking refuge in this knowledge, the soul attains to My nature. Then he is neither born in the cosmic manifestation, nor does he suffer (death) in the cosmic dissolution.

Text:
मम योनिर्महद्ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् ।
सम्भवः सर्व्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥३॥
Transcription:
mama yonir mahad-brahma tasmin garbhaṁ dadhāmy aham
sambhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ tato bhavati bhārata [3]
Translation: O Bhārat, I place the seed within My womb of primordial matter from which all living beings take birth.

Text:
सर्व्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्त्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः ।
तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता ॥४॥
Transcription:
sarva-yoniṣu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥ
tāsāṁ brahma mahad yonir ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā [4]
Translation: O Kaunteya, primordial matter is the original womb of all life-forms that are born of the wombs of all species, and I am the seed-giving father.

Text:
सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसम्भवाः ।
निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥५॥
Transcription:
sattvaṁ rajas tama iti guṇāḥ prakṛti-sambhavāḥ
nibadhnanti mahā-bāho dehe dehinam avyayam [5]
Translation: O mighty hero, Arjuna, the three modes of goodness, passion, and ignorance, born of material nature, bind the immutable spirit soul within the body.

Text:
तत्र सत्त्वं निर्म्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् ।
सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥६॥
Transcription:
tatra sattvaṁ nirmalatvāt prakāśakam anāmayam
sukha-saṅgena badhnāti jñāna-saṅgena chānagha [6]
Translation: O sinless one, of these three modes, due its purity, the mode of goodness is illuminating and wholesome, conditioning one to happiness and knowledge.

Text:
रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम् ।
तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्म्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥७॥
Transcription:
rajo rāgātmakaṁ viddhi tṛṣṇā-saṅga-samudbhavam
tan nibadhnāti kaunteya karma-saṅgena dehinam [7]
Translation: O son of Kuntī, know the mode of passion to be the embodiment of desire. Born of hankering and attachment, it conditions one to action.

Text:
तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्व्वदेहिनाम् ।
प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत ॥८॥
Transcription:
tamas tv ajñāna-jaṁ viddhi mohanaṁ sarva-dehinām
pramādālasya-nidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata [8]
Translation: And, O Bhārat, know the mode of ignorance, born of darkness, to be the ignorance of all the embodied beings, conditioning them to delusion, laziness, and slumber.

Text:
सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्म्मणि भारत ।
ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ॥९॥
Transcription:
sattvaṁ sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata
jñānam āvṛtya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayaty uta [9]
Translation: O Arjuna, the mode of goodness conditions the embodied being to happiness, and the mode of passion conditions him to action. But the mode of ignorance covers his knowledge and traps him in illusion.

Text:
रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत ।
रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ॥१०॥
Transcription:
rajas tamaś chābhibhūya sattvaṁ bhavati bhārata
rajaḥ sattvaṁ tamaś chaiva tamaḥ sattvaṁ rajas tathā [10]
Translation: O Bhārat, goodness overcomes passion and ignorance, passion overcomes goodness and ignorance, and ignorance overcomes goodness and passion. Each mode in turn defeats the others in an unending battle for supremacy.

Text:
सर्व्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते ।
ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ॥११॥
Transcription:
sarva-dvāreṣu dehe ’smin prakāśa upajāyate
jñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛddhaṁ sattvam ity uta [11]
Translation: When the gateways of the body are illuminated with knowledge, know this to be the manifestation of the mode of goodness.

Text:
लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्म्मणामशमः स्पृहा ।
रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ॥१२॥
Transcription:
lobhaḥ pravṛttir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśamaḥ spṛhā
rajasy etāni jāyante vivṛddhe bharatarṣabha [12]
Translation: O Arjuna, most noble of the Bharat dynasty, know that when the mode of passion predominates in a person, greed, exertion, undertaking of activities, unrest, and hankering become manifest.

Text:
अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च ।
तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥१३॥
Transcription:
aprakāśo ’pravṛttiś cha pramādo moha eva cha
tamasy etāni jāyante vivṛddhe kuru-nandana [13]
Translation: O Kuru Nandan, by the influence of the mode of ignorance, all the symptoms of dullness, inactivity, delusion, and bewilderment become manifest.

Text:
यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् ।
तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥१४॥
Transcription:
yadā sattve pravṛddhe tu pralayaṁ yāti deha-bhṛt
tadottama-vidāṁ lokān amalān pratipadyate [14]
Translation: If a person dies in a state of the mode of goodness, he goes to the pure planes where those who have higher knowledge reside.

Text:
रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्म्मसङ्गिषु जायते ।
तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ॥१५॥
Transcription:
rajasi pralayaṁ gatvā karma-saṅgiṣu jāyate
tathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣu jāyate [15]
Translation: When a person dies in the mode of passion, he takes birth as a human attached to worldly activity. If one dies in the mode of ignorance, he takes birth in the lower species.

Text:
कर्म्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्म्मलं फलम् ।
रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम् ॥१६॥
Transcription:
karmaṇaḥ sukṛtasyāhuḥ sāttvikaṁ nirmalaṁ phalam
rajasas tu phalaṁ duḥkham ajñānaṁ tamasaḥ phalam [16]
Translation: It is said by the learned that good actions result in purity, passionate actions result in sorrow, and ignorant actions lead to darkness.

Text:
सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च ।
प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ॥१७॥
Transcription:
sattvāt sañjāyate jñānaṁ rajaso lobha eva cha
pramāda-mohau tamaso bhavato ’jñānam eva cha [17]
Translation: From the mode of goodness, knowledge arises; from the mode of passion, greed; and from the mode of ignorance, delusion, bewilderment, and a lack of knowledge ensue.

Text:
ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः ।
जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः ॥१८॥
Transcription:
ūrdhvaṁ gachchhanti sattva-sthā madhye tiṣṭhanti rājasāḥ
jaghanya-guṇa-vṛtti-sthā adho gachchhanti tāmasāḥ [18]
Translation: Persons in goodness ascend (up to Satyaloka), those in passion remain in the middle (as humans), and the unfortunate, ignorant persons fall to the lower planes (of suffering).

Text:
नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्त्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति ।
गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥१९॥
Transcription:
nānyaṁ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṁ yadā draṣṭānupaśyati
guṇebhyaś cha paraṁ vetti mad-bhāvaṁ so ’dhigachchhati [19]
Translation: When a person sees the three modes to be the only impetus of this world, and knows the Supreme Lord, transcendental to the modes, then he comes to love Me with devotion.

Text:
गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भवान् ।
जन्ममृत्युजरादुःखैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्नुते ॥२०॥
Transcription:
guṇān etān atītya trīn dehī deha-samudbhavān
janma-mṛtyu-jarā-duḥkhair vimukto ’mṛtam aśnute [20]
Translation: Transcending these modes manifest in the body, he is liberated from the miseries of birth, death, and old age, and knows the joy of immortality.

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो ।
किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्त्तते ॥२१॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
kair liṅgais trīn guṇān etān atīto bhavati prabho
kim āchāraḥ kathaṁ chaitāṁs trīn guṇān ativartate [21]
Translation: Arjuna said: O Lord, what are the symptoms of a person transcendental to these three modes of material nature? How does he behave, and how does he transcend the modes?

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
प्रकाशञ्च प्रवृत्तिञ्च मोहमेव च पाण्डव ।
न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति ॥२२॥
उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते ।
गुणा वर्त्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ॥२३॥
समदुःखसुखः स्वस्थः समलोष्ट्राश्मकाञ्चनः ।
तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुतिः ॥२४॥
मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः ।
सर्व्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते ॥२५॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
prakāśañ cha pravṛttiñ cha moham eva cha pāṇḍava
na dveṣṭi sampravṛttāni na nivṛttāni kāṅkṣati [22]
udāsīnavad āsīno guṇair yo na vichālyate
guṇā vartanta ity evaṁ yo ’vatiṣṭhati neṅgate [23]
sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ svasthaḥ sama-loṣṭrāśma-kāñchanaḥ
tulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-saṁstutiḥ [24]
mānāpamānayos tulyas tulyo mitrāri-pakṣayoḥ
sarvārambha-parityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa uchyate [25]
Translation: The Supreme Lord replied: O Pāṇḍava, it is said that a person who has transcended the three modes of material nature neither resents illumination, activation, and delusion when they appear, nor does he hanker for them when they withdraw. Poised in the knowledge that the modes are engaging, he is not distracted by them; he remains unperturbed, unconcerned. Remaining self-satisfied, he sees joy and sorrow equally; he sees earth, rock, or gold with the vision of equality; he is wise, equipoised in desirable and undesirable circumstances, abuse and praise, honour and dishonour; he behaves fairly with friend and foe alike, and he renounces all mundane endeavours.

Text:
माञ्च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते ।
स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥२६॥
Transcription:
māñ cha yo ’vyabhichāreṇa bhakti-yogena sevate
sa guṇān samatītyaitān brahma-bhūyāya kalpate [26]
Translation: A person who, without deviation, serves Me with devotion, transcends the three modes of material nature and becomes qualified to know his internal divine identity.

Text:
ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च ।
शाश्वतस्य च धर्म्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥२७॥
Transcription:
brahmaṇo hi pratiṣṭhāham amṛtasyāvyayasya cha
śāśvatasya cha dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya cha [27]
Translation: I am the basis of the Absolute Truth, the inexhaustible nectar, the eternal Pastimes, and the ultimate ecstasy of divine love.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
ऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम् ।
छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥१॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
ūrdhva-mūlam adhaḥ-śākham aśvatthaṁ prāhur avyayam
chhandāṁsi yasya parṇāni yas taṁ veda sa veda-vit [1]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: It is said in the scriptures that this material world is like a Peepul tree, roots up, branches down, endless, yet transient. Its leaves represent the nourishing verses of the Vedas. One who knows this tree is a knower of the Vedas.
Comment:
The roots of the tree of this world are said to face upwards—its root cause is the highest plane. It is manifest by the potency of the Supreme Lord. However, it is rooted in aversion to the Lord. Its branches are said to face downwards since according to the law of karma all species of life from the highest to the lowest branch out from it. It is known as an Aśvattha tree. The word aśvattha means ‘ephemeral,’ or ‘that which does not remain in the same state for even a day.’ Yet, it is endless* in the sense that it constantly appears in the natural flow of cause and effect. The leaves of the tree represent the fruitive verses of the Vedas (karma-kāṇḍa). As the leaves of a tree nourish and beautify it, these verses similarly encourage and illuminate the world.
The external potency of the Lord, māyā, is eternal, and it manifests this world. Yet the world itself is perishable inasmuch as it again becomes unmanifest. Although the fruitive verses of the Vedas provide its nourishment, their underlying purpose is rather to enlighten the conditioned souls to take shelter of the Supreme Lord who is transcendental to the mundane. Thus, one who knows these fundamental principles is a knower of the true essence of the Vedas.

*The Peepul tree is known for its longevity.

Text:
अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा
गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः ।
अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि
कर्म्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके ॥२॥
Transcription:
adhaś chordhvaṁ prasṛtās tasya śākhā
guṇa-pravṛddhā viṣaya-pravālāḥ
adhaś cha mūlāny anusantatāni
karmānubandhīni manuṣya-loke [2]
Translation: Some of its branches extend upwards (as the planes of the demigods and celestial beings), some of the branches extend downwards (as the planes of the humans, animals, and lower species), and nourished by three modes of nature, its shoots are the objects of the senses. Some aerial roots also extend downwards, to take root in the land of karma, within the human plane.
Comment:
Within the expansive manifestation of this ‘perishable yet endless’ world, some of the living beings are nourished by the mode of goodness, and they wander throughout the upper planes, in the ego of gods and celestial beings. Others, influenced by the modes of passion and ignorance, are wandering throughout the planes of the humans, animals, trees, and lower species, assuming the identities of those species. The shoots of the tree of this world represent the objects of the senses—sound, smell, touch, taste, and form, as transformations of the five primary subtle elements (pañcha-tanmātra) produced from the ego of the living beings.
The principal roots face upwards, inverted, indicating aversion to the Supreme Lord. Further, some aerial roots face downward, entering the land of karma in the human plane. These represent the humans’ attempt to enjoy the fruits of their actions, and these aerial roots nourish the tree as a separate, secondary cause.

Text:
न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते
नान्तो न चादिर्न च सम्प्रतिष्ठा ।
अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलम्
असङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ॥३॥
Transcription:
na rūpam asyeha tathopalabhyate
nānto na chādir na cha sampratiṣṭhā
aśvattham enaṁ suvirūḍha-mūlam
asaṅga-śastreṇa dṛḍhena chhittvā
Translation: In the human plane, this inverted form of the Peepul tree of this material world is inconceivable (without Vedic knowledge)—neither its beginning, nor its middle, nor its end can be perceived. From the association of devotees, one acquires the sharp axe of detachment from the mundane.

Text:
निर्म्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा
अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः ।
द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञैर्
गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत् ॥५॥
Transcription:
nirmāna-mohā jita-saṅga-doṣā
adhyātma-nityā vinivṛtta-kāmāḥ
dvandvair vimuktāḥ sukha-duḥkha-saṁjñair
gachchhanty amūḍhāḥ padam avyayaṁ tat [5]
Translation: Free from vanity and delusion, aloof to unholy association, dedicated to self-realisation, desireless, liberated from the duality of joy and sorrow, undeluded, those surrendered souls reach the eternal goal.

Text:
न तद्भासयते सूर्य्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः ।
यद्गत्वा न निवर्त्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥६॥
Transcription:
na tad bhāsayate sūryo na śaśāṅko na pāvakaḥ
yad gatvā na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṁ mama [6]
Translation: The surrendered souls reach My eternal abode, never to return to this world. Neither sun, nor moon, nor fire—nothing can illuminate that all-illuminating supreme abode.

Text:
ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः ।
मनः षष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ॥७॥
Transcription:
mamaivāṁśo jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ
manaḥ ṣaṣṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛti-sthāni karṣati [7]
Translation: The soul is a particle (potency) of Mine. Although it is eternal, for worldly existence it adopts the five mundane senses and the mind, which is the sixth.

Text:
शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः ।
गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ॥८॥
Transcription:
śarīraṁ yad avāpnoti yach chāpy utkrāmatīśvaraḥ
gṛhītvaitāni saṁyāti vāyur gandhān ivāśayāt [8]
Translation: The soul is the master of the body. When it departs the body, it goes to enter another, carrying these (subtle) senses, as the wind carries fragrance from its source.

Text:
श्रोत्रञ्चक्षुः स्पर्शनञ्च रसनं घ्राणमेव च ।
अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥९॥
Transcription:
śrotrañ chakṣuḥ sparśanañ cha rasanaṁ ghrāṇam eva cha
adhiṣṭhāya manaś chāyaṁ viṣayān upasevate [9]
Translation: Presiding over the ear, eye, skin, tongue, and nose, and also the mind, the living entity enjoys the sense objects of sound, form, touch, taste, and smell.

Text:
उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम् ।
विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः ॥१०॥
Transcription:
utkrāmantaṁ sthitaṁ vāpi bhuñjānaṁ vā guṇānvitam
vimūḍhā nānupaśyanti paśyanti jñāna-chakṣuṣaḥ [10]
Translation: Those who are thus deluded cannot see the soul when it is departing the body, residing in the body, or when enjoying through the senses in the body. Those with the eyes of wisdom can see.

Text:
यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम् ।
यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः ॥११॥
Transcription:
yatanto yoginaś chainaṁ paśyanty ātmany avasthitam
yatanto ’py akṛtātmāno nainaṁ paśyanty achetasaḥ [11]
Translation: And the sincere seekers see the soul present within. But persons of poor understanding and lacking in self-control cannot see the soul, despite their endeavours.

Text:
यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम् ।
यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् ॥१२॥
Transcription:
yad āditya-gataṁ tejo jagad bhāsayate ’khilam
yach chandramasi yach chāgnau tat tejo viddhi māmakam [12]
Translation: The light of the sun that illuminates the entire universe, the light of the moon, and the radiance of fire—know that to be Mine.

Text:
गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा ।
पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्व्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः ॥१३॥
Transcription:
gām āviśya cha bhūtāni dhārayāmy aham ojasā
puṣṇāmi chauṣadhīḥ sarvāḥ somo bhūtvā rasātmakaḥ [13]
Translation: Entering the earth, I maintain all beings by My potency; and in the form of the life-giving moon, I nourish the crops.

Text:
अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः ।
प्राणापानसमायुक्तःपचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम् ॥१४॥
Transcription:
ahaṁ vaiśvānaro bhūtvā prāṇināṁ deham āśritaḥ
prāṇāpāna-samāyuktaḥ pachāmy annaṁ chatur-vidham [14]
Translation: Entering into the bodies of the living beings as the power of digestion, I digest the four types of eatables (chewed, sucked, licked, and drunk), through the agency of the ascending and descending vital airs.

Text:
सर्व्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्ठो
मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनञ्च ।
वेदैश्च सर्व्वैरहमेव वेद्यो
वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् ॥१५॥
Transcription:
sarvasya chāhaṁ hṛdi sanniviṣṭho
mattaḥ smṛtir jñānam apohanañ cha
vedaiś cha sarvair aham eva vedyo
vedānta-kṛd veda-vid eva chāham [15]
Translation: I am situated (as the Supersoul) within the heart of all souls, and from Me arises the soul’s remembrance, knowledge, and forgetfulness (according to his actions). I alone am the Sweet Absolute to be known through all the Vedas. I am the revealer of the Vedānta—Vedavyās, and I am the knower of the Vedas.

Text:
द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च ।
क्षरः सर्व्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते ॥१६॥
Transcription:
dvāv imau puruṣau loke kṣaraś chākṣara eva cha
kṣaraḥ sarvāṇi bhūtāni kūṭastho ’kṣara uchyate [16]
Translation: In this world, there are two types of beings: the perishable and the imperishable. All beings from Lord Brahmā down to the lowest stationary life-forms are known as perishable (as they are fallen from their true nature). But those who are situated in their eternal, spiritual nature are known as imperishable (eternal associates of the Lord).

Text:
उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः ।
यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः ॥१७॥
Transcription:
uttamaḥ puruṣas tv anyaḥ paramātmety udāhṛtaḥ
yo loka-trayam āviśya bibharty avyaya īśvaraḥ [17]
Translation: But completely distinct from both these types of beings is the Supreme Person, who is known as Paramātmā, the Supersoul. He is the Supreme Lord. Entering the three worlds in His eternal form, He maintains all beings in the universe.

Text:
यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः ।
अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥१८॥
Transcription:
yasmāt kṣaram atīto ’ham akṣarād api chottamaḥ
ato ’smi loke vede cha prathitaḥ puruṣottamaḥ [18]
Translation: Because I am transcendental to the perishable beings and also superior to My imperishable eternal associates, My glories are sung in the world and in the scriptures as Puruṣottam, the Supreme Person.

Text:
यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम् ।
स सर्व्वविद्भजति मां सर्व्वभावेन भारत ॥१९॥
Transcription:
yo mām evam asammūḍho jānāti puruṣottamam
sa sarva-vid bhajati māṁ sarva-bhāvena bhārata [19]
Translation: O Bhārat, one who is free from delusion and knows Me as the Supreme Person of eternal, all-conscious, ecstatic form, is the perfect knower of full-fledged theism, and he worships Me in all ways (in the spiritual relationships of tranquillity, servitorship, friendship, parenthood, and consorthood).

Text:
इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयानघ ।
एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत ॥२०॥
Transcription:
iti guhyatamaṁ śāstram idam uktaṁ mayānagha
etad buddhvā buddhimān syāt kṛta-kṛtyaś cha bhārata [20]
Translation: O pure hearted Arjuna, I have thus explained to you this most hidden treasure of all the scriptures. O Bhārat, embracing this in their hearts, the persons of fine theistic intelligence attain the perfection of their endeavours.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः ।
दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्ज्जवम् ॥१॥
अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम् ।
दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्द्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ॥२॥
तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता ।
भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ॥३॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
abhayaṁ sattva-saṁśuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥ
dānaṁ damaś cha yajñaś cha svādhyāyas tapa ārjavam [1]
ahiṁsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śāntir apaiśunam
dayā bhūteṣv aloluptvaṁ mārdavaṁ hrīr achāpalam [2]
tejaḥ kṣamā dhṛtiḥ śaucham adroho nātimānitā
bhavanti sampadaṁ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata [3]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: O Bhārat, these are the qualities of a person born of a godly nature: fearlessness, gracious-heartedness, absorption in self-knowledge, generosity, sense-control, sacrifice, study of the Vedas, austerity, sincerity, nonviolence, truthfulness, freedom from anger, worldly detachment, tranquillity, disinclination to see the faults of others, compassion, freedom from greed, gentleness, modesty, steadfastness, vigour, forgiveness, patience, purity, and freedom from malice and egoism.

Text:
दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च ।
अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ॥४॥
Transcription:
dambho darpo ’bhimānaś cha krodhaḥ pāruṣyam eva cha
ajñānaṁ chābhijātasya pārtha sampadam āsurīm [4]
Translation: O Pārtha, these are the qualities of a person born of an ungodly nature: pride, conceit, egoism, anger, cruelty, and ignorance.

Text:
दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता ।
मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ॥५॥
Transcription:
daivī sampad vimokṣāya nibandhāyāsurī matā
mā śuchaḥ sampadaṁ daivīm abhijāto ’si pāṇḍava [5]
Translation: The godly qualities have been described as the cause of liberation, and the ungodly qualities have been described as the cause of bondage. O Pāṇḍava, do not fear, for you are born of a godly nature.

Text:
द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन्दैव आसुर एव च ।
दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे शृणु ॥६॥
Transcription:
dvau bhūta-sargau loke ’smin daiva āsura eva cha
daivo vistaraśaḥ prokta āsuraṁ pārtha me śṛṇu [6]
Translation: O Pārtha, in this world the living beings are of two natures—the godly and the ungodly. I have elaborately described the godly nature to you, so now hear from Me about the ungodly:

Text:
प्रवृत्तिञ्च निवृत्तिञ्च जना न विदुरासुराः ।
न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते ॥७॥
Transcription:
pravṛttiñ cha nivṛttiñ cha janā na vidur āsurāḥ
na śauchaṁ nāpi chāchāro na satyaṁ teṣu vidyate [7]
Translation: The ungodly cannot distinguish between right and wrong action. No purity, good practices, or truthfulness can be found in them.

Text:
असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम् ।
अपरस्परसम्भूतं किमन्यत्कामहेतुकम् ॥८॥
Transcription:
asatyam apratiṣṭhaṁ te jagad āhur anīśvaram
aparaspara-sambhūtaṁ kim anyat kāma-hetukam [8]
Translation: The persons of ungodly nature say the world is without truth, baseless, godless, and that everything is born of male and female union. They say that life has no purpose beyond lust.

Text:
एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धयः ।
प्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्म्माणः क्षयाय जगतोऽहिताः ॥९॥
Transcription:
etāṁ dṛṣṭim avaṣṭabhya naṣṭātmāno ’lpa-buddhayaḥ
prabhavanty ugra-karmāṇaḥ kṣayāya jagato ’hitāḥ [9]
Translation: Holding such a view, the demons, depraved and of low intelligence, become powerful and engage in cruel, inauspicious acts for the destruction of the world.

Text:
काममाश्रित्य दुष्पूरं दम्भमानमदान्विताः ।
मोहाद्गृहीत्वासद्ग्राहान्प्रवर्त्तन्तेऽशुचिव्रताः ॥१०॥
Transcription:
kāmam āśritya duṣpūraṁ dambha-māna-madānvitāḥ
mohād gṛhītvāsad-grāhān pravartante ’śuchi-vratāḥ [10]
Translation: Giving themselves up to the delusion of insatiable desires for mundane enjoyment, such demons, full of vanity, pride, and arrogance, engage in grave malpractices.

Text:
चिन्तामपरिमेयाञ्च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिताः ।
कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्चिताः ॥११॥
आशापाशशतैर्बद्धाः कामक्रोधपरायणाः ।
ईहन्ते कामभोगार्थमन्यायेनार्थसञ्चयान् ॥१२॥
Transcription:
chintām aparimeyāñ cha pralayāntām upāśritāḥ
kāmopabhoga-paramā etāvad iti niśchitāḥ [11]
āśā-pāśa-śatair baddhāḥ kāma-krodha-parāyaṇāḥ
īhante kāma-bhogārtham anyāyenārtha-sañchayān [12]
Translation: Until death they remain burdened with endless cares. They think that mundane enjoyment is the goal of life. Bound by hundreds of wishes and overcome with desire and anger, they endeavour to amass wealth unlawfully to fulfill their desires.

Text:
इदमद्य मया लब्धमिदं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम्  ।
इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् ॥१३॥
Transcription:
idam adya mayā labdham idaṁ prāpsye manoratham
idam astīdam api me bhaviṣyati punar dhanam [13]
Translation: The ungodly persons say, “Today I got what I wanted, and tomorrow I will also get what I want. All this is my wealth, and I will have even more in the future.”

Text:
असौ मया हतः शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि ।
ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी ॥१४॥
Transcription:
asau mayā hataḥ śatrur haniṣye chāparān api
īśvaro ’ham ahaṁ bhogī siddho ’haṁ balavān sukhī [14]
Translation: “I have destroyed one enemy, and I will destroy others. I am successful, powerful, and happy.”

Text:
आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया ।
यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः ॥१५॥
Transcription:
āḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛśo mayā
yakṣye dāsyāmi modiṣya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ [15]
Translation: “I am wealthy and aristocratic. Who can compare with me? I shall perform sacrifice, give charity, and enjoy myself.” Thus they are deluded by ignorance.

Text:
अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृताः ।
प्रसक्ताः कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ ॥१६॥
Transcription:
aneka-chitta-vibhrāntā moha-jāla-samāvṛtāḥ
prasaktāḥ kāma-bhogeṣu patanti narake ’śuchau [16]
Translation: Their minds full of vain thoughts, caught in the net of illusion and engrossed in mundane enjoyment, such ungodly persons fall to a foul hell.

Text:
आत्मसम्भाविताः स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विताः ।
यजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्व्वकम् ॥१७॥
Transcription:
ātma-sambhāvitāḥ stabdhā dhana-māna-madānvitāḥ
yajante nāma-yajñais te dambhenāvidhi-pūrvakam [17]
Translation: Conceited, obstinate, and intoxicated by wealth and grandeur, ignoring the scriptural injunctions, these demons make a hypocritical show of sacrifices.

Text:
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधञ्च संश्रिताः ।
मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः ॥१८॥
Transcription:
ahaṅkāraṁ balaṁ darpaṁ kāmaṁ krodhañ cha saṁśritāḥ
mām ātma-para-deheṣu pradviṣanto ’bhyasūyakāḥ [18]
Translation: Given to egoism, pomp and power, lust and anger, they are resentful of Me, who am situated in their own and others’ bodies as the Supersoul, and they ascribe faults to the good qualities of the saintly persons.

Text:
तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् ।
क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु ॥१९॥
Transcription:
tān ahaṁ dviṣataḥ krūrān saṁsāreṣu narādhamān
kṣipāmy ajasram aśubhān āsurīṣv eva yoniṣu [19]
Translation: I continually cast these resentful, cruel, inauspicious, and decadent persons into the demoniac species, in the cycle of birth and death.

Text:
आसुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि ।
मामाप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ॥२०॥
Transcription:
āsurīṁ yonim āpannā mūḍhā janmani janmani
mām aprāpyaiva kaunteya tato yānty adhamāṁ gatim [20]
Translation: O Kaunteya, repeatedly taking birth in the demoniac species, these deluded persons cannot come to Me, and they descend to lower and lower planes.

Text:
त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः ।
कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् ॥२१॥
Transcription:
tri-vidhaṁ narakasyedaṁ dvāraṁ nāśanam ātmanaḥ
kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṁ tyajet [21]
Translation: Lust, anger, and greed are the three doors of hell leading to self-destruction, so they must be abandoned.

Text:
एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः ।
आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥२२॥
Transcription:
etair vimuktaḥ kaunteya tamo-dvārais tribhir naraḥ
ācharaty ātmanaḥ śreyas tato yāti parāṁ gatim [22]
Translation: O Kaunteya, one who is liberated from these three doors of darkness strives for the benefit of the soul, by which he attains the supreme destination.

Text:
यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्त्तते कामचारतः ।
न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् ॥२३॥
Transcription:
yaḥ śāstra-vidhim utsṛjya vartate kāma-chārataḥ
na sa siddhim avāpnoti na sukhaṁ na parāṁ gatim [23]
Translation: One who acts according to his own desires, disregarding the scriptural injunctions, cannot attain perfection, happiness, or the supreme destination.

Text:
तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्य्याकार्य्यव्यवस्थितौ ।
ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म्म कर्त्तुमिहार्हसि ॥२४॥
Transcription:
tasmāch chhāstraṁ pramāṇaṁ te kāryākārya-vyavasthitau
jñātvā śāstra-vidhānoktaṁ karma kartum ihārhasi [24]
Translation: Therefore, the scriptural injunctions for duty and non-duty are your authority. Knowing your duty in this world according to the scriptures (to act only for the Lord’s pleasure), you should act accordingly.

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
ये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः ।
तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः ॥१॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
ye śāstra-vidhim utsṛjya yajante śraddhayānvitāḥ
teṣāṁ niṣṭhā tu kā kṛṣṇa sattvam āho rajas tamaḥ [1]
Translation: Arjuna inquired: O Kṛṣṇa, what is the position of those who worship with faith, but ignore the injunctions of the scriptures? Is their faith in the mode of goodness, passion, or ignorance?

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
त्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा ।
सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां शृणु ॥२॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
tri-vidhā bhavati śraddhā dehināṁ sā svabhāva-jā
sāttvikī rājasī chaiva tāmasī cheti tāṁ śṛṇu [2]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: Faith is of three types, born of the embodied soul’s acquired nature, whether good, passionate, or ignorant. Now hear of this:

Text:
सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्व्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत ।
श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ॥३॥
Transcription:
sattvānurūpā sarvasya śraddhā bhavati bhārata
śraddhāmayo ’yaṁ puruṣo yo yach chhraddhaḥ sa eva saḥ [3]
Translation: O Bhārata, the faith of all living beings is governed by their consciousness. The living being possesses faith by constitution. He is that which his faith is.

Text:
यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः ।
प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः ॥४॥
Transcription:
yajante sāttvikā devān yakṣa-rakṣāṁsi rājasāḥ
pretān bhūta-gaṇāṁś chānye yajante tāmasā janāḥ [4]
Translation: Persons with faith of the nature of goodness worship the demigods, persons with faith of a passionate nature worship the Yakṣa demigods and Rākṣasa demons, while those with faith of the nature of ignorance worship the ghosts and spirits.

Text:
अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः ।
दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः ॥५॥
कर्शयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः ।
मां चैवान्तः शरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् ॥६॥
Transcription:
aśāstra-vihitaṁ ghoraṁ tapyante ye tapo janāḥ
dambhāhaṅkāra-saṁyuktāḥ kāma-rāga-balānvitāḥ [5]
karśayantaḥ śarīra-sthaṁ bhūta-grāmam achetasaḥ
māñ chaivāntaḥ śarīra-sthaṁ tān viddhy āsura-niśchayān [6]
Translation: Full of pride and egoism, motivated by ambition, attachment, and power, ignorant persons perform severe austerities unsanctioned by the scriptures and torture the body’s natural elements and the soul within. Know them to be of demoniacal faith.

Text:
आहारस्त्वपि सर्व्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः ।
यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं शृणु ॥७॥
Transcription:
āhāras tv api sarvasya tri-vidho bhavati priyaḥ
yajñas tapas tathā dānaṁ teṣāṁ bhedam imaṁ śṛṇu [7]
Translation: According to the three modes of material nature, there are also three kinds of food preference. In the same way, there are three kinds of sacrifice, three kinds of austerity, and three kinds of charity. Now hear of these:

Text:
आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः ।
रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः ॥८॥
Transcription:
āyuḥ-sattva-balārogya-sukha-prīti-vivardhanāḥ
rasyāḥ snigdhāḥ sthirā hṛdyā āhārāḥ sāttvika-priyāḥ [8]
Translation: Foods that increase longevity, vitality, strength, health, happiness, and loving kindness, and which are tasty, succulent, wholesome, and pleasant, are dear to persons of the nature of goodness.

Text:
कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरुक्षविदाहिनः ।
आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः ॥९॥
Transcription:
kaṭv-amla-lavaṇāty-uṣṇa-tīkṣṇa-rukṣa-vidāhinaḥ
āhārā rājasasyeṣṭā duḥkha-śokāmaya-pradāḥ [9]
Translation: Foods dear to persons of passionate nature are too bitter (nimba, etc.), too sour, too salty, too hot, too pungent (chilli pepper, etc.), too dry (dry-roasted chickpeas, etc.), and too heating (mustard seed, etc.). Such foods cause pain, sorrow, and illness.

Text:
यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्य्युषितञ्च यत् ।
उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ॥१०॥
Transcription:
yāta-yāmaṁ gata-rasaṁ pūti paryuṣitañ cha yat
uchchhiṣṭam api chāmedhyaṁ bhojanaṁ tāmasa-priyam [10]
Translation: Dear to the ignorant are foods that are stale, tasteless, foul-smelling or were cooked the day before, remnants (other than that of worshippable persons), and impure foods (such as meat, wine, and onions).

Text:
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदिष्टो य इज्यते ।
यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः ॥११॥
Transcription:
aphalākāṅkṣibhir yajño vidhi-diṣṭo ya ijyate
yaṣṭavyam eveti manaḥ samādhāya sa sāttvikaḥ [11]
Translation: That sacrifice which is in accordance with scriptural injunctions and faithfully performed by a person free from fruitive desire, is sacrifice of the nature of goodness.

Text:
अभिसन्धाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत् ।
इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम् ॥१२॥
Transcription:
abhisandhāya tu phalaṁ dambhārtham api chaiva yat
ijyate bharata-śreṣṭha taṁ yajñaṁ viddhi rājasam [12]
Translation: But, O Bhārat, know that sacrifice which is performed in fruitive expectation and as a display of pomp and grandeur, is sacrifice of the nature of passion.

Text:
विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम् ।
श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते ॥१३॥
Transcription:
vidhi-hīnam asṛṣṭānnaṁ mantra-hīnam adakṣiṇam
śraddhā-virahitaṁ yajñaṁ tāmasaṁ parichakṣate [13]
Translation: And that sacrifice which ignores the injunctions of the scriptures, which is performed without the distribution of foodstuffs, without uttering the appropriate mantras, without offerings to the priests, and without faith, is known as sacrifice in ignorance.

Text:
देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्ज्जवम् ।
ब्रह्मचर्य्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ॥१४॥
Transcription:
deva-dvija-guru-prājña-pūjanaṁ śaucham ārjavam
brahmacharyam ahiṁsā cha śārīraṁ tapa uchyate [14]
Translation: Worship of the deity, the brāhmaṇ, the spiritual master, and the enlightened soul, purity, simplicity, celibacy, and nonviolence—these all constitute austerity of the body.

Text:
अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितञ्च यत् ।
स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ॥१५॥
Transcription:
anudvega-karaṁ vākyaṁ satyaṁ priya-hitañ cha yat
svādhyāyābhyasanaṁ chaiva vāṅmayaṁ tapa uchyate [15]
Translation: Speech which does not disturb others, which is truthful, pleasing yet beneficial, as well as regular recitation of the Vedas—all these are known as verbal austerity.

Text:
मनः प्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः ।
भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते ॥१६॥
Transcription:
manaḥ prasādaḥ saumyatvaṁ maunam ātma-vinigrahaḥ
bhāva-saṁśuddhir ity etat tapo mānasam uchyate [16]
Translation: Self-satisfaction, benevolence, stability, mental control, and pure-heartedness are all known as mental austerity.

Text:
श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्त्रिविधं नरैः ।
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते ॥१७॥
Transcription:
śraddhayā parayā taptaṁ tapas tat tri-vidhaṁ naraiḥ
aphalākāṅkṣibhir yuktaiḥ sāttvikaṁ parichakṣate [17]
Translation: When performed by a desireless, devout soul with deep faith in the Supreme Lord, this threefold austerity of thought, word, and deed is of the nature of goodness.

Text:
सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत् ।
क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम् ॥१८॥
Transcription:
satkāra-māna-pūjārthaṁ tapo dambhena chaiva yat
kriyate tad iha proktaṁ rājasaṁ chalam adhruvam [18]
Translation: That unenduring, transitory austerity which is proudly performed for the sake of gain, worship, name, and fame, is known as austerity of the nature of passion.

Text:
मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पिडया क्रियते तपः ।
परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥१९॥
Transcription:
mūḍha-grāheṇātmano yat pīḍayā kriyate tapaḥ
parasyotsādanārthaṁ vā tat tāmasam udāhṛtam [19]
Translation: That austerity which is performed with dubious intent, causing self-torture, or meant to harm others, is known as austerity of the nature of ignorance.

Text:
दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे ।
देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ॥२०॥
Transcription:
dātavyam iti yad dānaṁ dīyate ’nupakāriṇe
deśe kāle cha pātre cha tad dānaṁ sāttvikaṁ smṛtam [20]
Translation: The gift which is given without expectation of return, with the conviction that it ought to be given, at an appropriate place and time, and to an appropriate recipient, is considered to be of the nature of goodness.

Text:
यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः ।
दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥२१॥
Transcription:
yat tu praty-upakārārthaṁ phalam uddiśya vā punaḥ
dīyate cha parikliṣṭaṁ tad dānaṁ rājasaṁ smṛtam [21]
Translation: Otherwise, the gift begrudgingly given in expectation of gaining something in return, or with a desire for a reward (such as the attainment of heaven), is considered to be of the nature of passion.

Text:
अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते ।
असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥२२॥
Transcription:
adeśa-kāle yad dānam apātrebhyaś cha dīyate
asatkṛtam avajñātaṁ tat tāmasam udāhṛtam [22]
Translation: The gift given disrespectfully and scornfully to an unworthy recipient at an improper place and time is considered to be of the nature of ignorance.

Text:
ओंत्सदिति निर्द्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः ।
ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा ॥२३॥
Transcription:
oṁ-tat-sad iti nirdeśo brahmaṇas tri-vidhaḥ smṛtaḥ
brāhmaṇās tena vedāś cha yajñāś cha vihitāḥ purā [23]
Translation: The scriptures say that the wordsindicate Brahma, the Supreme Spirit. At the time of the universal manifestation, the brāhmaṇs, the Vedas, and sacrifices were manifested by these three words.

Text:
तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः ।
प्रवर्त्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥२४॥
Transcription:
tasmād om ity udāhṛtya yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ
pravartante vidhānoktāḥ satataṁ brahma-vādinām [24]
Translation: Thus the followers of the Vedas always utter the syllablerepresenting Brahma, the Absolute, to initiate their prescribed duties of sacrifice, charity, and austerity.

Text:
तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः ।
दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभिः ॥२५॥
Transcription:
tad ity anabhisandhāya phalaṁ yajña-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ
dāna-kriyāś cha vividhāḥ kriyante mokṣa-kāṅkṣibhiḥ [25]
Translation: Persons aspiring for liberation utter the word, representing Brahma, the Absolute, to invoke the performance of various acts of sacrifice, charity, and austerity without motivation for the results.

Text:
सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते ।
प्रशस्ते कर्म्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते ॥२६॥
Transcription:
sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve cha sad ity etat prayujyate
praśaste karmaṇi tathā sach chhabdaḥ pārtha yujyate [26]
Translation: O Pārtha, the word, representing Brahma, the Absolute, indicates the truth and persons dedicated to the truth. Therefore, the wordis uttered to invoke the performance of auspicious activities.

Text:
यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते ।
कर्म्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते ॥२७॥
Transcription:
yajñe tapasi dāne cha sthitiḥ sad iti chochyate
karma chaiva tad-arthīyaṁ sad ity evābhidhīyate [27]
Translation: The wordis said to indicate the eternality of sacrifice, austerity, and charity. And action for the satisfaction of the Supreme Lord is called—truth.

Text:
अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतञ्च यत् ।
असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह ॥२८॥
Transcription:
aśraddhayā hutaṁ dattaṁ tapas taptaṁ kṛtañ cha yat
asad ity uchyate pārtha na cha tat pretya no iha [28]
Translation: O Pārtha, sacrifice, charity, and austerity or any action performed without faith in the Supreme is known as asat, or untruth. Such works cannot bring an auspicious result, either in this world or the next.

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
सन्न्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम् ।
त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिसूदन ॥१॥
Transcription:
arjuna uvācha
sannyāsasya mahā-bāho tattvam ichchhāmi veditum
tyāgasya cha hṛṣīkeśa pṛthak keśinisūdana [1]
Translation: Arjuna said: O almighty Lord, slayer of the demon Keśi, I wish to understand the subject of renunciation (sannyās) and detachment (tyāga), separately.

Text:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
काम्यानां कर्म्मणां न्यासं सन्न्यासं कवयो विदुः ।
सर्व्वकर्म्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः ॥२॥
Transcription:
śrī-bhagavān uvācha
kāmyānāṁ karmaṇāṁ nyāsaṁ sannyāsaṁ kavayo viduḥ
sarva-karma-phala-tyāgaṁ prāhus tyāgaṁ vichakṣaṇāḥ [2]
Translation: The Supreme Lord said: Learned, enlightened persons know the giving up of fruitive action as renunciation, and the giving up of the fruits of all action as detachment.

Text:
त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः ।
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे ॥३॥
Transcription:
tyājyaṁ doṣavad ity eke karma prāhur manīṣiṇaḥ
yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-karma na tyājyam iti chāpare [3]
Translation: Some philosophers hold that action should be renounced as imperfect. Others hold that action of the nature of sacrifice, charity, and austerity should never be given up.

Text:
निश्चयं शृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम ।
त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः सम्प्रकीर्त्तितः ॥४॥
Transcription:
niśchayaṁ śṛṇu me tatra tyāge bharata-sattama
tyāgo hi puruṣa-vyāghra tri-vidhaḥ samprakīrtitaḥ [4]
Translation: O best of the Bhāratas, hear My perfect conclusion concerning detachment, the quality of renunciation, since, O best of men, it has been clearly established that the quality of renunciation is of three types.

Text:
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म्म न त्याज्यं कार्य्यमेव तत् ।
यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम् ॥५॥
Transcription:
yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-karma na tyājyaṁ kāryam eva tat
yajño dānaṁ tapaś chaiva pāvanāni manīṣiṇām [5]
Translation: Actions of the nature of sacrifice, charity, and austerity should never be abandoned. They must be performed for they are the purifiers of the wise.

Text:
एतान्यपि तु कर्म्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च ।
कर्त्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्चितं मतमुत्तमम् ॥६॥
Transcription:
etāny api tu karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā phalāni cha
kartavyānīti me pārtha niśchitaṁ matam uttamam [6]
Translation: But even these actions must be performed abandoning attachment and fruitive desire. O Pārtha, know this to be My perfect, supreme conclusion.

Text:
नियतस्य तु सन्न्यासः कर्म्मणो नोपपद्यते ।
मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्त्तितः ॥७॥
Transcription:
niyatasya tu sannyāsaḥ karmaṇo nopapadyate
mohāt tasya parityāgas tāmasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ [7]
Translation: Renunciation of one’s obligatory duties is wrong. The delusion of giving up these duties is called false renunciation, or renunciation of the nature of ignorance.

Text:
दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत् ।
स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत् ॥८॥
Transcription:
duḥkham ity eva yat karma kāya-kleśa-bhayāt tyajet
sa kṛtvā rājasaṁ tyāgaṁ naiva tyāga-phalaṁ labhet [8]
Translation: One who abandons his obligatory duties out of fear of physical discomfort, considering them troublesome, performs renunciation of the nature of passion. Thus, he does not attain the fruit of true detachment.

Text:
कार्य्यमित्येव यत्कर्म्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्ज्जुन ।
सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलञ्चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः ॥९॥
Transcription:
kāryam ity eva yat karma niyataṁ kriyate ’rjuna
saṅgaṁ tyaktvā phalañ chaiva sa tyāgaḥ sāttviko mataḥ [9]
Translation: O Arjuna, performance of obligatory works as a matter of duty while abandoning attachment and fruitive desire, is renunciation of the nature of goodness. This is My opinion.

Text:
न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म्म कुशले नानुषज्जते ।
त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः ॥१०॥
Transcription:
na dveṣṭy akuśalaṁ karma kuśale nānuṣajjate
tyāgī sattva-samāviṣṭo medhāvī chhinna-saṁśayaḥ [10]
Translation: The wise renunciate who is absorbed in the nature of goodness, and who has slashed all doubts, neither resents disagreeable duties nor is attached to agreeable works.

Text:
न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्म्माण्यशेषतः ।
यस्तु कर्म्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते ॥११॥
Transcription:
na hi deha-bhṛtā śakyaṁ tyaktuṁ karmāṇy aśeṣataḥ
yas tu karma-phala-tyāgī sa tyāgīty abhidhīyate [11]
Translation: It is not possible for the embodied soul to fully renounce actions. However, one who renounces the fruits of his actions is a true renunciate.

Text:
अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रञ्च त्रिविधं कर्म्मणः फलम् ।
भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु सन्न्यासिनां क्वचित् ॥१२॥
Transcription:
aniṣṭam iṣṭaṁ miśrañ cha tri-vidhaṁ karmaṇaḥ phalam
bhavaty atyāgināṁ pretya na tu sannyāsināṁ kvachit [12]
Translation: After death, the three kinds of fruits of actions—good, bad, and mixed—accrue to those who have worldly desires, but never to those who are renounced.

Text:
पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे ।
साङ्ख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्व्वकर्म्मणाम् ॥१३॥
Transcription:
pañchaitāni mahā-bāho kāraṇāni nibodha me
sāṅkhye kṛtānte proktāni siddhaye sarva-karmaṇām [13]
Translation: O mighty hero, know from Me of the five causes of the accomplishment of all actions as described in the conclusive teachings of the scripture known as Sāṅkhya or Vedānta:

Text:
अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्त्ता करणञ्च पृथग्विधम् ।
विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवञ्चैवात्र पञ्चमम् ॥१४॥
Transcription:
adhiṣṭhānaṁ tathā kartā karaṇañ cha pṛthag-vidham
vividhāś cha pṛthak cheṣṭā daivañ chaivātra pañchamam [14]
Translation: (All actions are accomplished by these five causes:) The body, the performer (ego, a knot of spirit and matter), the instrument (the senses), the various endeavours, and the Divine (the Supersoul).

Text:
शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म्म प्रारभते नरः ।
न्यायं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः ॥१५॥
Transcription:
śarīra-vāṅmanobhir yat karma prārabhate naraḥ
nyāyaṁ vā viparītaṁ vā pañchaite tasya hetavaḥ [15]
Translation: Whether lawful of unlawful, whatever action a man performs with his body, mind, or speech, is caused by these five elements.

Text:
तत्रैवं सति कर्त्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः ।
पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्म्मतिः ॥१६॥
Transcription:
tatraivaṁ sati kartāram ātmānaṁ kevalaṁ tu yaḥ
paśyaty akṛta-buddhitvān na sa paśyati durmatiḥ [16]
Translation: But an ignorant person who sees only himself as the doer does not actually see, due to his impure intelligence.

Text:
यस्य नाहङ्कृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते ।
हत्वापि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते ॥१७॥
Transcription:
yasya nāhaṅkṛto bhāvo buddhir yasya na lipyate
hatvāpi sa imā̐l lokān na hanti na nibadhyate [17]
Translation: He who is free from egoism (aversion to the Absolute), and whose intelligence is not implicated in worldly action—even if he kills the entire world, he neither kills nor is he bound by action.

Text:
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्म्मचोदना ।
करणं कर्म्म कर्त्तेति त्रिविधः कर्म्मसन्ग्रहः ॥१८॥
Transcription:
jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ parijñātā tri-vidhā karma-chodanā
karaṇaṁ karma karteti tri-vidhaḥ karma-saṅgrahaḥ [18]
Translation: These three are the driving force of action: knowledge, the knowable, and the knower. These three are the constitution of action—the instrument, the action, and the performer.

Text:
ज्ञानं कर्म्म च कर्त्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः ।
प्रोच्यते गुणसङ्ख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि ॥१९॥
Transcription:
jñānaṁ karma cha kartā cha tridhaiva guṇa-bhedataḥ
prochyate guṇa-saṅkhyāne yathāvach chhṛṇu tāny api [19]
Translation: In the sāṅkhya scripture, knowledge, action, and the performer have each been classified according to the three modes of material nature. Now hear of these from Me:

Text:
सर्व्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते ।
अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम् ॥२०॥
Transcription:
sarva-bhūteṣu yenaikaṁ bhāvam avyayam īkṣate
avibhaktaṁ vibhakteṣu taj jñānaṁ viddhi sāttvikam [20]
Translation: That knowledge by which the one imperishable and undivided principle (My superior divine potency) is seen to be present in all the diverse living beings is to be known as knowledge of the nature of goodness.

Text:
पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान् ।
वेत्ति सर्व्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम् ॥२१॥
Transcription:
pṛthaktvena tu yaj jñānaṁ nānā-bhāvān-pṛthag-vidhān
vetti sarveṣu bhūteṣu taj jñānaṁ viddhi rājasam [21]
Translation: That knowledge in which one experiences in the plane of living beings many separate (independent) entities engaged in many separate (clashing) pursuits is to be known as knowledge of the nature of passion.

Text:
यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्य्ये सक्तमहैतुकम् ।
अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पञ्च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥२२॥
Transcription:
yat tu kṛtsnavad ekasmin kārye saktam ahaitukam
atattvārthavad alpañ cha tat tāmasam udāhṛtam [22]
Translation: And that ideology which is stubbornly attached to the peripheral only, considering it to be all-in-all, without spiritual or scriptural conception, without seeking the truth, and based on trivialities alone, is said to be knowledge of the (animalistic) nature of ignorance.

Text:
नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम्
अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते ॥२३॥
Transcription:
niyataṁ saṅga-rahitam arāga-dveṣataḥ kṛtam
aphala-prepsunā karma yat tat sāttvikam uchyate [23]
Translation: That obligatory action which is faithfully executed without attachment by a person who does not seek its rewards and who is unbiased by likes and dislikes, is said to be action of the nature of goodness.

Text:
यत्तु कामेप्सुना कर्म्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः ।
क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम् ॥२४॥
Transcription:
yat tu kāmepsunā karma sāhaṅkāreṇa vā punaḥ
kriyate bahulāyāsaṁ tad rājasam udāhṛtam [24]
Translation: And that action which is performed with great endeavour by an egotistic person desiring its fruits, is said to be action of the nature of passion.

Text:
अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम् ।
मोहादारभ्यते कर्म्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते ॥२५॥
Transcription:
anubandhaṁ kṣayaṁ hiṁsām anapekṣya cha pauruṣam
mohād ārabhyate karma yat tat tāmasam uchyate [25]
Translation: And that work which is undertaken out of delusion, without consideration for its consequences, its loss, harm to others, and one’s capability of performing it, is called action of the nature of ignorance.

Text:
मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः ।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्व्विकारः कर्त्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते ॥२६॥
Transcription:
mukta-saṅgo ’nahaṁvādī dhṛty-utsāha-samanvitaḥ
siddhy-asiddhyor nirvikāraḥ kartā sāttvika uchyate [26]
Translation: The worker who is unattached, without ego, patient, enthusiastic, and unaffected in success or failure, is known as a worker of the nature of goodness.

Text:
रागी कर्म्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः ।
हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्त्ता राजसः परिकीर्त्तितः ॥२७॥
Transcription:
rāgī karma-phala-prepsur lubdho hiṁsātmako ’śuchiḥ
harṣa-śokānvitaḥ kartā rājasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ [27]
Translation: The worker who is attached, desirous of the fruits of action, covetous, cruel, engaged in abominable practices not sanctioned by the scriptures, and always subject to joy and sorrow, is known as a worker of the nature of passion.

Text:
अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिकोऽलसः ।
विषादी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्त्ता तामस उच्यते ॥२८॥
Transcription:
ayuktaḥ prākṛtaḥ stabdhaḥ śaṭho naiṣkṛtiko ’lasaḥ
viṣādī dīrgha-sūtrī cha kartā tāmasa uchyate [28]
Translation: The worker who is uncontrolled, vulgar, arrogant, deceitful, offensive, lazy, despondent, and a procrastinator is known as a worker of the nature of ignorance.

Text:
बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं शृणु ।
प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय ॥२९॥
Transcription:
buddher bhedaṁ dhṛteś chaiva guṇatas tri-vidhaṁ śṛṇu
prochyamānam aśeṣeṇa pṛthaktvena dhanañjaya [29]
Translation: O Dhanañjaya, now listen attentively. I shall clearly describe the three types of intelligence and determination, according to the modes of nature:

Text:
प्रवृत्तिञ्च निवृत्तिञ्च कार्य्याकार्य्ये भयाभये ।
बन्धं मोक्षञ्च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥३०॥
Transcription:
pravṛttiñ cha nivṛttiñ cha kāryākārye bhayābhaye
bandhaṁ mokṣañ cha yā vetti buddhiḥ sā pārtha sāttvikī [30]
Translation: O Pārtha, intelligence of the nature of goodness is that which can distinguish (virtuous) engagement and abstinence (from vice), duty and non-duty, danger and safety, and bondage and liberation.

Text:
यया धर्म्ममधर्म्मञ्च कार्य्यञ्चाकार्य्यमेव च ।
अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥३१॥
Transcription:
yayā dharmam adharmañ cha kāryañ chākāryam eva cha
ayathāvat prajānāti buddhiḥ sā pārtha rājasī [31]
Translation: O Pārtha, intelligence of the nature of passion is that which causes an erroneous perception of religion, irreligion, duty, and non-duty.

Text:
अधर्म्मं धर्म्ममिति या मन्यते तमसावृता ।
सर्व्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥३२॥
Transcription:
adharmaṁ dharmam iti yā manyate tamasāvṛtā
sarvārthān viparītāṁś cha buddhiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī [32]
Translation: Intelligence of the nature of ignorance is covered by illusion, whereby vice is considered virtue, and everything is taken to be the opposite of the reality.

Text:
धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः ।
योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥३३॥
Transcription:
dhṛtyā yayā dhārayate manaḥ-prāṇendriya-kriyāḥ
yogenāvyabhichāriṇyā dhṛtiḥ sā pārtha sāttvikī [33]
Translation: O Pārtha, determination of the nature of goodness is that determination which unfailingly and with single-minded concentration controls the functions of the mind, the life-airs, and the senses.

Text:
यया तु धर्म्मकामार्थान्धृत्या धारयतेऽर्ज्जुन ।
प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥३४॥
Transcription:
yayā tu dharma-kāmārthān dhṛtyā dhārayate ’rjuna
prasaṅgena phalākāṅkṣī dhṛtiḥ sā pārtha rājasī [34]
Translation: O Pārtha, O Arjuna, determination of the nature of passion is that fruitive, attached determination which adheres to religiosity for the purpose of amassing wealth to fulfill mundane desires.

Text:
यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च ।
न विमुञ्चति दुर्म्मेधा धृतिः सा तामसी मता ॥३५॥
Transcription:
yayā svapnaṁ bhayaṁ śokaṁ viṣādaṁ madam eva cha
na vimuñchati durmedhā dhṛtiḥ sā tāmasī matā [35]
Translation: Determination of the nature of ignorance is that by which a foolish person does not give up sleep, fear, sorrow, depression, and arrogance.

Text:
सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं शृणु मे भरतर्षभ ।
अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तञ्च निगच्छति ॥३६।
यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम् ।
तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम् ॥३७॥
Transcription:
sukhaṁ tv idānīṁ tri-vidhaṁ śṛṇu me bharatarṣabha
abhyāsād ramate yatra duḥkhāntañ cha nigachchhati [36]
yat tad agre viṣam iva pariṇāme ’mṛtopamam
tat sukhaṁ sāttvikaṁ proktam ātma-buddhi-prasāda-jam [37]
Translation: O Bharatarṣabha, now hear from Me about the three kinds of happiness: That state in which one is joyful through his practices while attaining the cessation of all unhappiness, and which in the beginning is like poison but ultimately like nectar, and which is born of the purity of self-realisation—that happiness is said to be of the nature of goodness.

Text:
विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम् ।
परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥३८।
Transcription:
viṣayendriya-saṁyogād yat tad agre ’mṛtopamam
pariṇāme viṣam iva tat sukhaṁ rājasaṁ smṛtam [38]
Translation: That happiness which is born of the contact of the senses with their objects, and which is like nectar in the beginning but poison in the end, is said to be happiness of the nature of passion.

Text:
यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः ।
निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥३९॥
Transcription:
yad agre chānubandhe cha sukhaṁ mohanam ātmanaḥ
nidrālasya-pramādotthaṁ tat tāmasam udāhṛtam [39]
Translation: That happiness which is self-deception from beginning to end, and which arises from sleep, laziness, and illusion, is called happiness of the nature of ignorance.

Text:
न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः ।
सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्त्रिभिर्गुणैः ॥४०॥
Transcription:
na tad asti pṛthivyāṁ vā divi deveṣu vā punaḥ
sattvaṁ prakṛti-jair muktaṁ yad ebhiḥ syāt tribhir guṇaiḥ [40]
Translation: Amongst all species on earth including the humans or amongst even the celestial gods in the heavenly plane, there is no entity that can be free from these three modes which are born of material nature.

Text:
ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणाञ्च परन्तप ।
कर्म्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुनैः ॥४१॥
Transcription:
brāhmaṇa-kṣatriya-viśāṁ śūdrāṇāñ cha parantapa
karmāṇi pravibhaktāni svabhāva-prabhavair gunaiḥ [41]
Translation: O Parantapa, according to their natures, the duties of the brāhmaṇs, kṣatriyas, vaiśyas, and śūdras are appropriately classified.

Text:
शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्ज्जवमेव च ।
ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म्म स्वभावजम् ॥४२॥
Transcription:
śamo damas tapaḥ śauchaṁ kṣāntir ārjavam eva cha
jñānaṁ vijñānam āstikyaṁ brahma-karma svabhāva-jam [42]
Translation: Internal and external sense-control, austerity, purity, forbearance, straightforwardness, knowledge, realisation, and theistic mentality—these are the natural duties of the brāhmaṇs.

Text:
शौर्य्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम् ।
दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म्म स्वभावजम् ॥४३॥
Transcription:
śauryaṁ tejo dhṛtir dākṣyaṁ yuddhe chāpy apalāyanam
dānam īśvara-bhāvaś cha kṣātraṁ karma svabhāva-jam [43]
Translation: Prowess, valour, endurance, dexterity, never retreating in battle, generosity, and regality—these are the characteristic duties of the kṣatriyas.

Text:
कृषिगोरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म्म स्वभावजम् ।
परिचर्य्यात्मकं कर्म्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम् ॥४४॥
Transcription:
kṛṣi-gorakṣya-vāṇijyaṁ vaiśya-karma svabhāva-jam
paricharyātmakaṁ karma śūdrasyāpi svabhāva-jam [44]
Translation: Agriculture, cow protection, and trade are the natural work of the vaiśyas; and natural to the śūdras is service to the brāhmaṇs, kṣatriyas, and vaiśyas (assisting them in their various works).

Text:
स्वे स्वे कर्म्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः ।
स्वकर्म्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ॥४५॥
Transcription:
sve sve karmaṇy abhirataḥ saṁsiddhiṁ labhate naraḥ
svakarma-nirataḥ siddhiṁ yathā vindati tach chhṛṇu [45]
Translation: One who devotes himself to his own duty attains perfection in self-knowledge. Now hear from Me how a person attains perfection through devotion to his own duty:

Text:
यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्व्वमिदं ततम् ।
स्वकर्म्मणा तमभ्यर्च्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः ॥४६॥
Transcription:
yataḥ pravṛttir bhūtānāṁ yena sarvam idaṁ tatam
svakarmaṇā tam abhyarchya siddhiṁ vindati mānavaḥ [46]
Translation: A man achieves perfection by worshipping, through his prescribed duties, the Supreme Lord from whom all beings originate, by whom all beings are engaged, and who (individually and collectively) pervades the entire universe.

Text:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्म्मो विगुणः परधर्म्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् ।
स्वभावनियतं कर्म्म कुर्व्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥४७॥
Transcription:
śreyān svadharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt svanuṣṭhitāt
svabhāva-niyataṁ karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣam [47]
Translation: One’s own duty, though imperfect, is better than another’s executed well. Sin is never incurred by a man fullfilling the duty prescribed according to his nature.

Text:
सहजं कर्म्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत् ।
सर्व्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः ॥४८॥
Transcription:
saha-jaṁ karma kaunteya sa-doṣam api na tyajet
sarvārambhā hi doṣeṇa dhūmenāgnir ivāvṛtāḥ [48]
Translation: O Kaunteya, even though it may be imperfect, one should not give up the duty prescribed according to his nature. Verily, all action (karma) is (generally) covered by imperfection, as fire is covered by smoke.

Text:
असक्तबुद्धिः सर्व्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः।
नैष्कर्म्म्यसिद्धिं परमां सन्न्यासेनाधिगच्छति ॥४९॥
Transcription:
asakta-buddhiḥ sarvatra jitātmā vigata-spṛhaḥ
naiṣkarmya-siddhiṁ paramāṁ sannyāsenādhigachchhati [49]
Translation: One who has the wisdom of detachment from all mundane objects, who is self-controlled, and whose desires have gone—such a person attains the perfection of transcending all duties in the world by renouncing the fruits of his actions.

Text:
सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे ।
समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा ॥५०॥
Transcription:
siddhiṁ prāpto yathā brahma tathāpnoti nibodha me
samāsenaiva kaunteya niṣṭhā jñānasya yā parā [50]
Translation: O Kaunteya, now know from Me in brief how the person who has attained transcendence of all duties reaches the transcendental plane of the Absolute, the ultimate state of knowledge (spiritual awakening):

Text:
बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्यात्मानं नियम्य च ।
शब्दादीन्विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च ॥५१॥
विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः ।
ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः ॥५२॥
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम् ।
विमुच्य निर्म्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥५३॥
Transcription:
buddhyā viśuddhayā yukto dhṛtyātmānaṁ niyamya cha
śabdādīn viṣayāṁs tyaktvā rāga-dveṣau vyudasya cha [51]
vivikta-sevī laghv-āśī yata-vāk-kāya-mānasaḥ
dhyāna-yoga-paro nityaṁ vairāgyaṁ samupāśritaḥ [52]
ahaṅkāraṁ balaṁ darpaṁ kāmaṁ krodhaṁ parigraham
vimuchya nirmamaḥ śānto brahma-bhūyāya kalpate [53]
Translation: Endowed with intelligence of the nature of goodness, mentally controlled through determination of the nature of goodness, rejecting the sense objects of sound, touch, form, taste, and smell, abandoning attachment and aversion, free from the association of materialistic persons, a temperate eater, disciplining the body, mind, and speech, constantly absorbed in thought of the Supreme Lord, and fully detached from the mundane world; giving up ego, power, vanity, desire, anger, and possessiveness, not thinking of anything as ‘mine’; being at peace—such a person is qualified for spiritual realisation.

Text:
ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति ।
समः सर्व्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम् ॥५४॥
Transcription:
brahma-bhūtaḥ prasannātmā na śochati na kāṅkṣati
samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu mad-bhaktiṁ labhate parām [54]
Translation: The joyful-hearted, enlightened soul who has attained his divine nature neither sorrows nor desires. Seeing all beings equally, he comes to attain transcendental loving devotion to Me.

Text:
भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः ।
ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम् ॥५५॥
Transcription:
bhaktyā mām abhijānāti yāvān yaś chāsmi tattvataḥ
tato māṁ tattvato jñātvā viśate tad-anantaram [55]
Translation: Through devotion, he realises that I am the Lord of all potencies and the Sweet Absolute. Then, realising his divine relationship with Me, he enters the company of My intimate associates who are non-different from My very self.

Text:
सर्व्वकर्म्माण्यपि सदा कुर्व्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः ।
मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम् ॥५६॥
Transcription:
sarva-karmāṇy api sadā kurvāṇo mad-vyapāśrayaḥ
mat-prasādād avāpnoti śāśvataṁ padam avyayam [56]
Translation: Although ever active in all duties, those who have taken refuge in Me attain, by My grace, the eternal, indestructable plane (of service).

Text:
चेतसा सर्व्वकर्म्माणि मयि सन्न्यस्य मत्परः ।
बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव ॥५७॥
Transcription:
chetasā sarva-karmāṇi mayi sannyasya mat-paraḥ
buddhi-yogam upāśritya mach-chittaḥ satataṁ bhava [57]
Translation: Whole-heartedly offering your every action to Me, keeping Me as the supreme objective and fully dedicating your intelligence to Me (being unattached to worldly action), be ever devoted to Me.

Text:
मच्चित्तः सर्व्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि ।
अथ चेत्त्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि ॥५८॥
Transcription:
mach-chittaḥ sarva-durgāṇi mat-prasādāt tariṣyasi
atha chet tvam ahaṅkārān na śroṣyasi vinaṅkṣyasi [58]
Translation: Giving your heart to Me, you will be able to overcome all obstacles by My grace. But if out of pride you do not heed My words, you will perish.

Text:
यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे ।
मिथ्यैव व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति ॥५९॥
Transcription:
yad ahaṅkāram āśritya na yotsya iti manyase
mithyaiva vyavasāyas te prakṛtis tvāṁ niyokṣyati [59]
Translation: Out of pride you are thinking, “I shall not fight.” But your decision will be in vain, for your nature (as a kṣatriya) will compel you to fight.

Text:
स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्म्मणा ।
कर्त्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत् ॥६०॥
Transcription:
svabhāva-jena kaunteya nibaddhaḥ svena karmaṇā
kartuṁ nechchhasi yan mohāt kariṣyasy avaśo ’pi tat [60]
Translation: O Kaunteya, being bound by the duty born of your nature, that very action which out of delusion you are now avoiding will be inevitably executed by you.

Text:
ईश्वरः सर्व्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्ज्जुन तिष्ठति ।
भ्रामयन्सर्व्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ॥६१॥
Transcription:
īśvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ hṛd-deśe ’rjuna tiṣṭhati
bhrāmayan sarva-bhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā [61]
Translation: O Arjuna, the Supreme Lord is situated in the hearts of all beings, and by His deluding potency He causes them to revolve (through many existences) like puppets riding a carousel.

Text:
तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्व्वभावेन भारत ।
तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ॥६२॥
Transcription:
tam eva śaraṇaṁ gachchha sarva-bhāvena bhārata
tat prasādāt parāṁ śāntiṁ sthānaṁ prāpsyasi śāśvatam [62]
Translation: O Bhārat, wholeheartedly surrender to Him. By His grace you will attain the supreme peace and the eternal abode.

Text:
इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया ।
विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ॥६३॥
Transcription:
iti te jñānam ākhyātaṁ guhyād guhyataraṁ mayā
vimṛśyaitad aśeṣeṇa yathechchhasi tathā kuru [63]
Translation: I have now revealed more and more profound teachings to you. Duly consider this, and then do as you wish.

Text:
सर्व्वगुह्यतमं भूयः शृणु मे परमं वचः ।
इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम् ॥६४॥
Transcription:
sarva-guhyatamaṁ bhūyaḥ śṛṇu me paramaṁ vachaḥ
iṣṭo ’si me dṛḍham iti tato vakṣyāmi te hitam [64]
Translation: Now again hear My supreme teaching, the most hidden treasure of all. I tell you this for your benefit as you are most dear to Me.
Comment:
According to Śrīla Bhakti Vinod Ṭhākur (Vidvad-rañjan commentary, 9.1), the wisdom of self-realisation in the second and third chapters of Śrī Gītā are the profound (guhyam) teachings of Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa. The Lord’s descriptions of Himself as the Supreme in the seventh and eighth chapters are more profound (guhyataram), as such knowledge gives birth to devotion. In chapter nine, the most profound (guhyatamam) teachings begin as the Lord describes the symptoms of exclusive devotion (kevalā-bhakti).
Because His devotee Arjuna is very dear to Him, the Lord now repeats His secret teaching for Arjuna’s benefit. This time, however, He reveals His most hidden of all hidden treasures (sarva-guhyatamam), His most affectionate advice: “Arjuna, you are most dear to Me, so I will now divulge the whole truth to you.”

Text:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु ।
मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ॥६५॥
Transcription:
man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru
mām evaiṣyasi satyaṁ te pratijāne priyo ’si me [65]
Translation: Think of Me always, devote yourself to Me, worship Me, and bow to Me, and surely you will come to Me. I promise you this, because you are dear to Me.

Text:
सर्व्वधर्म्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज ।
अहं त्वां सर्व्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥६६॥
Transcription:
sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śuchaḥ [66]
Translation: Give up all kinds of religion and surrender to Me alone. I will liberate you from all sins. Do not despair.
Comment:
Here, the glory of the hidden purpose in the Bhagavad-gītā is sung (Gītā-gūḍhārtha-gauravam): “Give up all engagements and come to Me. You won’t have to repent, Arjuna, because I am everything to you, and you are everything to Me. This is the most hidden of all hidden truths. What more can I say? And you will find this in Vraja.”
There is a literary ornament in Sanskrit called dhvani, echo, by which a word ‘echoes’, suggests, an additional meaning. Here, such a hint is given. Mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ ‘vraja’*: “You may ‘go’ to ‘Vraja’, and there you will find the most hidden of all hidden truths (sarva-guhyatamam). The deepest secret of the inner loving heart has been fully revealed there: I am beyond all conceptions of religion, society, friends—everything. My position is above everything, and in the heart of the heart of everything. In the eternal land of Vraja, you will experience the whole conception of beauty. Dismiss all other engagements and prospects, and come to Me alone. Your inner hankering will be fulfilled beyond your expectations. You will find such dignity in Me that you will be beyond reaction and repentance.” This is the deepest meaning of the highest glory.
If one comes to this conception, everything else will be seen as sin (Ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi). Everything conceived of as duty or purity in this material world will be seen as sinful, and all conceptions of religiosity will be reduced to the level of sin. In the absolute plane, everything and everyone belongs wholly to Kṛṣṇa, and the slightest deviation from this ideal is no better than committing sin. Mere self-forgetfulness leads to impersonalism and culminates in deep slumber. But self-forgetfulness (sarva-dharmān parityajya) in Kṛṣṇa’s service (Mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja) is positive and living. It is the full, absolute plane of life.
The hidden treasure attracts our hearts. We have been deprived of our heart’s deepest interest. Though we have a natural claim to it, it has been hidden from us (śrutibhir vimṛgyām). In an unparalleled way, Śrī Gītā considers all these aspects and directs us to the correct meaning and conclusion of all the Upaniṣads. From this point, the Śrīmad Bhāgavatam begins.

*In the verse, vraja means ‘go’, and Vraja is also a name of Vṛndāvan.

Text:
इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन ।
न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ॥६७॥
Transcription:
idaṁ te nātapaskāya nābhaktāya kadāchana
na chāśuśrūṣave vāchyaṁ na cha māṁ yo ’bhyasūyati [67]
Translation: You should never disclose this hidden treasure to one devoid of self-sacrifice, to the non-devotee, to the faithless, to one who is averse to My service, or to one who is resentful towards Me.

Text:
य इमं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति ।
भक्तिं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः ॥६८॥
Transcription:
ya imaṁ paramaṁ guhyaṁ mad-bhakteṣv abhidhāsyati
bhaktiṁ mayi parāṁ kṛtvā mām evaiṣyaty asaṁśayaḥ [68]
Translation: One who reveals the glories of this supreme secret to My devotees will attain transcendental devotion to Me and reach Me without a doubt.

Text:
न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्चिन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः ।
भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि ॥६९॥
Transcription:
na cha tasmān manuṣyeṣu kaśchin me priya-kṛttamaḥ
bhavitā na cha me tasmād anyaḥ priyataro bhuvi [69]
Translation: In the entire human society there is no one who pleases Me by his actions more than he (who preaches the glories of the), nor will there ever be anyone in the world dearer to Me than he.

Text:
अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्म्यं संवादमावयोः ।
ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः ॥७०॥
Transcription:
adhyeṣyate cha ya imaṁ dharmyaṁ saṁvādam āvayoḥ
jñāna-yajñena tenāham iṣṭaḥ syām iti me matiḥ [70]
Translation: And one who studies with devotion this holy conversation of ours will worship Me by the ‘knowledge-sacrifice’. This is My conclusion.

Text:
श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च शृणुयादपि यो नरः ।
सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्म्मणाम् ॥७१॥
Transcription:
śraddhāvān anasūyaś cha śṛṇuyād api yo naraḥ
so ’pi muktaḥ śubhā̐l lokān prāpnuyāt puṇya-karmaṇām [71]
Translation: Simply by hearing this with an open heart, a faithful person is liberated and attains to the auspicious abodes of the virtuous souls.

Text:
कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा ।
कच्चिदज्ञानसम्मोहः प्रणष्टस्ते धनञ्जय ॥७२॥
Transcription:
kachchid etach chhrutaṁ pārtha tvayaikāgreṇa chetasā
kachchid ajñāna-sammohaḥ praṇaṣṭas te dhanañjaya [72]
Translation: O Pārtha, have you listened to this with your undivided attention? O Dhanañjaya, are you now free from the darkness of your delusion?

Text:
अर्ज्जुन उवाच ।
नष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत ।
